Great phone for casual smartphone users who are not into latest tech or FOMO.

If you just want the core Google Android smartphone experience then this is a good mid-range priced phone. Other mid-range phone may have higher specs in certain areas like 120hz refresh rate, but those tend to have bad custom OS and not as smooth as stock Android in my opinion.

Now onto the features of the phone and my opinions on them

--Features/Specs

-Display:
1080p 60hz display. Unless you’re coming from a high end 4k 120hz display smartphone/tablet/etc then the display is pretty basic and won’t bother you.

If you are coming from a high end device, I don’t know why you would downgrade unless this is a temporary replacement or in a rough finical situation than before

-Battery: All day battery life if you’re a casual smartphone user. Good screen on time.

Unless you’re marathoning a streaming series, keep refreshing social media page for updated notifications, and/or gaming the entire day; you most likely you don’t need to charge your phone mid-day.

Also the more you use the phone/with more android updates, machine learning will optimize the battery life

-Camera:

Only 12.2 MP rear and 8MP front. Gives really good quality pictures and selfies.

It’s not a night-and-day difference compared to flag ship/high end phones (In fact it’s better than some $800+ phones). Only tell if you have same photo taken side-by-side or nit pick on the details

For video it runs 4K at 30fps or 1080p at 60fps. There’s a speech enhancement option to pick up on voices and mutes wind/background noise

The camera visor is more flush to the phone compared to the Pixel 6 and Pixel 6 Pro.

Has the magic eraser feature, which is an automated content-aware fill (on photoshop) where it can take out unwanted people and objects.

-Fingerprint sensor:

Not as good as the rear fingerprint sensor/reader in past ‘Pixel a’ line phones. The under-display fingerprint gets better the more you use it, more profiles you put for it with same finger (which I recommend with different angles).

Seeing it’s light based, works while your fingers are wet without having to wipe your hands down on your shirt or towel

Other authentication options are PIN (4 numbers) and pattern for password. No facial recognition/unlock

-IP67 Dust and Water Resistance

-Live translation.

Translation features on the phone are very accurate. Can use in translation, recording, and camera app

-Phone Call Options

Hold for me option. When disputing a transaction or have questions with a business related call/operator; when elevator music goes on, just tap the ‘Hold for me’ button and the phone will notify you to pick up when they hear a live person while you do other stuff.

Also when calling a business phone with operator; it will translate everything via text on screen, and able to select numbers based on the text instead of pin pad

-Supports 5G. Seeing major carriers no longer support 3G ,and 5G becoming more common; should be standard nowadays in every phone.

-5 year pixel updates. Get the latest OS updates first compared to Samsung and other 3rd party Android manufacturers

--Other things to note:

-No headphone jack this time around for the ‘pixel a’ line of phones. There was a pre-order bonus for free pixel buds

-Only 1 sim card slot. No expandable storage

-No wireless changing

Overall, good phone to get if you want the core Android experience without the bells and whistles. 5 year of updates is very enticing if you’re not one to upgrade every year or every few years.

It's rather difficult for me to sit here and tell you that Seidio makes the best cases around. Everyone has different expectations and tastes when it comes to cases for their phone.

Let me just say that I began purchasing the Seidio surface case about three years ago when the Galaxy Note II was released. I was quite impressed with the quality of the case, the feel of its backside in my hand, and the little kickstand that enabled me to place the phone on an airline tray and watch movies while traveling. When the Galaxy Note III was released, I bought another Surface case for that. Same outstanding build quality and level of protection.

Now I am back to the iPhone after several years. Was very happy to see that Seidio made a case for the iPhone 6 and 6 Plus. I have the Plus model. I ordered the case directly from Seidio as Amazon was not yet offering it. Went with a red case for my Space Grey phone. Even with the iPhone brand, Seidio still makes the same quality cases that are slim in form and offer durable protection.

The only problem with these cases is the difficulty in removing them. It's not a showstopper problem. You just have to learn to apply pressure to the disconnecting point where the top and bottom case halves meet.

I suppose the best way to describe the Seidio surface case is that it adds a very classy look to your phone. It's slim enough that your phone will fit in most docks without having to remove it. I just bought the Henge Dock and the company tells me the dimensions of the case are fine. It really doesn't add too much bulk to the phone.

I have been testing out many iPhone 6S Plus cases over the past few weeks. You can research my reviews to see which ones I liked and didn't.

This falls squarely into the didn't like category.

It's a handsome case, and yes, thanks to the rubber insert, it is shock absorbing. The problem is, it takes a lot of manipulation to get the phone in and out of this case, and I could never get my PLUS to fit properly into it.

The case has a inner rubber shell. That is what you slide your iPhone into. That rubber shell then slides into the outer hard case. The problem is, I was struggling to get all that rubber to fit properly into the hard shell. Even when I thought I had it in there --- it was apparent that I didn't as I could not access the power button.

All the cut-outs around the phone are perfect. The problem is, the case is too thick to easily flip the ringer switch on the upper left side of the phone. You have to dig your fingernail in the case to do it.

I'm sorry, I really wanted to like this case. It seems there are many positive reviews for it. For me, I could never get the everything to fit together nicely. The case is also too thick to be able to easily access the ringer silence switch.

You know that feeling when you buy a terrific iPhone case that you really have fallen in love with, only to not readily use it because it's not designed to be protective?

That is my love/hate relationship with this iPhone case from Amanico.

I purchased the movie slate board iPhone case. It's beautiful. Being a film lover, it was something that really spoke about my personality when I had the iPhone in my hand with the cover showing. The design is excellent. The company has really captured what a movie clapboard looks like.

The phone fits wonderfully inside. All the ports are perfectly exposed -- perhaps too much --- and therein lies my problem with this case....

It offers the most minimal protection possible. It may prevent the back of your phone from scratching, but oh man, if you drop your iPhone, this case will not protect it. The top and bottom of the phone is completely exposed. There is only a slight overhang of bumper along the front sides -- not enough to remotely protect your screen from shattering if you were to drop it face-forward.

I totally understand the approach of making a very lightweight carbon case shell. The company succeeded in doing a beautiful job with that. I would love nothing more than to walk around with this case on my phone everyday simply because of the design. However, I would not have any sound of mind about my phone being well protected.

If you go into this case loving the design as I did, and knowing what it is before you purchase it, you will be happy. On the other hand, if you are looking for a case that will actually protect your phone front and back, then this isn't for you.

This is the fourth Android phone model we've used in our household used since evolving into smart phone use six years ago. Before I begin gushing about the phone itself, some context about *any* mobile phone purchase (if you're already comfortable with the variables of mobile phones and their providers, you can skip this part):

These major factors that will contribute to (or detract from) the smart phone experience of *any* user:
(1) the hardware...the phone itself
(2) operating system software
(3) installed applications
(4) the network (overall, and where you happen to be using the phone at a particular time)
(5) the service provider.

Serial dependencies come from these factors, as many phones are tied to specific operating systems or service providers. Just remember that even if you make a great phone choice, deficiencies in the other areas can still diminish your overall user experience.

Make your move to your next (or first) smart phone only after researching these choices. Which provider? Which mobile operating system? Who offers the best service, or the best combination of value and service? The rest of this review assumes you've already figured out why a phone with the Android operating system might be a better choice for you than an iPhone, a Blackberry or a Windows-based smart phone solution (yes, Blackberry and Windows options remain alive...sort of).

Every provider --including Sprint-- has a variety of pricing options. If you're a Sprint customer with two years on your current hardware and are ready to re-up: many phones are available at a deep discount from both online retailers and big box stores. I've asked Sprint if they will match those prices..and they won't. It's no big deal; you won't hurt their feelings if you buy from another source (but you might not be able to take a suddenly balky phone into a Sprint store and have it replaced at no charge, either). For this phone, Amazon's pricing was at a 90% discount vs. buying from Sprint. Be sure and negotiate with them and look for plan pricing discounts for loyatly or organizational/corporate affiliations.

For the phone itself: this is an outstanding device for each of telephony, online activity, still photography and video capture. The display is crisp and brilliant, the controls (each of the three buttons on the phone's side, the home/soft keys on the front and the options within the OS) are intuitive and responsive. The touchscreen keyboard is the best onscreen text input experience I've ever had...period.

Audio output (for phone calls and music --streamed via Bluetooth or via the aux connector) is excellent.

Both photographs and video are easy to use. The 16 megapixel rear camera images (still and moving) are outstanding in quality. Obviously the front is a grainier at 2 megapixels.

But wait there's more: the rear phone cover easily removes to reveal an easily user-replaceable battery. Shame on phones that don't offer this. You'll also find access to a slot for a microSD card adjacent to the battery.

Charging and sync are via a USB 3.0 port. If you don't have any cables with this form factor, this phone ships with one, but you'll want extras so you don't have to keep tabs on a possible single point of failure. This is not a negative...bust an issue if you replacing a phone with a different cable type.

Outstanding smart phone that will keep you from the clutches of the Apple tree. Select with confidence.

I kind of collect phone cases, and I firmly believe that you should have a good one to protect your smartphone, and those who don't definitely are courting disaster, because eventually you WILL drop it.

For the GS8+ I currently have 6 and my daily would be my leather Pierre Cardin case. Nice, but doesn't exactly provide the best protection...in fact it provides just barely ANY protection at all, even though I really LOVE the case and how slim it is. It didn't take long before I realized I really needed something slim but much better at protecting my phone. I looked around and decided on the Spigen Liquid Crystal model. It didn't take long to realize I had made a really good decision.

Not only does it protect really well, it is very slim (just barely bigger than my leather case which protected very little) and when it says, See-Thru, they MEAN it. Finally I can see the silver accent on the back on my phone without obstruction whatsoever. It even came with a Zeiss cleaning pad which was a very nice gesture. It fit PERFECTLY (as I would expect a good Spigen case to do) and I am thrilled to say that not only does it cover the back and all 4 sides, the buttons are easy to depress, unlike some silicone cases I've bought where you really and I DO mean REALLY have to press hard in order for your phone to register that you were pressing anything. Always a pain in my side. But Spigen has obviously thought of the consumer and made this case one that is simple to operate without compromising the ease of use that comes naturally with the device by itself uncovered. SMART. The headphone jack, power, microphone and speaker are all left unobstructed, which is another win in my book, even though I pretty much expected all of that anyway.

Not much more to say, either. For the price, honestly, you just cannot beat this case. Oh yeah, one more thing: on previous silicone cases, it was pliable, but also tended to attract lint in my pockets like glue. So far, the exterior is smooth, but plenty of grip to it so that you won't feel like your phone will drop (yet another plus) and plenty of protection which I am fervently hoping I never have to put to the test.

All in all, a solid case that protects without bulking up the phone and allowing the sheer beauty of the amazing design show through. Oh, and the price! What an amazing deal! I wouldn't hesitate buying another one and I recommend this highly.

I've got several wireless charging stands and this one could very well be my new fav. It's so dang simple in it's design, yet very well thought-out. I cannot tell you how nice it is that you can fold it when you want until it's thinner than my phone. Yes, it does have fast wireless charging, but that DOES come with a bit of a catch...

The charger comes with a cord that will support fast charging, but it doesn't come with a charging brick that does. So, if you want fast wireless charging, you'll need one of those, too. Without it, you'll still get wireless charging, but not nearly as fast as you'll get with one. Yes, it's a compromise, but I'm okay with that since I have several fast charging bricks it didn't bug me at all.

Just keep that in mind before purchase and I think you'll be just as happy with yours as I am with mine. It even works when you sit your phone on it's side. Just a great convenience. I used to think that wireless charging was more of a novelty, but the truth is, the technology is flat-out amazing. No more grabbing your cord to plug in--not that doing so is such a difficult task, and for those who think so, trust me, could there BE a bigger "First World" problem issue than this? I don't think so. But...once you get a taste for using one, you're going to find it difficult to ever go to a phone that doesn't support it. I know I'll never own one unless it's an option from here out.

This iPhone 7 case has exceeded all my expectations. It is just the right size. It is not too bulky and not too small. The color matches my rose-gold iPhone perfectly, and really compliments it! I was worried about the built in ring in the back of the phone case because I wondered if it would be too big/annoying, but the ring actually hides perfectly in the back. The ring is less bulky than the socket holders, too. The awesome thing about the ring-holder, is that it rotates back and forth in a circular motion, depending on what angle you want to hold your phone. While popped out, the ring also is very sturdy and can hold your phone up horizontally on a counter while you binge watch a show!

Much to my surprise, this case also came with a screen protector which was much needed since my old screen protector had about 6 different breaks in it. Overall, the price is great for what you get. The phone case has great different uses, too, and you can’t beat that. Highly recommended.

This is something like my 7th wireless charger and it works great. I've read some other reviews and some of the adverse situations a few others have experienced, and I gotta say, I haven't had any of those issues. The charger came with one of the longest cords I've seen (which is fantastic because many I've purchased over the years came with the shortest of cords possible). It would have been nice if it had been USB C but oh well. Some places just have resisted switching over to it, but oh well.

It works right out of the box just great. It takes a second to figure out the profile of how you lay your phone to make sure it's charging, but that doesn't take long, trust me. It lays firm and doesn't slide off easily either. Not sure what went wrong with the other reviewers chargers, but so far, mine just works great.

I am as much of a Samsung fan boy as some people are fans of apple. I MUST say however, that Samsung is trying my patience in the last year or so, and not in a good way. First off, I "upgrade" to a Galaxy Watch 3 only to discover about the ONLY way it is an actual upgrade is a bump in memory (which I must say was nice to see, especially since apple watches have had the option of 16GB for at least a couple years if not more), and the addition of blood oxygen and ECG. Other than that, I have been profoundly disappointed in what I got vs what they charged over my original Galaxy Watch. Poorer battery life (by at least 25%, which is rather significant when you consider it's almost 3 years NEWER and software updates alone should give us at the bare minimum, the same battery life the original GW offered) and absolutely zero bump in performance despite a marginal increase in RAM. Sorry Samsung, but this is unforgivable. For the rather large increase in pricing, you gave us almost nothing in return.

Over the last few years, Samsung has had a lot of entertainment poking fun at apple for some of their manufacturing decisions, such as making fun of them for eliminating the headphone jack. It was hilarious and stupid, right? Yup. Until, that is, Samsung did the exact same thing. They made fun of apple for not including a power brick, too...and then did an abrupt about-face and did the same. This is astonishing hypocrisy is what it is. It is also a douche-move on Samsung's part. Yes, the S21 Ultra is cheaper than the S20 Ultra variant a year before...but seriously guys, NO charging brick? Sheesh. Very petty move on your part.

With that out of the way...I gotta say, I love this phone. First off, the design is just cutting edge cool. That camera bump is kind of significant, but it looks wicked cool, though. It's easily fixable with a case, too. Without one, placing the phone on it's back can present an issue since it does not sit flush on any surface. Again, a typical case will fix this so keep that in mind. The back has a bit of a matte finish (I have phantom silver) so it doesn't leave fingerprints quite the same as the shiny phone surfaces do, which is a huge plus if you ask me. No matter HOW tough Samsung claims the phone is, trust me, if you dropped it, you can still do damage, so I recommend a case no matter what.

After coming from a Note, which typically has a more square-ish shape around the corners, the S21 Ultra is a nice change of pace. Rounded edges which look sleek and very modern. Overall, I am very happy with this new design...especially having moved up from a 3 year old device. The S21 Ultra is noticeably heavier, too. Yes, the battery has a noticeable bump in size and overall the phone is slightly bigger and thicker, though not in a way that bothers me personally...but you may be different, so keep that in mind before making a purchase.

Performance-wise, the difference is flat-out astonishing. There are at least 2 major reasons for this. First off, I my carrier is Sprint, and I was told it had great service when I moved to my area - it wasn't. Turns out their service (where I live, at least) was flat-out terrible. Easily the worst I've ever seen in the last decade. After T-Mobile purchased them, I expected the service to improve significantly. Well, it improved marginally, which is better than nothing, right? I was eventually given a T-Mobile SIM card and whoa, INSTANT and dramatic service improvement. It sure took long enough (2+ years on Sprint has made me jaded regarding pretty much everything they do). The other reason for the bump in performance is definitely due to the newer Snapdragon 888 chip. I had the older 845, and while it was cutting edge when it came out 3+ years ago, you can see that time in between has allowed for some marked improvements all across the board. It's hard to describe overall. Android 11 on top of UI 3.1 has made some stark differences between one and the other. This may be the longest time I've waited in between upgrading a device and the performance boosts that came with almost every newer model I've used has been much less noticeable. Well, I'm happy to say that performance boost is definitely a biggie.

The screen is fantastic. I have noticed that I have it on about half the screen brightness of my Note 9 and feel it is roughly the same. Let's hope that also translates into longer battery life as well. The maximum brightness is off the charts fantastic. The 120Hz display however...let's just say I thought it would be incredibly obvious, but so far, it just hasn't been what I was expecting. Yes, the phone operates buttery smooth. I don't think I've seen a single hiccup or stutter since turning it on. Much of this has to do with that refresh rate. I wish we had the option of dialing it back to 90Hz, just to save battery...but maybe at some point with a software update perhaps...? Also, after being the first company to introduce curved bezels beginning with the Galaxy Note Edge, and then with the Galaxy S6 Edge+, they have tapered it off quite a bit over the years, but this is the first year Samsung has essentially said goodbye to it. Oh, the screen still curves a very small amount right at the edges, but it is almost entirely flat. I'm a bit torn on this. I LOVED the look of the curved edges. Many companies still employ a version calling it a Waterfall Display, but this screen is gorgeous, it's difficult to be mad at the decision making that lead to this. The biggest obstacle with curved edges was a decent glass screen protector that actually fit properly and stayed put. That should no longer be an issue...though a known issue with the ultrasonic fingerprint reader is that many glass screen protectors make them even harder to read your fingerprints. Even when you purchase one that says fingerprint reader compatible. DO YOUR RESEARCH before buying one. Read as many reviews as you can so you can know the one you end up with actually does work well with the fingerprint reader. One of my biggest gripes has been the fact that I use the gesture controls...and with a case on, it is a bit harder to swipe upwards, and as such, it often just goes to the main screen instead of going back to previous screen, which is what I want it to do 99% of the time when I use this function. You can switch it to the sides as well, but I had a much harder time getting used to that. Maybe I'll end up doing that anyway because I'm really frustrated at how the gesture controls work, but ONLY with a case on (and I have 3 of them now, and it's an issue with all 3).

The fingerprint sensor has been...meh. I really expected a tremendous boost from the 2 previous years of improvements. I hear the sensor is definitely much larger than before, but for me though, sometimes it works great and is very fast, other times not so much. Considering how almost all reviews of this phone has sung the praises of the new sensor, I figured I would notice a big improvement or at least see that it was as quick and reliable as the back sensor I had on my Note 9 but so far, nope. Maybe a software update will help this. I worry about the accuracy further with a screen protector, too. I have held off buying one for this exact reason, but I don't think I can do it much longer.

The cameras on this phone, in a word: WOW. Coming from a 3 year old device, trust me when I say that improvement is absolutely gigantic. My Note 9 had a whopping 2 cameras. The S21 Ultra however, has 5 circles...but I think at least one is a Time of Flight sensor and not a camera. The zoom is just fantastic. The ability to erase specific items in a photo is just amazing and under many circumstances, it works pretty great. Obviously you won't be able to convincingly erase anything from a pic, but you'd be amazed how well it can do especially on small objects within a photo. Night photos are vastly improved, too. Actually every single thing about the cameras and video abilities has been improved tremendously over my old phone. I hear the iPhone 12 Max still has better video, but only marginally...which is saying a lot because of how biased many reviewers are towards apple. Either way, you're going to like what it can do. Everything from pro mode to raw photos to directors cut and more, very cool indeed and a worthy upgrade.

Let me quickly say how much of an improvement Android 11 and One UI 3.1 is over previous versions (the Note 9 had reached it's cap of upgrades so it stopped with Android 10 and One UI 2.5). It just LOOKS better. Some of the newer options are just fantastic. Truly fun options like the option of video to play with every phone call you get. Sure, it's a novelty, but it is certainly one that I use and enjoy. There are too many tweaks to the experience to include them all here, but they are definitely diverse and very useful.

Oh, I almost forgot the chief reason why I bought the S21 Ultra: S-Pen support! After hearing word from Samsung that a Note 21 probably wouldn't happen this year due to computer chip shortages (but they DO plan to bring it back in 2022), I think giving this option to the S21 is - for me at least, a game changer and one which really sold me on it. Now, is the device 100% the same as what the pens do on a regular Note device? No. However, it'll do 99.9% of what I want an S-Pen to do, so I am perfectly happy with this. For those who have had a Note previous and never really utilized the S-Pen, first off, I'm sorry. That pen is ridiculously convenient in more ways than I can write here. But if it isn't a deal breaker for you or you never really used it much, I would seriously consider getting the S21+ instead. The big trade off is fewer cameras and a polycarbonate back (ie fancy word for tougher plastic)...but for many that is a sacrifice that is definitely worth it. Oh, and the phone is lighter, too. But back to the S-Pen. Imagine my surprise when I found that the Samsung branded case which can hold the new S-Pen designed specifically for the S21 Ultra was flat-out ridiculously priced. It's like Samsung is turning into apple here, and not in a good way. Wow guys. That is highway robbery. The S-Pen from my Note 9 works beautifully with it, however. But the case that fits the S21 won't fit the older, smaller S-Pen's. I'm sure that is by design to sell more pens and make more money, but still...$40 for that little pen is extortion. Fortunately Spigen makes a case which will hold the pen designed for this phone and it's like $20...but still, not a cheap purchase since as far as I'm aware as I write this, the only compatible pen that works with Samsung and fits that case still costs $40. Oh well. I made the purchase knowing this...but that doesn't mean I have to like it, right? I'll be getting the case and pen soon.

One more note...since the S20 & Note 20, Samsung has eliminated expandable storage. I had totally forgotten this before opening up the box. I pulled out the SIM tray...and did a quizzical look at my wife. What happened to the...ohhhhh, that's right. They dropped it. Another mistake I'm afraid. The ONLY reason why I would find this excusable would be IF in exchange for that and the space saved, they enlarged the battery even more than they already did AND started the base model with 256GB of storage. The fact they did neither feels like a big "F-U" to us Samsung loyalists. Not sure why they went this route, either. Yes, I know every square millimeter is precious inside something that is more powerful than what ALL of NASA had the day they landed on the moon...however, previous phones DID have this (plus the S-Pen built-in on the Note devices) and well, it's becoming harder and harder to justify these types of changes other than to save a buck for the manufacturer at the expense of still charging the consumer essentially the same price (yes, I know the S21 is cheaper than the S20 equivalent devices, but those phones also had charging bricks). Again, not necessarily a deal breaker (for some) but it COULD be for others. Just keep this in mind before you buy.

All in all, a very significant improvement over my older phone...however if you have the Note 20 Ultra from what I gather, the difference is so slight that upgrading is simply not worth it...though this is a decision only you can make. I am thrilled with my phone, even despite my deep concerns which I brought up earlier regarding the lack of charging brick and yet the price still remains rather significantly high. Just seems very cheap to me no matter what their excuse may be. I hope my review may help you in any way possible.

***UPDATE***

I discovered a trick to make the fingerprint scanner much faster, and I'll be darned, it really did work. If you already have an S21 (no matter which model) follow this...If you already have one, delete your current fingerprints completely. When you re-scan, the fewer fingerprints you use, the less the scanner needs to process in order to unlock your device. The more fingers, the longer it will take. You can improve this greatly by registering both thumbs alternately as the first fingerprint, and any other finger you plan to use, alternate that one as well when scanning. Basically, as you scan, place your right thumb down, then your left, then your right and soforth until the first fingerprint is registered. Don't worry, you'll scan it plenty in different positions so that it'll still read properly. I noticed not just an improvement, but an immediate one. Faster. Cleaner. Easier. More accurate, too. The key when unlocking the phone is to press firmly down each time. You don't have to do it long or even hard. Just do it firmly and it should work pretty much 100% of the time (provided you place your finger in the proper place). This trick has really helped me, and I'm hoping it can help you, too.

***UPDATE #2***

I have been constantly amazed at how good this phone performs. When I open up the Google Play Store it opens virtually instantaneously. The Note 9? Well, let's not go there. When I ordered my phone, I had an alert saying that 5G service may not be available in my area. Even though it says 5G in the status bar at the top...so I went ahead and downloaded a speed check app and I'll be darned, we actually have 5G here. Kudos to T-Mobile. Even though I'm with Sprint, this phone came with everything T-Mobile...which is not just a minor improvement, either. Now, I have to say that even though we have 5G here, it is definitely on the lower end for uploads and downloads...but considering the fact I didn't expect there to BE 5G in my area, I'm still happy.

Operating the big screen with one hand can be daunting and a challenge at times. For those with small hands, this is something you REALLY ought to be concerned with before buying. This is NOT a small device. Yes, that has been said before but seriously, it cannot be stressed enough. The S21+ is still a big phone, but definitely smaller...and even more so for the regular S21. Yes, you trade-off a plastic back, no S-Pen support, smaller battery and some camera bonuses, but that may be worth it for some. Just keep that in mind.

While I'm thinking about it, what's up with the plastic back on the other two S21 phones? For what Samsung charges, that is just shy of criminal. Look how many other premium phones cost less and yet still adds all the highest end chips, battery, cameras and premium build. Hard to justify this as anything more than simple apple-inspired greed if you ask me. I'm glad I upgraded to the S21 Ultra...however, on the flip-side, I am definitely PAYING for it, too. This is the first time the phone I have cost more for the base model than the equivalent iPhone Max. As much as Samsung has made fun of apple over the years, this astonishing hypocrisy of following suit and now surpassing them...? It's all just very disappointing.

***UPDATE #2***

After the latest update, whenever I sit my phone down on my wireless charger, it now shows it is charging with the same animated style...but then about 5-12 seconds after that, the phone vibrates quickly twice, and a message pops up at the top of the screen exactly the way it would if I received a text, informing me that the phone had started fast wireless charging. Not sure why this second stage of the charging experience was added, and is way more of a distraction than I expected it to be. The number of times I hear the haptic vibrate followed by the screen turning on, I believe I've received a message is off the charts. What I further do not understand about this, is the phone does not do this when fast cable charging has started, so why add this to the wireless function only? Not only is this an unnecessary distraction, it is a very unwelcome one. I wish I could turn the function off as it adds nothing to the user experience, and instead, is rather bothersome in it's implementation. Samsung...this is a step backwards. One of the things I disliked about my Note 9 is the screen would turn all the way on each time I placed it down to charge wirelessly. With a newer version of One UI, I noticed just the little animation and then the screen shut off...easy peasy. But now, this added step. It's just annoying...and unnecessary.

I am Brand New to the apple eco system and one of the things I have always done with my Samsung smart watches is bought an extra charger for work to top off my watch during the day before I head home and use it during exercise. That was my main purpose for getting this for my brand new Apple Watch 7. It says specifically that it is compatible with the series 7 in the description...but I and others like me went out on a limb and bought it before we saw enough reviews telling the truth: that this charger is NOT compatible with the series 7 AT ALL.

I place my watch on the charger and it LOOKS like its going to charge...for all of 3-4 seconds perhaps. Then the screen continuously switches between the screen that says it's charging, to the regular watch face screen, to going black, then back to the screen lock page, and then it cycles thru all of those pages once again. It's on an endless loop, and all the while, it never charges one tiny bit.

In short: THIS PRODUCT DOES NOT WORK if you have a Series 7 apple watch. DO NOT BUY. Enough of us have posted this very fact that I feel we not only require a refund but an apology as well. Please do not make that mistake.

I also caution you to probably not buy this because if it doesn't work for the series 7, there is a good chance it won't work for the earlier watches as well...I mean, if they lied about it being compatible with one, who's to say, it may be incompatible with all the others as well. I'd just avoid it if I were you.

One more thing, just as I started writing this, my watch as at 90% and in the short time I've spent writing this, not only did it NOT charge, but my watch went down to 86% (roughly 5 minutes, which is definitely NOT normal for my series 7). Once again, DO NOT TRUST this product.

I have a love/hate relationship with solo-loop bands. I primarily use my watch to keep track of my exercising and the downside to a band made of material like this is it tends to soak up the sweat from your wrist, and if you're exercising outdoors in the heat, it can get even worse.

I knew this going in, and chose to buy it anyway. The UPSIDE is that because of the material, it's no big deal to remove the strap and wash the band. You're just out a few hours while it dries, no biggie. The stretch ability is nice, too. Keep in mind, over time that elasticity, like the collagen in your face, is slowly going to be less stretchy, but it should last you a while, so don't worry about that. The band breathes better than the typical silicone ones, and that's a good thing IMO.

I love how it's adjustable, too. I like my watch bands fairly snug without cutting off circulation and this does an admirable job of keeping on my wrist without sliding around (I just hate a loose watch band and when I see one on people's wrists, it gives me anxiety - and yes, I realize that's odd).

Best of all: it looks great. Win-Win. I won't hesitate getting another one from PROATL.

This device only charged my Android phones (various Pixel and Pixel Pro models) at less than 5W, and an iPhone at 1W, not the advertised 10W/7.5W. I used several different phones to test, both Android and iPhone, as well as a USB tester/multimeter. On the Android phones I also used the Ampere app. 5W is acceptable for the earbuds charger, but not the phone charger. In addition to using the included charging brick and cable, I also tested with an authentic high quality name brand QC3 charging brick and cable as well as a PD charging brick. All had the same poor results. I don’t have a smartwatch to test the watch charger at the top.

The clock doesn't keep time well.

If the description were accurate about the phone charging speed and if the clock kept proper time I'd be more forgiving, because 5W is actually what I prefer for overnight charging on my nightstand. However, during the day I want true fast charging for top-offs. The inaccuracy makes me question everything else about this, including its safety.

I've also tried two other brand chargers that look almost identical to this one and have identical chargers, cables, instruction manuals, warranty card, and packaging. I believe they all came from the same manufacturer. They have all the exact same problems. The performance of the other chargers does not impact my review of this product, but seems to confirm my suspicion that this is not a one-off mistake.

The only reason this product is getting 2 stars instead of 1 is because it was easy to install, once you figure out which of the videos on the website you're directed to goes with your product. No bubbles or spots, fits the S22 Ultra perfectly.

I actually had to use both of the covers that came in the package. The first cover I installed was completely pulled off the phone by the last sticker (step 4)! Even when it was off the phone, the "step 4" peel off didn't want to let go of the cover. Of course once the adhesive layer of the cover was exposed to the air, it immediately attracted dust and such and had to be thrown away.

The second cover went on just as easily, but the right corners (upper and lower) keep trying to come up, only held in place by the phone case.

There seems to be some interference with the fingerprint scanner, it works, but I've had to reset my fingerprints twice.

There's also bad glare off this cover, difficult to see the screen in the sun.

The biggest problem is that my fingernails put permanent marks in the cover almost immediately, and the S-pen left squiggles all over it. I wouldn't trust this screen cover to protect against actual damage. I'm looking for a new cover now.

The case in khaki is a saddle color and while not leather still looks professional and protects the phone well. The inside has three card slots as well as a full length slot for cash or other notes. The phone is held in place by a flexible TPU bezel that allows for easy installation and removal of the phone.

I wasn’t sure I really even needed a case like this but am so glad I ordered it (for my Apple series 4 40mm watch). The case has a hard shell with a full zipper closure. Inside is a custom tray for the watch and charger. You remove the tray to insert the charger then replace it leaving the usb end free to plug in when needed. Once the charger is in place, the case holds both it and the watch securely to allow for fast charging. I keep this on my nightstand to protect my watch when charging overnight and just grab and go when traveling. There is a carbiner to attach to the inside of a tote or duffle bag and a lint cloth is included as a bonus. Great value and a nice accessory to protect my watch.

This is a decent quality leather folio case for the iPhone 12 Mini. I like the pebbled leather and the interior is a softer sueded leather. The interior also features three card slots as well as a full length slot for smaller notes or business cards. The case also comes with a wrist strap attachment and small ear buds holder. The phone itself fits securely in the case with easy access to controls. The magnet works well to keep the cover closed but it's not the best to activate the sleep/wake feature. Not a deal breaker for me since I use folio cases for protection - anything else is a bonus. I also have the Visoul pebbled leather iPhone 12 Mini case on another phone and at a similar price point, the Visoul is a softer and better quality leather but without the wrist strap and ear buds holder. Overall this is a good choice and does a good job of protecting the phone.

The quality of this case exceeded my expectations. It is beautifully crafted in genuine leather with a sturdy but pliable bumper frame to hold the device in place. The interior features three card slots and one full length slot to hold an ID, business card or notes. I have a pebbled leather VISOUL case for my iPhone 12 Mini, which is also great but I prefer the suppleness and feel of the leather of this case. The case has matching suede lining and fits my iPhone 11 Pro Max perfectly. The power and volume controls are protected by the bumper frame but still allow for easy access and use (no extra exertion needed to operate the controls). The frame does not interfere with the charging port and the magnetic latch keeps the case closed when not in use.
I have used this with wireless chargers with mixed success - most work through the thickness of the leather but a few have not. But that's a small trade off for me for the quality, style and protection that this case provides.
The olive green is a nice change from the usual line up of mobile device covers and looks professional and sophisticated for any gender. It's nice to find such exceptional quality in an affordable genuine leather case.

This case is well constructed and provides good protection without adding excessive bulk to my phone.
The iPhone 13 Pro Max is already a large device so I wanted a wrist strap that connects in a way that gives me more confidence that it will not snap, like the thin black cord straps. This case closes securely and the magnet holds the cover closed. I do not carry much beyond a few business cards in my phone case so I can't attest to how this works if you use it to carry credit cards and ID.
While it is not genuine leather, it has a nice, tactile feel that comes very close. An advantage to this cover is that is cleans up more easily than real leather, which tends to discolor from skin oils, etc... The case looks good and is a good value.

With the release of the Z1 compact and Z2, seems like there are actually not many cases for the Z1 anymore (at least when I was searching for one). This case is a pretty basic TPU case. Nothing fancy. I got the black glossy one. One thing I like is that the case provides an opening over the SIM slot. This is great for those that need access to the slot, especially during traveling. But oh no, it covers the magnetic charging port. *sigh There is an opening for the SD card door, but I would think that the magnetic charging port would be more crucial than the SD card slot. Oh well, seems like all the other cases are no better (they would either block the magnetic port or the SIM slot, or even both). Can't win.

I like the ridges at the sides, providing better grip. The power and volume buttons are covered, but I don't find them difficult to press. The shutter button is also covered, and pressing it half-way with the case on may not be easy. Good think most will simply use the on-screen shutter button. There is a hole for the lanyard too if that's important for you.

For the price, it's hard to pass up. If you want a basic case that offers a convenient opening for the SIM slot, you may not be able to find other better cases out there.

Without doubt the BlackBerry Q10 is the best QWERTY keyboard smartphone on the market. It's also arguably one of the most expensive keyboards on the planet, with little in the way of stand out features to justify its cost. You'll get more for your money elsewhere.

For
Full physical keyboard
Impressive web speeds
Updated BB10
Decent battery life

Against
Odd screen size
Lack of apps
Very expensive
Not great for games/movies
Perfect and easy to replace

The cream of the crop. It is the best blackberry phone out there.
IF you do not consume media on your phone, this is the best device you can buy.
I have to admit i already owned a Blackberry Z10 before, which i did like very much besides the unacceptable battery life. So i did already know the OS. I especially like the Hub to handle communication easily. It integrates very well with my Exchange account, and social platforms to update contact information or the contact image. For me it is the first device that doesn't give me the feeling i need to return to my computer to answer longer emails. The wider screen makes reading PDF and Excel attachments more comfortable as well.

As a blackberry purist, i must say that this a return to source. I would say that it is the best keyboard/touch blackberry device next to passport.

Pros:
Its physical QWERTY keyboard is still aces
Lots of good software touches
BlackBerry holdouts will love the throwback design

Cons:
Finding (and installing)apps can be tiresome
Hit-or-miss performance
Can't remove the battery
Mediocre camera

If you want to upgrade from a Q5/10 just because you want a bigger screen, it is worth it. But if you have more cash to spend, grab the passport.

I have an iPhone Xs Max in Silver/White. I used this case in combination with [[ASIN:B07HLXYVWY SopiGuard Skin for Apple iPhone Xs MAX Precision Edge-to-Edge Vinyl Sticker (3M Satin Silver)]] and [[ASIN:B07GR5FJ6W iCarez 3D Full Coverage Tempered Glass Double Defense Screen Protector]]. Note that the silver vinyl skin is why the detail pops. When I put this case over my naked white iPhone, the detail was muted against the white background.

The case, itself, seems very protective. It has extra bulging at the corners as drop protection. However, that extra protection adds weight and girth to the ultra-slim phone, making it thicker and slightly heavier. This case fits well, slips on easily but does not slip off without a bit of effort. It has protected my phone when I've misjudged slipping it into my pant pocket, which was about a 3" (1m) drop onto a tile floor.

I use this case as my goto for outdoor/gym activities where the phone may be subjected to drops on concrete or moving (velocity) falls but will switch it out when I need something slim to fit in a party purse or pocket.

Goes on easy ajd looks good but it's too small by about a mm all the way around. This makes it lose two points. It's just too small

This matters less than you'd think as the seam is on the curve so it's not very noticeable. My better half couldn't tell if installed it until I showed her. She agrees the seam is a very very very minor thing

The boxes came with six protectors and did include fluid which I wasn't expecting. Compared to at least one other wet install I did it went on very easy. The corners adhered easily

One tip that's sort of hard to do with apple watch due to the curve. You have to let the protector start drying a bit to sick the sides. Don't overwork the sides or you will wind up with small runs (they show as minute white veins if you will). Try to press rather than rub and be patient to hold for five mins

My first instal is good enough. The second would be better. Trying to do the old fashioned stretch to make up the slightly small cut. Unlike devices where the protected has inches of area and you can do a small stretch out when drying by hand you'd need heat

Bottom line - it's good enough that nobody would ever notice it's there. If you're super retentive you'd want another mm of shield. Avoid the bad habit of obsessing and slap one of these on for a reasonable protective solution.

Orange peel effect is minor but there. Again you'd have to be staring at the watch from six inches to notice it - and know what to look for - and look for it.

Compared to the prices and quality do some others I've seen its hard to rate this lower than a four. It's a solid effort at a good price. While the cut is slightly small it will probably prevent peeling up and the seam is all but unnoticeable.

I'm pretty particular and even though the cut is a hair off I won't be replacing it with anything else.

I have small hands. Most of the phones out there are big now. And I am looking for a small phone that is not premium or flagship priced. And Pixel 4a meets that need. Since there are already excellent reviews in YouTube and Amazon, I will just add my personal experience with the phone. Pixel 4a fits nicely in my hand now. I can use the phone with one hand without fumbling it. My previous phone Moto G7 while great still was a little big for my hands. I do not support iPhone ecosystem, so I was looking for Android. My wife's phone Pixel 3a was great in my hands so I thought I would check out Pixel 4a and it has bigger screen yet smaller than 3a! So I grabbed it and using it for a month, I am really satisfied. Size and weight are perfect and the performance is more than I need (I am not a gamer, so I don't need a powerful processor). The screen is OLED and is clearly better than IPS LCD screens. The colors are just more rich and pop out than IPS LCD. Battery lasts more than 1 full day (my average screen time is between 3 - 4 hours). And the camera! Wow... It is really good. I've heard it was good, but actually seeing it - I was really impressed. The ONLY thing I wish Pixel 4a had was: Water and dust proof feature (IP 68). But for $350, this phone is amazing. And you also get 3 year software update guarantee. So if you are not looking for a premium phone, but something small yet powerful and takes great pictures, then Pixel 4a is an excellent choice! Highly recommended!

I like trying and using different headphones and earbuds. I ordered this earbud so I could use it at work and keep one ear open. I hate walking around looking like something off STAR TREK and this was small so it looked like it fit the bill. It came NICELY packaged in an orange box with a magnetic clasp"Really Classy". Inside the box was a Black & Orange zippered case with the earbud, a USB charger,3 additional replacement plastic comfortable ear buds, a manual and a card that tells you how much they would appreciate a review such as the one I am leaving here now. It is comfortable to wear so far since it does not have much mass. Sound quality is good but not HI FIDELITY excellence. Hey! It's a monaural earbud. It has very Low Distortion at higher volume levels. No crackling or buzzing. The one button on the side will pause your device playing music or answer and disengage a phone call. Making a call is easy but you have to dial your phone manually. Voice quality is good both on both ends. I asked the people who I called how my voice sounded and all said my voice appeared to be clear and I was satisfied with their voice clarity.  I liked the voice prompts only because nothing is sexier than having a lady with an English accent talking into your ear. This earbud has great reception from my LG V20 phone. I get at least 40 feet distance in my home. All in all I was satisfied with this earbud. Why didn't I give it 5 stars? Something has to be exceptional to get 5 stars and I REALLY have to love it. These are nice but I felt the fidelity and response could have been a bit better and voice dialing would have been a nice feature to include to earn 5 stars.Call me "HARD TO PLEASE"

This repair is not for the impatient or clumsy. If you take your time and watch the videos on-line it's not as hard as you think. Best thing to do is take a piece of cardboard, draw a sketch of your phone and, as you remove each screw and component poke holes in the cardboard for the screws so you know where they go. It'll make reassembly vastly easier since the screws vary in size and length.

Cheap, clear, thin, strong. No complaints. The installation instructions aren't as clear as they could be so I detailed my method in another Amazon review, https://www.amazon.com/review/RTIIAU0D7ZL3S/ref=cm_cr_rdp_perm, and I'll paste it here for convenience.

Place the screen protector exactly where you want it on the phone and use two or three pieces of tape (either the included tape or your own) along one long edge of the phone to hold the protector firmly in place. That way you can swing the protector off to the side, clean the heck out of the phone glass, remove the adhesive backing and drop the protector right where you want it. Before you remove the backing be sure to test it and practice placement a few times. Otherwise it'll be off by a hair and it'll bug you every time you look at it.

I've repaired 150+ phones over the last few years (check my other reviews for more detail, hints, tips) and this is one of the best I've found, especially at this price. It's as clear, bright and responsive as the original part. Most non-Apple screens stick up about 1/2 mm higher than the stock one and while that may not sound like much it's almost the thickness of a credit card, which is definitely noticeable once installed. This one doesn't. Even the stylus works nicely, unlike most of the "bonus" bits you often get with a kit like this.

Let me start but mentioning that there is another newer version of this tripod called the Pixi Mini Evo that cost twice as much but offers a lot of other features. The Pixi Mini Evo is a bit larger, has a twist screw compression for the ball head, a cut out for the camera to do portrait mode or face completely up or down, extending legs that also fold out flatter for far more versatility and standing options. This tripod, has a spring loaded button compression for the ball head, is much smaller than the Pixi Mini Evo, the legs do no extend and only fold out to one position, and to connect this to your camera you have to twist the tripod on.

Although this tripod doesn't offer as much as the Pixi Mini Evo, it does offer fantastic portability, simplicity, and rock solid functionality. I have been on the fence on whether or not to buy this tripod until I saw one in an Apple Store and got to try it out a bit. The ball head adjustment button was surprisingly easy and sturdier than you could imagine it would be. It feels light weight but solid at the same time and it is just easy to carry around.

I'm using this with a Sony A6300 and have used the PZ Sony G 18-105, Sony Zeiss 24, and the Canon 85 1.8 with the MC-11 lenses. I have to say I doubted the 2.2lb estimate and didn't think it would keep the A6300 sturdy with the reasonably large and heavy 18-105 but it did with flying colors. Once I release the button, the camera stops moving and is perfectly steady. It is really amazing how such a simple system can be so reliably sturdy. Of course using it with the other much lighter lenses works just fine as well and when I have my camera on the tripod I don't worry about it falling over as the legs are sturdy enough to keep it from tilting over.

It also apparently works as a short seflie stick but that wasn't really my intent with it. I don't use camera grips and I didn't buy the A6300 for a selfie camera but it is nice that it can be used in this way.

You can buy the Pixi Mini Evo for around $50 and I'm sure it is great, but I got this because I want something small that I can carry around effortlessly that works quickly and simply. Manfrotto didn't cut corners on this and it shows in quality materials and intelligent design elements. It is easy to carry in your bag or even your pocket and it is even easier to use when you need it. At $25 this tripod is easy to recommend and I will update my review should I find any more positives or faults with this.

Also, I wanted to note that although I did test this tripod with the A6300 and 18-105, the pictures I posted are with the Zeiss 24 1.8.

I haven’t really been a fan of this style screen protector for my devices for a while now. You know the fancy stretchy plastic that self heals and keeps helicopter blades scratch free. Ever since glass screen protectors came out I haven’t looked back because I like the feel of glass and those plastic ones sometimes left an orange peel effect over the screen. However when looking for a screen protector for my first Apple Watch(3), the glass protectors didn’t have great reviews so I decided to try this one at least as temporary protection for my watch.

So I followed the directions and installed the first protector in the pack. It is a wet install with a little bottle of soapy water and a rubber squeegee. Install was fairly easy and I was surprised how it almost effortlessly wrapped around the curved glass of the watch. The problem was that the install wants you to get out the air bubbles and let it sit before pressing down the sides. In this time I must have got some lint or dust that made bubbles in the protector making it look bad.

I decided to redo with a fresh install and instead of leaving it out with the sides up, I used a warm hair dryer to help the drying process and pressed down the sides while using the warm air to help it begin to stick. It seems to have worked well as I think I have a near perfect install free of bubbles lint or dust.

You can barely tell the protector is on by just looking at it and I think it actually looks great. I see no degradation in the screen resolution due to the protector and a very limited orange peel effect that is only noticeable at certain angles with a lot of light like can be seen in my pictures. While the protector feels good to slide my finger over you do feel that slightly more tacky and rubbery feel of thenprotector as compared to the glass. For the trade off of some scratch and bump protection I am personally more than willing to use this.

I did ready one review of this protector that said the sides peeled up after washing their hands a lot. I am a compulsive hand washer and rarely get my watches wet enough to cause these issues so I’ll have to see if I experience anything like that in which case I’ll update my review.

For the price and the protection of my new pricy device I really am impressed with the screen protector so far. The feel isn’t quite glass but it isn’t bad at all and not nearly enough to remove a star. I can easily recommend this if you’re looking for protection for your new Apple Watch.

The stock Samsung charger charges the Galaxy Watch Active 2 40mm in an hour from 5% to 100%. These chargers, which are supposedly the same 5V/1A (5W) rating, take 2.5h for the same 5%-100% charge. This is VERY slow, and nowhere near even the already slow stock charger. And this watch doesn't even support fast charging like the newer Galaxy Watch models, so that's not even being considered here.

The only reason I didn't give 1 star is because they do actually charge. But they do not match the power output of the stock Samsung charger, and instead only output about 40% as much power to the device. Either they're just lacking windings in the coil, or it's an incredibly inefficient design, but regardless, they are VERY slow to charge even the smallest watch battery in the series. The 44mm will take even longer.

[[VIDEOID:2c19d45959b8eab7b8adff965c12bad0]] I’ve been buying and using UAG cases on the last 5 phones that I have owned over the years. I have probably bought 6-7 cases. They have always been great in every possible way. I was so happy with them that I constantly encouraged others to buy them.
We now have the freshly released iPhone 11. This phone was released 9 days ago just for reference of my perspective. We ordered the UAG case and I am disappointed. I expected better. The materials are great as always but the fitment execution is very poor. The case doesn’t feel good in the hand. It feels more bulky than any of their prior cases. The front lip is too high and flimsy and I can see the color phone bezels at all 4 corners.

I have included my old iPhone 8 Plus UAG case for reference. Note how much more bulky the 11 case is.

My hope is that UAG will re-design this case and fix these issues. I would still happily recommend any of the prior USG cases for iPhones but this one I would not suggest unless it is re-designed.

Update 7/10/17: I did find a small issue with the case. The holes exposing the microphones caused a minor video recording issue. I was recording a baby shower and had the camera zoomed in on the soon to be parents, and the audio for them was muffled and sounded like white noise. I removed the case the issue was gone. Original review:
I've had 5 different cases for the G5 Plus; mostly because I got this phone when it was brand new and cases were just coming out. This one, is right now my favorite!

Unless Otterbox comes out with a case for the G5+, there isn't much else that can compete. The Spigen Tough Armor case comes 2nd to this one while Incipio is 3rd. (Albeit a distant 3rd since it's $20 and only at Best Buy)

This case feels very durable but it could use some improvements. The material on the back (The colored aspect of the case) is rather slippery and can be easily dropped if you're not careful. The lips around the screen could be raised up a bit more; if the phone belly flops it will damage the screen. (Unless you have a screen protector) This is more of a request, but I'd like to see a durable case cover the charging and headphone jack ports. (One of my cases does, but the material for it is terribly made plastic)

Other reviews mentioned issues with the buttons, I personally don't have a problem with the buttons on this case. There wasn't any difficulty pressing any of them on this phone while this case was on. I actually like them as there's texture to feel what you're about to press. (Some cases have treated the power button and volume rocker as 3 separate buttons, rather confusing)

Love this case, just wish I bought this one first so I wouldn't have 4 other cases for this phone lying around.

I admit, I thought these were tempered glass screen protectors. Now, for film these do a decent job. But there are bubbles in the screen and it barely fits the display. (Albeit because the phone has a curved screen)

For the price, I'm satisfied with them in case my glass protectors fail and I need something in the mean time for replacements. However, I wouldn't trust them as a permanent solution to protecting your screen.

Like CoverOn's cases, but this one has one issue and it's the plastic screen protector. Even after a few minutes having put it on, the issue became noticeable. It's not horrible, but it is a problem when typing on this phone and when using the capacitive buttons.

But both this case and phone are cheap, and it does everything else wonderfully. I forgive the price solely that it was $11. Spending more than this for a phone that cost $50 would be ridiculous.

I've said to people that CoverOn is a poor man's Otterbox, and I feel it's somewhat accurate. It is a nice case, but not the same quality of Otterbox. For $11, I'm happy.

I actually bought this phone last year, but couldn't stand the horribly low amount of internal storage, but now that a 64 GB model is available it is an excellent phone! I do recommend getting said model even if the price is $50 more. The 64 GB version has 47 GB available and 4 GB of ram, making multi-tasking easier and having the phone be a tad more future proof.

Battery life is pretty good, I'd expect a full day's use on a single charge. It'll need topping off at night of course, but thankfully the quick charge here is incredibly fast. Went from 20% to fully charged in little over an hour. Only complaint so far is the charger's cable is way too short. Get a 3rd party cable from Anker to help with that.

Runs stock 9.0 very well, no hiccups or slowdown thus far. Even has the Moto gestures which are pretty handy. It feels like a budget Pixel phone. Have a factory reset once you get it caught up with all the updates, it'll help with performance and battery life.

The processor is nice for gaming. Obviously not going to be on par with flagships, but it'll do just fine. (Plays Pokémon Go wonderfully and many other 3D games with almost no slowdown) I put a screenshot of my Geekbench score for an idea of what to expect.

Only downside is the camera. Not the best by any means, it can be improved with a modded version of the Pixel camera called GCMod5. (Look on the XDA Developers forums for a link) My pictures here of the sunset and horse statue are taken in this order from 1st to last:

Stock camera
Wide angle camera
GCMod5

While the stock cameras produce 'okay' results, the GCMod5 camera is way better in low light.

Overall, I wouldn't exactly call this a flagship killer; but for this price it absolutely can be. While it has all the 'features' of a flagship, just don't expect it to be as good. Meaning it CAN take 1080p 60fps video, it can shoot 4K videos, has NFC and all other hardware sensors (Proximity, light sensor, gyroscope, etc.) it won't do some things as well as a Galaxy device or Pixel. But it has a headphone jack, which is nice.

Very nice quality and feel, this is a true glass screen protector and is super simple to install.

take some painters tape and apply to the left side of the outside part of the screen protector
lay the protector down on the phone and line it up so it is perfect then press the tape down on the side of the phone so it makes a hinge
take the alcohol pad,clean your screen
wipe it down with the microfiber cloth
carefully inspect for any dust
pull off the protective film from the protector
flop it over onto the phone, it is lined up perfectly!
press down in the center of the glass, the air dissapears!
take the squegee and rub it down the edges to make sure it is sealed well
remove the painters tape and admire your perfectly installed protector

So far so good. What a great deal. A brand new cell phone for $185. Do the Amazon ads bother me? Nope. Spend $50 more if they bother you.

I love this phone. Some reviewers fault the faux metal look. Really? Who doesn't immediately put a case on their new cell phone? Some reviewers fault the speaker sound. Really? Sounds great to me. I like how it is located on the front of the phone. I spent two years with an LG G3 with a rear speaker that always got covered by my hand. Some fault the micro USB charger. Not me. I have several micro B cables including my car charger that all work with this phone. Saved me money not having to buy all new chargers. I mean who really just uses one? I love the feature when you pick it up that is shows you the time. Face it, many people rely on their phones instead of a watch these days. The fingerprint unlock feature is cool.

Two things I had to add. There was not a Gallery app. The phone is set up for you to select either Google photos or Prime photos. Ick. I downloaded a simply gallery app from the Play store and no ads. it is made by Simple Mobile Tools. The other app I needed to download was a voice mail app. I have Verizon and simply downloaded their visual voicemail app from the Play store. The phone is lightning fast with Android 7.0 Nougat. I wish I could edit the soft keys but I will get use to it. I prefer the back button on the right.

Battery life is fantastic. I played with it all day yesterday and it still had over 50%. Very fast charging with the Turbo charger supplied in the box. It charges just as fast with my old LG G3 chargers. If you want a nice case, look at the Tudia Slim-Fit Heavy Duty. Definitely slim with a nice slip resistant grip on the sides.

So also think about this... Square Trade insurance for a cell phone costs $99 a year. The deductible is $99. That is more than the cost of this phone!. I read several reviews that said this is the best bargain cell phone. Absolutely. Thanks Amazon. I'm never spending $600 for a new cell phone ever again.

UPDATE 8/11/17. I lowered my rating from 5 to 3 stars. Telephone reception is horrible. I see from other reviews that this is a particular issue on Verizon which I have. The reception is garbled for most calls. This is very frustrating as everything else is great. I sent back the first phone. The replacement does the same thing. The replacement is being returned for a refund. Bummer.

I don't know how they created a film substance that fits around curved edges (and CORNERS!) without folding, but they did, and it seems to work beautifully. I've used ArmorSuit with great success on other devices, and REEEALY like the results I've gotten. but have to admit I was really skeptical about how it would work around the curved edges of an Apple Watch. Especially around the corners of the device.

I followed their instructional video. I'm a bit ham-fisted, so had trouble with the finer detailed task of keeping the skin aligned as I removed the excess moisture using the provided squeegee. Fortunately, the skin stayed just loose enough that I could keep it aligned evenly all around. After the recommended wait time, I was able to smooth the edges down. I was flat-out amazed that the shield stuck to the rounded edges AND the corners without a ripple or bubble or wrinkle (TIP: use the provided cloth when doing this - there may still be some of the spray liquid hanging around, and the cloth will absorb this moisture as you glue down the edges). By the time I was finished, I couldn't even tell there was a film over the face of my watch. Oh, wait; yeah, I COULD tell - the scratch in the middle of my watch face wasn't visible any longer.

I waited overnight for things to dry out, and this morning it looked even better than it had the night before. It looks like a pristine Apple Watch face again, without that darned scratch in the middle. I understand why you need to wait for an extended period of time after you've glued down the edges and corners - just makes the skin anneal better to the unnatural edge and corner contours.

VERY pleased with the results. ArmorSuit comes through again. Their skins may not be the easiest ones to apply, but if you follow the instructions, and are patient, the results are amazing. If you're not willing or are unable to do the fine detail work and wait for the best results, this skin surely isn't for you. But if the final product is what you're after, I think this is the best skin for Apple Watch on the market.

I have purchased Supershieldz for multiple devices and had good results. They are very easy to align & install correctly using the included sticker "tabs". I use 2 tabs: Place a tab on diagonally opposite corners (ie, top right & bottom left) of the screen cover, align the cutouts with the camera, mic & speaker holes, and gently lay it down onto the display. If you mess up, gently lift the screen cover up and retry.

Pros:
1. Feels just as smooth as a naked phone screen, no bumpy film or "orange peel" effect
2. Package includes alcohol wipes & microfiber swatch to clean screen prior to installing screen cover
3. Inexpensive
4. No "halo" or "rainbow" effect (yet)

Cons:
1. Only 1 screen cover per package (many competitors include 2 screen covers for about the same price)
2. The dot matrix is visible under direct light
3. Touch sensitivity is slightly diminished after installing, which is why I gave 4 stars instead of 5
4. Hides the Sony logo (if that is important to you)

Some people I have read are upset that this wont work with the older MagEz car mounts but we are in a transition period that Pitaka has to follow. Apple had made transitions for the future and this is Pitaka turn to move over to MagSafe as that is going to be the way of future. This case now opens the door to be compatible across all MagSafe products.
Like other thin cases that are compatible with MagSafe this one has the benefit of added magnets, so instead of it just being compatible, it adds to the MagSafe magnetic pull that allows this to work with MagSafe car docks for example. Other thin cases might be compatible with MagSafe charging but not with docks as the case takes away from the magnetic pull since they have no added magnets in them.

I like to collect power banks for emergency and even better, power banks that has a plug, that you can charge into a wall outlet. In my test of this power bank, I am charging my iPhone 11 Pro Max, which has a capacity of 3,969 MaH.

Here are my results. Please note, I have not use my phone heavily only mildy during its charges:

1. 11:25AM @ 60% - 1:23PM @ 100% => 3/4 bars left
2. 8:50PM @65% -9:07PM = Charges in 16 minutes this means it definitely is quick charge
10:05 @ 100 % => 1/4 bars
3. 4:05PM @ 68% - 5:55PM @ 100% = depleted

So I was able to charge my phone 3 times, to about 107% total.

Now here's where my confusion is the power bank advertise it at 10,000 MaH, I looked closely and it said "Rated Capacity: 5800 MaH (only 5V=3A)" but the description on Amazon states "Battery extra output capacity: 5800mAh（5V / 6A)". This can be confusing to people especially when people expect the battery bank to hold 10,000 MaH when in fact it does not. Then I did my research it looks like rated capacity is really is what is actually inside the powerbank, the physical battery!

Now I know there can be some conversion loss with charging, so here's my calculator on theoretical USB output. Convert 3.7V to 5V.
ACTUAL 5V mAh = 3.7 X Advertised mAh / 5
= 3.7 X 5800 / 5 = 4,292 MaH

This means there is a reduction loss of 26% but I'm sure there's a bit more due to conversion loss during charging your phone. As expected, this should be the actual battery capacity that is consistent with my test. Would I recommend it, sure only if they put some accuracy in their description that won't cause confusion. Also the price is a bit steep for its price you can get a better bargain out there. I wouldn’t say this battery is lightweight it’s a bit hefty.

This is very grippy silicone rubber. It feels almost adhesive. Think back to the rubber wall walkers, where you throw them on a wall, and they stick, or maybe roll down the wall slowly. Similar stuff.

Because of that, you can stick this to any shape of dashboard, at any angle, and it will stay put so long as it's not up-side-down. If it's on a slight curve, then it will pinch your phone (gently) and hold it in place really well.

If it's on a slight convex curve, a very thin device might gap. I had no problems with an iphone 5s and a TPU thin case.

The grip is good, so I don't have to worry about hanging cables off of the side.

An iPad Mini is a bit too much mass for this, and it isn't very stable. It doesn't slide, but it moves around due to its own mass.

The only real drawback is that it will collect dust, which you cannot simply wipe off. Whenever it's car-cleaning day, you'll want to just wash this in the sink with normal soap and water, then shake off the excess water. If you towel it off, it will pull lint from the towel.

This is also very low cost. The sale price is pretty much the normal price for these kinds of holders. It would be easy to have one in each car, and maybe one for a desk. A desk would be fine for a 7" tablet, since it's not moving around.

The product reviewed was provided as a free sample, but I liked it so much I bought a second one for the other car.

The key here is be vigilant about making the screen absolutely pristine before you apply the protector and to do it on a level surface. I used the kitchen counter top. Wipe the phone all in one direction with the provided wet wipe and then the dust cloth. REPEAT. Repeat again and do not touch the screen. Wait a few seconds for it to dry completely. Then apply the screen protector - since this is a tempered glass unit, it's stiff and will set in place much more easily than the old fashioned plastic ones, but it's also harder to re-position, so go slow and get it right. This unit is precisely measured and fit my phone exactly on the first attempt and I have zero bubbles. See the photo.

Fits my LG V40 perfectly. All of the buttons are fully functional and fit in the cutouts fine. The case doesn't interfere with the camera or fingerprint scanner. There's just enough lip to offer a bit of protection to the front of the phone as well.

As a camping lantern, this is super useful. You can use it as a solar battery charger for phones or a Kindle. They do charge slowly, but better than nothing out in the back country.

I've been using this for a couple days now, as I finally decided to stop carrying around a separate GPS when I travel. I've used this mount on a few different model cars and haven't had an issue yet with it. It attaches in just a few seconds and allows me to easily view my phone (iPhone 6s) while driving. I can manipulate my phone and it doesn't move around at all while mounted. I only have a couple of complaints, but they are minimal.
1) The metal plate that I put in my Otterbox case definitely added a little bit of weight to my phone. I notice it when I carry the phone around, but it's not too bad.
2) There's no way to angle the phone fully towards you when mounted. It would be nice if the mount was just a bit longer, or had some way to angle the phone towards me. Instead, the phone faces partially to the rear of the car and can't adjust towards me because the mount doesn't stick out far enough. Once again, not a huge concern, but still an inconvenience.
3) I've almost left this device in the rental car on more than one occasion given that it blends in with the dash. I'm sure that this is just because it's new to me, but making the mount black doesn't help.
4) There's always a fear of carry around a magnet near electronic devices, but haven't had any issues with it yet. I just make sure that I don't carry the mount in my laptop bag, but rather my suitcase when I travel.

The complaints I have are minimal and overall would recommend this to anybody wanting a quick, easy way to view their phone while driving.
