As a former fan of the much bulkier military grade impact cases, I decided I would try for a thinner case that offered similar protection. I had Otterbox and LunaTik cases on my iPhone 5, and I have gone through a dozen cases on my 6+, not being able to decide on one. This one seems very similar to the OtterBox, but with substantially less bulk. It certainly weighs less and has a smaller profile then the metal and glass cases that I have been using on my 6+ (Mostly knockoffs from China that weighed as much as a small child).

I use a belt holster pouch to hold my phone while I'm at work, I work in a machine shop and I am always up on my feet and I don't like the feeling of a huge phone in my pocket when I'm walking around, especially if I have to be moving and bending a lot while working. I wanted to try a case that would not add weight, so that my belt would not be sagging around my ankles by the end of the day.

The built-in screen protector is better quality than most built-in screen protectors I come across. The clear plastic on the back of the case seems a good quality, but obviously won't stop a bullet.

Will this protects against the drop off of a ladder? Probably not, but most cases won't.

Will this protects your phone most likely if you drop it pulling it out of your pocket? Yeah.

Will this keep your phone from bending? Most cases will, unless you stick the thing in a vice.

All in all, this appears to be a great case for the casual user who just wants a little more protection.

The wallet features of this are good, 3-5 cards can be easily carried. One thing to be aware of - all these cases of this design are made by the same manufacturer despite having a different company name on the product page. I have the identical case with a different image on the material. That one came from Vaburs, but other than the color and decorative design they are identical. The construction is 2 thin layers of material stitched together to form the wallet and then glued to the back of the rubber phone enclosure case.

With this case in the Mandala color the outer edges of the material is white so you can easily see how well, or poorly, the wallet part is glued down. I chose this one because of the color but I am concerned how long the wallet material will remain glued to the rubber phone enclosure. I’ve seen a few reviews that mentioned it peeled away from the enclosure fairly early. The Vaburs version of this case, different color, has black edges to the material and looks to be better glued to the rubber enclosure. I’ve been using that one for over a month and there has been no separation of any of the material. Pick what color you want from any of these listings since they are all made identically with the same materials, just be aware that durability and quality of gluing process will vary. For the price these are sold at that’s really not unexpected.

I needed a magnetic car CD slot mount that moved the phone up and out of the way of my car's in-dash display. This product does that enough so that I can see my screen contents while keeping the phone in the magnetic dock. The magnet is strong enough to hold my iPhone 7 Plus with a case. I ordered a few of these to try and this one isn't the strongest magnet out of the bunch I tried, but I think its just right in terms of strength for my needs. It holds the phone very securely and it won't fall off even on bumpy roads, but its not too strong as to make it hard to remove from the dock. You don't want it too strong because it can damage your CD slot or wear our the ball head of the mount.

The only thing I'm concerned about is the ball head is connected to the mount via a thin plastic stalk. I'm concerned that over time it my break or wear out. Other than that, I really like this mount, it suits my needs perfectly and is a good balance of style and function with just the right strength magnet.

Never has "real, premium leather" smelled so much like a hot beach ball. If this is leather, I'm a fairy princess. And I'm not. I fully expect this case to last about as long as it takes to write this review.

The inner liner -- which in the product pics appears to be fabric -- is (say it with me) cheap plastic. The part which holds your phone is very snug, but not so much so that it's impossible to get the phone inserted. The button covers are rudimentary and a bit difficult to push, but they do work as intended. Though not really leather, the imitation is fair, so long as you don't actually touch it. It not only smells like a beach ball, it feels like one in your hands.

To be fair, it does fit the phone well and seems to provide as much protection as one might expect of this sort of case. But lordy, that SMELL is really not very nice. I'd hate to work at the factory in Taiwan or wherever this was made. I'm hoping it dissipates in time, though I doubt I'll be using the case long enough for that to happen. This was probably one of my most disappointing Amazon purchases. I can't believe they can get away with billing this as "real leather." I'd try for a refund if I thought it would be worth the hassle.

UPDATE: OK, in the interest of fairness, the smell of this case has begun to dissipate. I hosed it down twice with cologne and let it air dry. Took a few days, but the initial stink has worn off. It still doesn't smell like leather, but it doesn't smell like a chemical warfare agent, either. I still have my doubts over whether this is really leather, but I'm willing to concede it *might* be. I guess for the price it could definitely be worse.

One very nice feature is that it comes with two glass screen covers, and they work (all be it a little small compared to the screen). The pop out finger hook/stand feature is OK, but it creates a new pivot point over that of the camera bump. The cover is thin and light, but with the provided glass screen cover there is no raised edge to protect the front of the phone. It is not bad looking, it keeps your phones profile thin on the edges, but adds bulk on the back. It fits well, and is better than nothing. As soon as it arrived I knew this is NOT the cover I wanted protecting my $1,200 iPhone, so it is going back. The only case I have found that could possibly provide a sense of security for my 12 Pro Max is the Pelican Shield G10, but that starts at $70.

Since it was $11 and came with two glass screen covers I am not sending it back.

Choices!

The most important take away will be this: The military does not drop test cell phone cases and only has a very broad drop test standard (MIL-STD-810G 516.6), which most false advertisers abbreviate to MIL-STD-810G, which in turn means absolutely nothing about a phones ability to withstand drops from hip height. The best judge of a cell phones durability boils down to their own advertised drop rating. Beyond that standard everything is entirely subjective without repeatable lab tests that produce the same results. A falling cell phone encounters all manner of variables; was the drop slowed by the brushing against the leg, did it hit a stopped or in motion shoe before the ground, did it hit a corner, the side, at an angle, was the screen cracked prior to the drop, had it been previously dropped, wind, was the ground asphalt, rock, concrete, dirt, chipped wood, etc.?

As far as I understand it, there are only three insured case manufactures: Otterbox, Lifeproof, and Pelican. Insured implies that you have an avenue to prove that it was a case failure that resulted in the phones failure from a drop, and doing so is a steep climb on a good day.

If you’re only out for basic phone protection, and a low cost combo option (glass screen saver and basic body protection) this might do? The finger ring/adjustable stand is not great, and the screen saver is to small for the A53 5G I have, so I’m guessing this was initially for another phone, and they said “close enough”? The hard case that snaps over the TPU phone wrap is OK, although they are far from a perfect match. I put my new phone in my drop pocket in my 5.11 pants while I’m on my bike/motorcycle. After a 44 mile, one stop round trip a corner was no longer seated over the TPU. The TPU does provide a small amount of front edge protection, and this is a better than nothing solution combo pack. It was $16 so I’m not sending it back, but I’ve already ordered a 3 pack of known name brand 9h glass screen protectors, and a two piece (TPU/full hard cover) that will be here in two days. As long as this holds out the two days, I’ll take it off and file it as a lesson learned.

I purchased this AmazonBasics stylus for use with the Nintendo Switch console to specifically play Super Mario Maker 2. I knew that using my fingers on the touchscreen wasn't ideal for me because my fingers always hurt after a little while, and I can't see precisely what I'm doing because my hand gets in the way. The Switch requires a capacitive stylus, so my 3DS stylus doesn't work. I liked that this product has three different stylus tip sizes, but I always used the smallest size. The screen responds to it perfectly. The tips are soft, and they have a slight give to them, so you can press down without worrying about smashing your screen.

The only flaw I found with this product is that the cap is terribly loose. It has a clip on it, but there's no way you can carry the stylus clipped on something as it will just come apart. The cap slides off when I use it, so I just keep the cap in my case and use the stylus on its own. A major annoyance in an otherwise perfect product. I still recommend it, but this is the only stylus I've tried, so there may be better options out there if you're willing to pay inflated prices. 3.5/5 stars

I have been installing screen protectors, both name brand and unbranded for nearly 20 years. Some are better than others, but there are very few that are actually bad. This one is bad.

The frame for installation is a tight fit and has clips that come close to feeling dangerous for the device. A near perfect seating is required to avoid the camera from causing the device to not sit level. This only matters long enough to realize the cheap frame is likely warped, causing the first tube of glue to pour right down the device and on the table. After getting all set up again and accounting for the warping, the second tube of glue will have the same fate when trying to set the protector into it and realizing there is almost nothing to keep the frame stable. If enough glue manages to stay on the screen, the protector will never make it on the screen.

The protector itself feels cheap and weak. It does not feel like glass or any sort of quality material. It feels like the cheap plastic cover that comes in a full-body case meant to hover over the screen. It is not something that doesn't feel sturdy enough to trust against keys or even a sharp fingernail. Placing it into the frame will feel like it is about to shatter and probably cause a lot of doubt. Trust the doubts. This was a terrible idea.

The case received is not the same as the case in the photos. The photos are much like an artist rendering of an upcoming phone or game console. They appear sleek, with sharp curves and crisp colors. The final product is a bit more rounded and dull. Like any artist rendering, it is also much more bulky.

The case is not bad. It is the same material as virtually any case you pick up in a mall kiosk for a couple dollars. It will protect against minor drops, but also has exaggerated holes in the back to show off the parts of the phone with lights and logos. I would not have purchased a case like that, had it not been shown as just slightly thicker than the cover included with the phone.

This case assumes you have lost the rubber stopper for the side port. If you still have it, it is not going to work well with the case. There are separate openings for the USB-C and the contact strip, which seems a little pointless when considering that the case prevents using any accessories that will need them. You are now forced to make the choice between leaving the stopper in place and not being able to remove it easily when using the USB-C port or removing the stopper and needlessly exposing the contact strip to the outside world.

This case does not feel "secure" in the sense that it has a firm grip on the phone. Instead, the frame seems to be thick enough that the phone cannot fall out of it without some intentional flexing. The rubber is not as flexible or soft as advertised or that may not have worked out to prevent simply falling out of the case.

For the price, the case is good enough. I would have given it a much lower rating for being so far off the listing if it had cost any more than it did. If you are looking for something that matches the original cover in a softer material, keep looking. If you want a version of the cover that is three times as thick, then this may be the case for you. Just remember not to take the photos literally. They are a mock-up.

I am not a stranger to installing screen protectors which use Loca glue, and you need many things to come together to make the install successful. While you don't have control of fluid dynamics, there are things you can do to help increase your odds of having a successful install.

You MUST:
Be in a clean room that doesn't have dust particles flying everywhere.
Use a surface that is completely level, even a slight tilt will ruin your install.
Press the screen protector down with your thumb instead of using the weight included.

My suggestion is to use your bathroom counter to apply the screen protector. Turn your shower on as hot as possible, wait until the steam condenses on your bathroom mirror. This will make all of the air born particles of dust fall and increase your odds of success.

The instructions / youtube video instruct you to place the Loca glue in two places on the phone. I've noticed this increases your odds of having trapped air under the screen as sometimes the loca glue spreads in such a way that both puddles of glue connect together trapping air inbetween them.

If you get so much as a single bubble in the glue or air trapped inside, you're probably screwed. A razor blade is included so you can lift the screen and try to get the air out. I have never been in a situation where I had to use the razor and had a successful install afterwards. Typically it sends the glue to both edges of the phone and off the side leaving you with not enough glue to successfully adhere the protector.

I tried to install my screen protector three times. The first time the install went perfectly, however the fingerprint reader would not work at all; every time it would say I needed to completely cover the sensor.

My subsequent attempts failed miserably. The second attempt had a huge air bubble in the middle, I had to lift the screen protector with the razor to try and get the bubble out, but it only introduced more air and made the glue immediately retreat to either edge of the phone and vanish into the tray.

My third attempt there wasn't enough glue in the bottle so there wasn't enough to spread leaving a gaping blank spot on the phone even after sitting 10 minutes.

Whether you get a successful application is mostly chance, but part proper preparation.

With all of this said, once you get a successful install done, you'll see it's well worth the frustration and effort. The phone looks amazing, and using it just feels better than a film screen protector.

**Update:
This update is a review for Anker's 6ft lightning usb cable. I was given a sample of the product to test out. My evaluation of it will be honest. After using Anker's 3ft cables for a while, I was very pleased that they offered a 6ft long version. Sometimes you just need a longer cord. I was worried though that the length would be counterproductive to my apple devices' charging speeds. To test this, I let my ipad mini, iphone and ipod touch batteries die down to about 30% twice. Once to charge with the regular Apple cord and the 2nd time with the Anker cord. I was suprised that the Anker cord actually charged all of my devices faster than the Apple.

In terms of looks...the lightning plug and usb plug look identical. A big bonus is that the Anker 6ft cord is noticeably thicker. I think this will help with the durability and therefore longevity of the cord. As we all know actual "Apple" cords tend to break apart quickly. The Anker 6ft cord is actually thicket than their 3ft version as well. And while it is thicker, it isn't bulky at all and rolls up nicely.

Most important to me after reliability is price. Apple cords are usually $19.99. Anker definitely beats that. So it's a great cord for the price whether you're replacing your worn and tattered Apple cord or you just want an extra cord for travel.

I’ve used Spigen (Ultra Hybrid variant) cases with my iPhones since my 5s and have always loved the small footprint and great protection. When I purchased my iPhone 12 mini, the Ultra Hybrid case was already sitting on my desk, waiting for my phone to arrive. Fast forward a few months later, and I really want the benefit of using the MagSafe charger, car mount, and wallet. Unfortunately, this isn’t possible with a standard case.

I first tried the Ultra Hybrid with MagSafe, but as others have mentioned, I just can’t get past the unsightly text. I came across this Mag Armor case and ordered it right away.

I’m not a huge fan of the textured design on the back (hence only a 4-star rating), but it otherwise meets every expectation I’ve come to expect from Spigen and then some. The case is still incredibly thin and light, offers the same excellent phone and camera protection, and we FINALLY have tactile buttons! Best of all, there is no unsightly text on this case.

I have tested this case with the Spigen MagSafe wallet (which is also excellent), the official MagSafe charger, and a MagSafe car mount, and couldn’t be happier.

If you’re looking for a great MagSafe compatible case that not only works well with Apple’s official MagSafe accessories, but with some of Spigen’s variants as well, this case offers the best of both worlds - great protection and functionality at an affordable price.

This is by far one of the best phones I've ever had. I don't say that lightly as I once was an iPhone owner from the very 1st iPhone to the iPhone 6. I switched to Android and bought a refurbished Nexus 5 then the Nexus 5X which I loved. I always had an eye open to Alcatel but was relectant to try one.

When my Nexus 5X died for the second time I needed a new phone and had heard that Alcatel had quietly released the Idol 5s. After reading product reviews and comparing it the Nexus 5X I decided to give it a try and I've been loving it ever since.

The build quality of this phone matches if not exceeds that the iPhone and I dare say any other flagship phone on the market now. It's extremely rigid without any give the aluminum is Rock solid and Dragontail glass IMO is better than any of the Gorilla Glass screens on my iPhones.

The dual speakers are amazingly crisp and clear and they're stereo speakers. They're not as loud as the boom speakers on the older HTC phones but they're louder than any of the speakers you'll get on an iPhone or Galaxy S phones.

The Idol near stock Android software is the best I've seen on any phone besides Motorola phones. It's so light that it shouldn't be called a skin at all. If you like stock Android (which I do) then you'll the Idol 5S. With the exception of a few apps that Alcatel has which can all be disabled the only slight difference you'll see in the UI is the update which is just as nice as the stock version. The dialer looks exactly like the stock Android dialer except it's green instead of blue. The settings menu is the same as stock Android and so is the pull down menu.

The Snapdragon 625 chip along with 3gigs of RAM easily handles anything you throw at it. I haven't had any real issues scrolling, multasking, browsing or watching movies on my idol 5s and I have over 112 apps installed on my phone.

The only thing missing from what my iPhone and Nexus 5S had is NFC. You can still use Android Pay on the web and in apps but not in retail stores. That aside, this is a really good mid-range phone and one you should really consider. If Alcatel did an incredible job on this phone and I'm already looking forward to next year's model.

I hope you find this informative. I don't work for nor am I affiliated with either Amazon or Acatel.

This is a great inexpensive phone.  My Lumia 1520 died a month ago and I don't get an upgrade until April so I searched for a low priced phone I could use in the interim.  I am 100% satisfied with my decision to go with the Moto G.  The hardware runs Lollipop fantastically smooth and the phone will run all the apps I download for it.  Switching between apps is quick and painless as well.  The battery lasts all day even with me playing games, texting, responding to emails, and making calls.  The waterproof shell is outstanding!  I've cut the grass several times with this phone in my pocket and my Bluetooth headphones on and the phone becomes drenched in sweat.  It still functions perfectly fine which is a huge plus. The only complaint I have is that 8GB is not enough storage for my mobile device habits but that is totally on me for not buying the 16GB version.  I put an SD Card in it but a lot of apps still store content on the internal storage and a lot of apps do not let you move them to the SIM.  Again, this is on me, if I could go back I'd have probably paid extra for the 16GB version instead but I would have still gone with the Moto G!  Get it!  You won't be disappointed.

SO, this is a good value tempered glass I'd say good for a good majority of customers out there looking for value, the adhesion is good, installation decent, but the quality of glass is a B. To get an A quality glass, you'd have to spend twice as much.  The real difference is the quality of the glass in high transparency, high definition and fingerprint resistance.  This clarity is not top notch, in outdoors, plus direct sunlight, it glares, and really shows off the inferior transparency, contrast, and color saturation, and it easily leaves fingerprints all over, hence requires constant cleaning and wiping.  Where as the A quality tempered glass does not require constant cleaning, less direct sunlight issues, finger glides smoother, and screen clarity is top notch.  Now, keep in mind, I'm being exacting here, because I tend to gravitate towards higher quality products. So as long as you're willing to pay twice the price, look elsewhere. I purchased this to replace my Moto E4 Plus, which I liked very much, but had gotten a little aged. I agonized between this and the G6 but ultimately went with this because of the price difference. It's a secondary line for me, as I use it for work, so I really didn't need the upgrades that a G6 brought.
The G6 Play has a beautiful finish and, although the body is plastic as opposed to aluminum and glass, visually it's very sharp looking. I love the placement of the fingerprint sensor on the back, it was always awkward for me personally to position my thumb or finger on the front. This was actually a sticking point for me on the E4, and I never grew to like the front fingerprint sensor. This was also a big reason I went with the G6 play over the G6.
The dimensions make it MUCH more comfortable to hold, as it's longer and more narrow than the E4. It fits comfortably in hand. The slightly larger screen is nice and the over all design is sleek and modern, but not too spacy like the Galaxy S9... I think it's a very clean look, and I prefer it over the G6... That's a personal preference though.
Set up was a breeze. The battery is 4000 mAh with quick charge, which I anticipate will last me two days easily with light to moderate use (mostly calls, texts and some light browsing). The screen is bright. The resolution is fine for such an inexpensive phone. It's much lighter and less bulky than my E4, and I really love that. The sounds is very good, speaker phone is loud and clear. Music sounds better than I expected. I love the moto gestures, which I didn't think I would use and thought they were gimmicky, but have found them pretty useful. Admittedly, they take some getting used to, but then it's piece of cake. The phone came with less bloatware than I am used to and Moto has cleaned up the UI since the E4, it's easier to navigate now and not so busy. The phone seems quick, much quicker than my E4... Noticably so. My Nest streaming video plays well and doesn't bog down the system. So far, there's no lag and the phone hasn't frozen yet. I mention this because usually when migrating from one phone to another, there are freezes and various apps may need to be updated. I haven't experienced that yet. I took it to my grandson's soccer game and was surprised at the quality of the camera. The pictures are better than I expected. While I won't be using it much for photos (my primary phone is the Mate 10 Pro), I was still surprised at the quality. They memory is expandable, which is always nice "in case" I need to use it to store files or large downloads. It has a respectable 3GB or RAM. And it has a headphone jack (yay)! I'm one of those people who still use wired headphones when I'm watching Netflix, and a headphone jack means I can use my AUX cord in the car! That was nice for me, I missed that in my Mate 10. The fact that it IS compatible with ATT, TMobile, Verizon AND Sprint is just icing on the cake, and means I can switch between prepaid services without having to get a new phone. That's awesome!
On to the cons... It isn't a Type C charger... Which means that I still have to carry both micro USB and type C in the car and when I travel. I really wanted a Type C so I didn't need two different chargers, but in the end that wasn't worth $50 more to me for a second phone. That's the only thing I would change, and is the only concern I can see so far. The phone comes with charger, clear silicone case and even the Sim key (takes a nano SIM), but didn't come with any headphones.
Overall, I have to admit I was surprised how much I liked this phone immediately. I'm excited to use it further. The price point is pretty incredible and it was too hard to pass up for me. I've had many cases for my iPhone 11 but this is one of the much nicer ones.  It looks and feels very sturdy and solid and not to mention stylish.  The fit is also perfect and all the button openings lineup nicely & are functional & working.  Overall it's a great value since it comes with 2 tempered glass Screen Protector & 2 Lens Protector in a beautiful classy packaging.  I never seen packaging for a case like this .. not even Apple has such a classy complete package. The only thing missing was a MagSafe ring which I can order separately & complete this (so to fit into my car's mag charger.  I read from another reviewer that the tempered glass was slightly small for the screen but then that's how it is with almost all tempered glasses made for iPhone 11 because unlike the iPhone 12, the edges are curved and unless the glass is also curved, it would not hold well or bubbles up .. but once you put the case on, you won't notice much gap.

The case arrived quickly & I promptly installed it on my Pixel 7 phone. The packaging included the case (all in one piece) as well as a short installation guide. The installation was somewhat tricky & long or at least longer than what I'm used to but once you follow the direction, you'll have it installed in a few minutes but I suggest you have a small screwdriver on hand as it makes it much easier to separate the front transparent plastic from back of the case.

The case was just as described as it is sturdy & felt substantial in hand. I didn't have any issue with the fit and putting the front plastic on top was easier once I got used to it. No more trying to seat the phone perfectly and squeezing out any bubbles when installing the screen protector. The grip was just perfect perhaps due to its texture and the material but most importantly, it does offer decent protection, and yet it's still slim enough that I can easily take it out of my pocket.

My only issues with it are: 1) the transparent plastic front, although adequate, it doesn't have the same sensitivity of the tempered glass & protection wise, it probably won't be as good either. That said, at least I didn't have any issues with the fingerprint sensor as it worked just fine 2) installation is a little more involved and if you are not careful, you can crack the case when separating the top and bottom and last but not the least 3) the wireless charging with this case is not perfect. I had to move the phone around a few times to get it to charge on the my wireless charger stand but had no issues with the wireless pad charger.

Overall, I like how sturdy and secure this case is but the flimsy stand and not so great screen protector does set it back a little.

Poetic was gracious enough to send me this product to review. This is my unbiased and personal opinion of the product to help you and other consumers to make an informed decision to buy the product or not.

Pictures tell a thousand words! I have uploaded a lot of photos of the Poetic Atmosphere for the Apple iPhone 6 Plus!

Pros: Thin & Almost “Naked” Experience, Lay on the Table Design “Raised Lip”, Perfect Cut-Outs, Fits like a Glove, Protects Rear Protruding Camera, Stylish, Does not interfere with the camera flash, Tempered Glass
Cons: None
“Whatever”: Potential Scratches & Yellowing of Clear over time

I reviewed the Poetic Atmosphere for the iPhone 6 and it is essential the same as for the iPhone 6 Plus.
Here is the review:

“I’m a big fan of slim cases and the Poetic Atmosphere is no slouch. I like how thin the case is but still provides a day to day protection by having a lay on the table, protection for the protruding rear camera, and firm fit. Furthermore, the Apple iPhone 6 looks amazing in this case as the back is clear while accented with grey buttons and a grey border.

Speaking of the grey borders, Poetic made a conscientious decision to mimic the antenna bands on the iPhone 6. It makes the phone look uniform but my only gripe here is the border didn’t go all the way at the top due to the camera cut out. This was done to prevent camera flash interference so it is definitely understandable. All of the cut outs were spot on this case and I had no issues with my accessories being plugged in. I did notice that while the buttons were tactile, there was a break in period for them.

Overall, I am very happy with this case. I highly recommend this case for those people that are looking for protection against daily hazards; however, for those who drop/damage their phones often may look into thicker cases.”

Good: Premium Feel & Non Slippery Grip, Soft Inner Lining, Spot On Cut Outs, Tactile Buttons, Unrestricted Access to Bottom Cut Outs,Compatible with both the iPhone 6/6s, Screen Protection via Raised Front Lip
Bad: Leather Wears
"Whatever": Button needs break in for it to be tactile.

Pictures tell a thousand words. Check out my pictures!

GMYLE sent me the Ultra Slim Leather case for the Apple iPhone 6/6s an unbiased review.

Attention to detail is the name of the game. GMYLE's choice in material and workmanship is definitely top notch. The time I spent with this case definitely made me happy and made my iPhone feel very premium.

The first thing I noticed while using this case is the buttons are relatively stiff and require use to become very tactile. This is also the case with it's direct competitor, the Apple Leather Case. The next thing I noticed is how well the leather repels water. I wanted to see how well the leather material was and I am happy to say it didn't soak in water but rather repel it to further protect your phone. The last thing I observed is the wearing of leather. Any type of leather wears over time and how gracefully it ages is dependent on the owner and the materials. While the case aged fine during my testing period, my top two corners show a lot of wear (see pictures).

In conclusion: If you are looking for a premium case for your premium iPhone 6/6s without the premium price tag, check out GMYLE's Ultra Slim Leather Case!

Good: Lightweight, Non-Slippery, Vibrant Color, Raised Lip, Tactile Buttons (after break-in), Great Cut Outs, 2 Piece Silicone/Polycarbonate Protection
Bad: N/A
"Whatever": Vibrate Switch is a tad bit recessed. Buttons are a tad bit stiff but does loosen up over time.

Pictures tell a thousand words. Check out my photos.

Sometimes I have projects using my GoPro that requires the GoPro to be ON all the time, so a high capacity portable powerbank was my best option. This powerbank was the most inexpensive powerbank with PD charging. It also has coupon when I got it. It was an excellent choice.

The product arrived in a small box with minimal branding and picture. The product was a tight fit inside the box, so I had a hard time unboxing it and eventually broke the box. Inside the box is just the powerbank and a short micro USB cable. The first thing I noticed is how heavy the product is. I can easily tell it is full of battery compared to my 10000mAh powerbank. The product looks nice with a combined matte and glossy finish. The glossy finish is a fingerprint magnet, but that’s ok with me. I like the LED indicator to see how much battery is left, a very good indicator if you need to recharge the powerbank. This powerbank has a lot of ports, 2x USB, 2x USB C, and a micro USB. That’s great as I can charge other devices while I’m doing my project on my GoPro. The 18W PD charging is very fast, excellent for smartphones and iPads. I can charge my mobile devices while inside my bag, and by the time I need to use them, they are fully charged.

Overall, I am very happy with my purchase. I got this primarily because of the high capacity, but surprised how small it is for such capacity. The build is premium and good quality. Highly recommended.

[[VIDEOID:4f8b975bc8fdfda63f61706ae59c63e7]] This watch is a very nice change for the individual who wants a unique looking watch. The wood looks very nice and the contrasting grains on each piece of the "chain" give it a cool look. I also like that they incorporated a quality metal clasp into the wood design to open and close the watch. The wooden links fit together seamlessly and do not catch on each other. The links can be removed so that you can resize the watch, you just must have the right tools to do so. A video of how to do so can easily be looked up online.

In my opinion the date window is a little hard to see at times but it is a smaller faced women's watch so I understand why. To change this date window, slightly pull the metal pin on the side and turn it towards you. It will cycle through all the numbers until you reach your correct date. I was confused at first about how to change the time until my girlfriend helped me but you actually pull harder on the metal pin and it goes outward into a 2nd setting where the time can be changed. Push the pin back in all the way to set it.
There are 4 screws on the back of the watch that can be used to access the battery if you need to change it, although this watch came with a working battery already.

I have had this for a few days now and it has kept time very accurately. The watch came in a very nice box that you could gift it in around a plush pillow. I would recommend this watch if you are considering it. I have posted some pictures along with my review to give you a better look at it. I was given this watch at a discounted price in return for my honest opinion and review. The company was very responsive in all of my messages and I would highly recommend them.

*I received this item at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review.

This is a stellar product. This battery is a life saver for an frequent traveler with high draw devices.
I travel by plane on a semi-regular basis. Huge strides have been made in making outlets available in airports but there still aren't enough and using my Galaxy S6 as much as I do it drains very quickly.

But this tops it back off quickly. And unlike most quick charge compatible batteries it charges using quick charge itself which allows it to fill back up in a reasonable amount of time. It even has a nifty feature where it stops providing power once the device stops drawing power.

Unlike most batteries where, as long as a USB cable is plugged into the output port, it is providing power this one has an on button that starts the flow of power, and when the phone or tablet is full it turns itself off. Overall I like it.

I have to say this was ALMOST the perfect case. The design is slick, the build is solid, and the color goes great with the Nebula Blue OnePlus 7 Pro.
I've dropped it a couple times already in the couple weeks I've been using it with no evidence of damage to the case or phone.

There is also enough raised space on the back that if you put a metal plate on the back for magnetic mounts, the plate won't touch anything when placed on a flat surface, preventing those unsightly scratches and keeping your phone looking clean, even with a metal plate on the back.
It doesn't really add any bulk. It's still comfortable for me to use and I have small hands. It is slightly slicker than the normal rubber it TPU cases but for me, it just makes it easier to get my phone in and out of my pocket.
I only have 2 points of disappointment with this phone.
First, the clear back is very prone to fingerprints than some other plastics and is very east to scratch.
Second, and probably the worst for me, is the cutouts areas of the case that are thinner (by the front facing camera, side slider, and charging port) seem to be weak and are stretched by how well the case fits. This causes those portions to be a bit weak and become slightly distorted, almost losing it's shape. When I slide the rocker, the case moves a lot. By the charging port, it seems the case starts to pull slightly away from the screen, albeit slightly. It's more cosmetic than anything. Lastly, the portion between the phone speaker and front facing gets pulled away from the screen, causing the camera to be covered slightly. When using the front facing camera, the bottom of the image is blurry. Not a huge deal for me but I also don't ever use the front facing camera.
Overall this is the best looking and feeling case for me. Couple that with the price and good protection with little added bulk and you have a real winner.

I bought the S7 Edge to replace my six-month old LG V10. I liked the V10, and sadly Samsung does not use the 32bit DAC which the V10 has. But the V10's poor battery life just was too much of a problem to be used by a road warrior. The S7 Edge's battery life is substantially better. With average daily use (a few phone calls, regular email and text sending and receiving, an hour's with of Internet searching) the phone has about 30% of power left after a 16-hr day. I was concerned that the always-on screen would sap the battery. I ran a test and found that the screen eats up about one and a half percent per hour.

The screen is beyond gorgeous. Colors pop. Black is black. Typefaces are razor sharp. The 5.5" screen is a right-sized screen - - big enough to comfortably use and small enough to slide into Jean pockets without sticking up out of the pocket as a beacon to pick-pockets.

The Bluetooth is solid, as is WiFi. The external speaker is loud but useless for serious listening. Serious listening requires earphones. The DAC is only 16bit (the mediocre norm), so this phone can never be more than just an okay audio playback device. The camera is excellent. The screen edge isn't nearly the gimmick I thought it was. It is actually a useful feature. I really like the always-on screen displaying the time, battery level, date, and number of messages. The fingerprint scanner works nearly 100% of the time. It will go from 40% power to 100% power in two hours using a wireless charging.

Some things to consider if you are weighing getting this phone. It is really expensive, it's made of fragile glass, and it's curved edge compromises how well a protective case can protect the phone without interfering with the edge functions. I would be afraid to own this phone without carrier insurance to replace the phone in the event of owner-caused damage. I see so many reviews that rave about this phone's style and tactile feel. But it's style and slipperiness scream to have a protective case which will cover up all that style and certainly change the tactile feel.

All things considered, this is the best phone I have ever owned. I would highly recommend it to anyone who doesn't seem the negatives I have reported as deal-killers.

I just got this phone today February 2nd 2016. I am completely impressed with this phone. It feels much more expensive than what it costs and looks amazing, you won't be embarrassed of this phone in public I promise you. Now the metal back doesn't feel as premium as say the Iphone 6, but it doesn't cost as much either. It still feels very nice. I was afraid this phone would be to big but it really wasn't. I was coming from using an LG G2 which has a 5.2 inch screen. This phone is just a little taller but no wider than my G2 with a case on it. It surprised me by how compact it really was. Like i said taller, but no wider than my G2 was in it's a case. It has tons of features and settings and the fingerprint scanner is spot on. Nice touch!!

With all the settings it can seem a little overwhelming even with somebody like me who is used to android phones. The Honor UI is gonna take some getting used to without a doubt. First thing i done was install Action Launcher and it made the phone feel a little more like stock Android but there's just some things a launcher won't fix. All in all the Honor EMUI has some nice touches, I don't hate it. But like I said it has tons of settings for you to play with and figure out. My phone came with 54% battery and I set it up and downloaded all my apps and changed things to the way I wanted them and that took a little over 3 hours and the battery had 7% when i put it on the charger. So the battery seems like it's going to be great, which is what most of the reviews said that iv'e read. I haven't tested the camera other than a few quick shots just to make sure it worked. It doesn't have OIS though and that really sucks. It does have FM radio on it though. Honor also informed me that they was pushing out the first update for this device February the 15th maybe even a little before then. It's not the marshmallow update though, probably a security patch. It's nice to see them standing behind this product like they said they would. That's a good sign. Also i haven't had any lag what so ever. The phone is responsive and feels very fast. Not flagship fast but it's just as fast as my LG G2. I'm really happy with my purchase! If you're still thinking. go ahead and get this phone. You will love it! Nice build, fast fingerprint scanner with plenty tricks, fast and smooth during streaming and browsing and all for $200.00! You can not beat this phone at that price. It's impossible.

UPDATE: (2/3/16) I just found out from HONOR that this phone is running the Snapdragon 616 not the 615!! Another plus! Sure enough I ran Antutu on my phone and it says Qualcomm Snapdragon 616 and scored around 34,000 to 36,000. Also this battery is a beast!! I got a little over 6 hours of on screen time with my phone being off the charger for over 20 hours! I am so happy with this phone. Also if you use a 5V/2A charger the phone will charge in about 2 hours. You're looking at over 3 hours if you use the charger that came with it. I just used my LG G2 charger as it was 5V/2A. Today was my first full day using the phone and i am extremely pleased with it so far. WIFI and Bluetooth worked perfectly and the sound through the headphones is really good. Has more bass than my old LG G2. Sounds good in my car through Bluetooth as well. Also on my LG G2 i constantly had around 2 bars of reception while indoor. NOT with the 5X. It must have a really strong antenna because right now my LG has 2 bars and the 5X has 4 bars! And they are sitting next to each other. That's another big win for Honor. The more bars you have the better the signal and the faster the LTE!

UPDATE: (2/8/16) I had to return my first unit for a replacement because it felt like the bottom left side of the phone was coming separated also the screen was going bad, it had lines running through it. It was hard to see for the most part but using the Hello app it was really noticeable. I have already received my replacement and so far so good. It seems like it's put together and built the way it was meant to be in the first place. Also on the same Hello app there are no lines. Screen looks perfect. I'm so glad to because i completely love this phone so i was praying that my replacement would come and work perfectly and it did!! Also for anyone that needs to know how to move your photos and music and contacts among other stuff download the app SUPERBEAM. All you have to do is download it on both phones you want to send and receive with and presto! It's amazing, easy and free! It's super fast. Way faster than Blutooth and so simple. Try it out, trust me you will love it. I transfered all my music (around 120) in about 7 to 8 minutes. It's the way to go with no NFC and faster than Blutooth. I LOVE MY HONOR 5X!!!!!!

UPDATE: (2-9-16) I have Straight Talk and I'm unable to use the WiFi Hotspot. Every time I try it informs me to dial 611 from the phone which takes me to Straight Talk hotline. Just wanted to let all the Straight Talk users know that the WiFi Hotspot is basically useless unless someone knows someway around it. I don't. However my LTE speeds are awesome! I've been getting around 20 to 28 just about all the time! Never was able to get them speeds on my LG G2 unless I was outside. I'm getting these fast speeds in my house! I can not praise the antennas on this phone enough. They are top of the line!!

UPDATE: (2-11-16) Just thought I would let you guys know about a couple of tweaks I've used that seem to speed the phone up quite a bit. It does involve going into the developers options settings. If you're not sure how to get the dev settings all you do is go to about phone and tap on build number 5 or 6 times and it'll tell you that you're now a developer. Then back out to the settings and you should see developer options down by the about phone setting. Anyways I've enabled the Force GPU Rendering setting which may cost a little battery life but I'm not sure yet because I've just done it. I'll update if I notice any change in battery. But it seems to make the phone run so smooth. I've also changed all the animation settings that you will find in dev options to .5. This setting speeds up all the animations on the phone for a dramatically faster experience.
So I Forced GPU Rendering and changed animation settings to .5 and my phone is running like a top!! Give it try and you'll see what I mean. I will update tomorrow about the GPU Rendering and if it does of doesn't eat more battery life. Until then those are the tricks I've tried for a better faster experience.
P.S. Don't go changing any of the other dev option settings unless you know what you're doing.

UPDATE: (2-12-16) So I've went through the day with the GPU rendering forced setting turned on in Dev options and it has not made any difference in my battery life. No better no worse but the phone is buttery smooth and with my animations on .5 it's twice as fast. I wish I would have known about these tweaks with some of my older slower phones! Folks these tweaks work and I haven't noticed them interfering badly with the phone at all. So give it a try and you will see. Also I have played around with the camera now quite a bit and while I won't go into the settings of the camera right now the pictures it takes are middle of the road. In good lighting the pics are fine, as long as you don't hold the same pic next to a more expensive phone like Iphone 5 or 6 or an LG G3 or Samsung S6. The camera on this phone isn't even close to as good as the one on my LG G2. In low light the pics aren't that good at all. I have seen worse but the camera really struggles in low light. In good lighting though the pics are fine for social media and things such as that but don't get this phone if you want a great camera.I agree with most of the other reviewers of this phone about the camera. Good lighting equals decent pictures but low lighting is going to equal low quality pictures which actually isn't the case on more expensive phones. I have still took some decent pics with this phone though so it's not all bad. Also mediocre camera and all this is the best phone on the market right now in it's price range and even a little above it's price range. It's just a great phone and anyone that says otherwise must have got a dud or just don't know a good phone when they see or use one. For this price there isn't a better phone in the American market. If you have any ?s for me leave them in the comment section and i'll try to help answer any of your ?s that i can. Thanks for reading my review. I will still be making updates as I go along using this phone.

UPDATE: (2-23-16) Today the phone received a software update. Although it isn't the marshmallow update it does include January's security patch and improvements. I haven't really noticed anything different since the update other than the January security patch. It's still nice to see them update the phone in a timely manner. My LG G2 is still on Decembers security patch. If they push a MM update out in a timely manner they will be winners in my book because they did what they said they would do, which a lot of phone companies do not.

UPDATE (3-3-16) So I'm still loving my phone. I've got the window animations set to .5 and the force GPU rendering option turned on in developer settings and this phone is really fast! I'm super satisfied with this purchase. I've also noticed im getting so much better battery life with the GPU rendering turned on. If you haven't turned it on try it, you don't know what you're missing. It makes the phone buttery smooth. If you're still thinking of buying this phone go ahead and get it, do yourself a favor, you won't be disappointed!

UPDATE (3-10-16) Today I just achieved 5 hr. 15 min. of on screen time while my phone had been off the charger for 17 hr. 15 min.!!!! Still had 3% battery left! That is impressive. The battery on this phone continues to please me. I never worry about a mid-day charge. And that's with my screen brightness set on 68%! Just yet another reason for you to BUY this phone! I have also noticed that if I am out in the bright sun the text on the phone intensifies so I can see it better! You do have to turn that feature on in the settings though. So many nice features on this phone. I'm still finding out new things that this phone can do. Best phone I have had to date.

UPDATE (3-11-16) HONOR is now accepting beta testers for the Marshmallow update! You can go to the Honor Community page to sign up if you would like to test the new update before it is released to the public. I've already signed up. Better hurry though, it's first come first serve! Honor is making good on their word of updating this phone in a timely manner. It makes you feel good knowing a company is doing what they said they would. I can't wait for Marshmallow!

Hopefully the case will maintain its clarity and transparency for a while.

"Phone Camera Lens, Faireach 4 in 1 Cell Phone Lens Kit - 18X Zoom Telephoto Lens +180° Fisheye Lens + 120° Wide Angle Lens+ 20XMacro Lens Compatible with iPhone X XS Max XR/8 Samsung Android"

I've had an interest in add-on camera lenses for smartphones, but typically they are fit-specific to one model, and I tend to change smartphones annually or even more frequently. So, this universal design has some appeal to me because of the additional compatibility.
The universal design basically relies on a clip to hold a mount onto a phone. When in place, there is a screw that helps prevent accidentally opening the clip, then the various lenses attach to the mount.
The fisheye, wide-angle, and macro lenses worked as expected on my iPhone Xʀ, although all three had noticeable fuzziness around the edges. I was able to get nice results with all three although occasionally I would get unwanted glare or some reflections. Still, I was happy with the additional capabilities provided by the lenses.
The telephoto lens, unlike the other three, is about 4.5" long, which puts a lot of weight on the clip compared to the other three low-profile lenses. Also, because of the amount of zoom, you really have to hold the phone steady or use a tripod for best results. Again, I was able to get results using the zoom lens that were better than a digital zoom.
One improvement would be if the clip used clear plastic, since it can cover a small portion of the screen while in use.
Due to the universal design, I also used it with a Pixel 3a with similar results.
Overall, this is a fun to use set of lenses and the universal design helps make it compatible with multiple phones.
This replacement SIM tray is exactly as expected, with working SIM card and microSD card

I've installed many tempered glass screen protectors from various brands, including amFilm. As you'd expect, this 3-pack of screen protectors includes 3 sets of screen protectors and cleaning cloths.
The screen protectors have wings on each side to help with handling, and a notch for the camera which you can use to align during installation. The Razer Edge's screen is pretty much flat, so I was hoping that the protector would go completely edge-to-edge, but I found it left about 1.5mm on the left and right (when holding the Edge in Landscape), about 1mm along the top, and 0.5mm along the bottom. If you think the glass was intentionally narrower to avoid interfering with the Razer Kishi v2 controller, that doesn't appear to be the case because the controller never covers the glass.
The screen adhesive seems evenly applied and there are no signs of bubbling / halos around the border. As expected, touch responsiveness is unchanged.
Overall, I'm happy with the initial release of these screen protectors. I do hope that amFilm will release an updated OneTouch version that is completely edge-to-edge with a cutout for the camera.

I'm the type of person who shops for the screen protector and case for a phone before I actually buy the phone itself. The Moto G4 Play only came out in July of 2016 and I was concerned there would be few screen protectors available for it! I was happy to see Klear Cut had them available in both the clear and anti-glare versions. I've started buying the anti-glare version because it doesn't show fingerprints on the screen as easily as the clear versions do! Klear Cut gives you twice as many screen protectors as most of the other brands, so its's a no-brainer to get these ones. It turns out to be a good idea in my case, since I bought the Amazon Prime version of the Moto G4 Play and the Verizon pre-paid version as well. So I ended up using two of them. I've gotten good at applying these things so it's very rare I screw up an install. For people without much experience installing screen protectors, the extra ones in a Klear Cut 6-Pack might come in very handy!

I'm one of the people who picked this up from Best Buy during the 3 day sale they had on the Life Max for $79.95. It's now back up to $129.95 there. So here's my review.
BLU makes some nice inexpensive phones and the Life Max is no exception. It is fairly svelte for a 5.5" phone and has a fingerprint sensor on the back that's quite responsive. This must be one of the cheapest phones around to sport the fingerprint sensor and it's very nice to have this feature. The 1280 x 720 screen is decent, but many 5.5" phones have 1920 x 1080 screens or greater. Nevertheless, the Life Max's screen is very clear and readable. The Life Max's other big virtue is its huge, removable battery. At 3700 mAH, it ought to keep this phone going for several days on a single charge ...with light usage. The MediaTek processor used in the Life Max is the MT 6737, which is a slightly faster chip than the MT 6735 used in the BLU R1 HD, giving it slightly better performance.

Here's what I got on a couple of the common benchmark programs:

Geekbench 4
Single-Core 535
Multi-Core 1479

AnTuTu v6.2.7
27887

Let me say that I installed a PNY 64GB u3 micro-sd card [[ASIN:B01G26R652 PNY Elite-X 64GB microSDXC Card with Adapter - UHS-I, U3 - up to 90MB/sec (P-SDU64U390EX-GE)]] when I got my Life Max and set it up as adoptable internal memory, a really nice feature of Android Marshmallow. This might have a small effect on the scores I received. As you can see from those scores, the BLU Life Max is not going to blow away an iPhone 7 or Galaxy S7 in heavy game play, but it should play the less demanding games without dropping too many frames.

The camera is an 8 megapixel on the back and a 5 megapixel on the front (with flash on front and back!) The 8MG takes good pictures in the daylight, but don't expect great photos in low-light conditions. Phones in this price-range don't give you flagship quality cameras!

One of the nice features of this phone is its dual-sim ability. You could have a T-mobile sim in one slot and an AT&T sim in the other. The micro-sd card gets a third slot, so you don't have that "either-or setup" that many so-called dual sim phones have. It takes Micro-sized sims in both slots, so you may need an adapter if you have a nano-sim.

Unlike the BLU R1 HD, the Life Max includes a pre-installed screen protector and an extra one in the box, as well as a nice soft TPU case , which is really convenient, since there isn't much available case-wise, until Jan 30th.

The BLU Life Max uses the old micro USB-B instead of the newer USB-C and has the charge port on the top left of the phone which seems a bit odd! It would have been nice if BLU had used Android Nougat on this phone instead of Android Marshmallow, since this is such a new model and Nougat has been out for a while. BLU is not known for updating their phones with newer operating systems, although they come out with security patches every so often.

This a an excellent backup phone for someone who has a flagship model and a very nice starter phone for a teen or grandparent. I give it 5 stars if you can get it for less than $100 and 4 stars if you have to pay more than $110. The 5" Amazon Prime Moto G4 Play is strong competition at $99 and if you can only get the BLU Life Max for $120+, I'd spend a little more and get the Amazon Prime Moto G4, which is much faster and has a 1920x1080 screen. But you have to be a Prime member!!

My iPhone 6 WiFi and GPS had been failing after lightly dropping my iPhone on the ground (the antenna is located on the upper left corner), reinstalling firmware and resetting the phone wasn't working. My WiFi was running slow and connection was bad. My GPS would take a long time to find my location and at times I had to reset my phone for the GPS to work. This WiFi antenna replacement is very easy to do, it comes with the screw drivers needed to remove all the screws to access the antenna. The only tool that you will need is a suction cup to remove the iPhone screen. There are a few videos online on how to replace the antenna, it took me about 15 minutes to replace it. Once I replaced the antenna, my iPhone 6 is working like new now! The WiFi signal is strong and internet connection is fast and the GPS is able to locate me within seconds.

I have sheathed my cell phones in Spigen cases for at least 5-6 years. They are well-built and fit like a glove. They've protected my phones and I've never had a cracked screen. I've never been tempted to try any other brand, so keep that in mind, I'm a Spigen fan.

I recently bought a Galaxy Watch 4/44mm and wanted to try the Spigen Liquid Air case cover for additional protection. Of course, you have to remove the watch band to install and be mindful of the spring pins that have a tendency to fly off, never to be seen again.

Installing it was a bit of a challenge, but that's what you would expect for a close-fitting cover. The case does stretch but it still requires some effort. Once it is on, it gradually molds itself to the watch, although at first it is not quite form-fitting.

Once installed, be aware there are some pros and cons with this case, and they are to be expected. First, the case makes the two side buttons harder to press and I had to use a thumbnail to press them. Second, the case covers the edge of the bezel just enough that app buttons close to the edge of the watch face can be tricky to activate. Again, this is not a design flaw, it's the reality of this kind of case.

As a result, I have decided to use my Liquid Air case only when I travel and am out knocking around on hiking trails. It's really overkill for me to wear it at home or at work. That said, I'm confident it will provide the kind of protection I am looking for.

Altogether, a good product from a great company.

When I got my iPad Pro 10.5, I also got an Apple Pencil. I took it on a recent trip, thinking I'd start using it then. At one point, I became nervous that it would become separated from the iPad sometime and place when I wasn't aware of it, and I'd lose it, so I stopped taking it with me every day. Unfortunately, that was a last minute decision, and I didn't make a good mental note of where I was leaving it. I haven't been able to find it since then. Having lost it without ever having used it, I wasn't going to order it again: it's just too expensive. This looked like the answer to my needs, so I ordered it, and it does appear that it is, at a much more attractive price point. The pencil functions just like the Apple pencil does as it was demonstrated to me, except that you need to push a button to turn it on and off, and it charges by mini USB. I can live with that for all the money saved.

If my review was helpful, please click the button below.

First off, the one I received did not come with a Qi charger. Something "non-essential" is occasionally missing from Vine products, and it seemed like something I could supply, except that the ones I had, and could locate had given up the ghost. Now, problem solved, getting this up and running took a more complicated setup than most things do on Apple products. Then I discovered that it doesn't actually do what the product description says it does, i.e., locate your phone. What it does do, provided you have the card in the chosen object, is send a message to your phone in response to your activating the Safedom app on the phone, telling you where that object is – well, sort of. With my phone sitting on top of the purse with the "lost" item inside it, I got the message that it was about 150' down the street on the opposite side. Okay, so I tried pinging it to get it to ring, and got nothing, although that worked when I first set it up.

So, I don't know how useful this will be, though I'll keep trying. It will, at least help me find my wallet when I've taken it out of my purse at home.

Update 4/8/17

I went to Best Buy today and found a great Samsung rep that let me try the case out with the S8+.

First off, kudos to this guy, he and I chatted for a few minutes about fingerprint reader placement, facial/iris recognition, amount of pre-orders Best Buy has lined up (compared to the debacle that happened to many of us for the Note 7 release). He was great and only added to my brand loyalty.

I apologize if the photos are not up to quality, I didn’t want to wear out my welcome, so tried to snap fast. Some of the pics have the other Spigen case that I had pre-ordered for you to compare (The Spigen Thin Fit).

The Rugged Armor w/carbon accents went on and off easy, it’s rubberized which is a feature. This is my normal case for previous Notes and my LG V20. Solid access to buttons, clear and wide openings for power headphone jack access.
Although the rugged is just like my V20 the pocket fit was much slimmer. I’m guessing a few micro bits smaller due to the seamless edge of screen allows it to be a thinner fit. Thus I LOVE the pocket difference compared to a naked phone.

Back to the conversation today. They really feel that once confidence builds in the facial and iris security unlock is built up, people will rarely use the fingerprint scanner on back. One of the guys said and demoed his phone unlocking with his glasses on, very quick. He said that he checked his phone other night no glasses and it unlocked! I asked about Knox and using it with corporate MDM (we use AirWatch) and he said that it is supposed to support or surpass any company’s benchmark of security, so we shall see, but could be pretty cool.
He also mentioned and then I think realized he was giving away too much… That there is an option to swipe the fingerprint left or right and it open different apps like camera. That may be out there already, but if it isn’t, could be a cool addition.

Enjoy!

The OtterBox Commuter Series case for the Note 10+ is the ‘middle child’ of the lineup. Not quite the mass of the Defender but not quite as light as the Symmetry. Coming in at 2.4 ounces, for many G’lock phone owners, it’s just right! (Search the interwebs for Goldilocks Principle for some scientific fun)

It’s also part rubber part hard plastic… The rubber portions are quite pliable but more important are the side parts your hand uses to grip your big phone, making it for a very comfortable hold despite its size.

The photos will show it has a raised top and bottom to protect the screen from roughing it up when face down, has generous cut outs with rubber covered buttons. It has a port cover for the power port. Note: This is built into the design interior so no removal without tearing. It would have been nice for that to be an option.

I use magnetic mounts and wireless charging pads. With that rubber/plastic layered approach my magnetic mount had trouble keeping the phone in place. The phone found its charging sweet spot easily on the Sammy OEM charger.

I’ll keep this one in my bag for times that I need some extra protection over thinner cases, but with the quality of OtterBox and this nice grip, many are going to make this a daily case.

This is the middle child of the OtterBox lineup that says to its siblings “go show off all you want, I got this and am conformable in my skin”.

It’s huge. See my Sammy Note 20 Ultra next to it in the pics. I could put another phone… or perhaps they want you to be able to have a case for your phone installed too and can take the case!

The Hengwin Genuine Leather Holster/pouch/belt clip can hold the biggest phones out there, with room to spare.
A nice exterior feel from the leather, I would have loved to have same or suede like feel to cradle my baby as opposed to the vinyl like interior.
The belt ‘loop’ is not a snap on, which allows the rivets to be smaller but requires you to install in advance of the work day. You also get a hook for quick adding or installing on a backpack, man purse or well.. a belt loop.
It has a magnetic snap closure that is quite solid and easy to attach.

It weighs 88grams and has a proper genuine leather smell. Room to grow but a bit big for me at this time!

The Helloleiboo USB C charger 45W Mini with pull ring packs the right punch for your latest fast charging needs.

Please note, you won’t get super-fast charging when these phones are above 50/75 and 90%. Mfgs put governors on them so they won’t over charge or warm the phone up to the point of, well you know Samsung’s history with batteries!

That said when charging up my Sammy 21 Ultra 5G and my Pixel 6 Pro that were sub 25% I got incredible an appropriate charge rating for each.
Pic shows the details right off the unit: output is 5V 4.8A on USB-C.
From Ampere: 4710 mA!! (see attached)

Definite keeper.

That’s right, the excellently named DigiFunk USB Outlet is a nice piece of kit that adds two USB outlets - one a welcome USB-C to a standard outlet (single) box.

One nice feature of this particular outlet box is the nice slide in cable connections you can tighten with a screw. Much easier to deal with than the old screw models (such a pain) to the ‘push in good luck ever getting it out’ holes. The outlet also supports daisy chaining to the next outlet. The ground plug is also a slide in/tighten screw connection.

But what we all want to know…how does it charge?

Quite well it turns out. Each outlet is 100% independent. The power screen shot is my Pixel 6 Pro charging at a respectable 2430 mA on the USB-C port. After I plugged in the Ryobi USB battery, the number didn’t move. The standard USB port however only charged the phone at around 1100 mA (second shot).

For some reason, the USB-C port is a little ‘shallow’ in that the connector doesn’t go in all the way or click home. I don’t know if this is a defect or is just the way it’s designed, perhaps due to space considerations. It did not appear to affect the performance.

That’s the only thing that I’d say is a con... not enough to dock a star and if it fits the configuration, you are looking for, it great job.

The lugs on this steel band are nice. They fit tight and are machined with close seemingly tolerances, so they mate up to the cutouts on the Apple Watch body really well. It lays pretty flat, and is fairly lightweight considering it's stainless steel. The watch band sizing tool wasn't the greatest quality, but most aren't. The finish on the band is a brushed steel which looks great with my stainless steel Apple Watch, but it would probably look good with the aluminum models too. The lugs are a polished finish though, so something to keep in mind. They match the shiny chrome look of the stainless model perfectly.

As carriers are now dropping contracts and people have to pay full price for their phones (either up front or via monthly installment plans), folks are soon going to realize, "Oh wow - that fancy phone really IS going to cost me $700!!". So once this sinks in, many will eventually realize that most don't really NEED phones in the $500-$850 range. Enter the Moto G.

First it's important for people to realize that there are two variations of this newest model (3rd generation). For around $179 you have a version that has 8 gigs of built-in storage, and for around $229, you have one that has 16 gigs of built-in storage. Both will allow you to add up to a 32 gig micro SD card to add to that (some report success using higher capacity cards, others report glitches). There is one other rarely advertised difference, though - the 16 gig version comes with TWO (2) gigs of RAM, while the 8 gig version only comes with ONE (1) gig of RAM. RAM allows you to multitask more apps, speeds up app opening and closing a bit, and can in some cases help some with certain types of video use. So, should you splurge on the model with more RAM? Maybe - see below...

I first tried out the lesser of the two models and it did fine with everyday tasks like talking, texting, checking Facebook/Twitter, etc. But when I tried a more intensive app like Periscope (a video social media app), I noticed the phone at times, sporadically, sputtered and occasionally a green 'flash' would appear on my video broadcasts that both I and those watching me, could see. I also noticed that the 'available' internal memory was just under 4 gigs of the 8 claimed, due to the OS and other things pre-installed. So, I took it back and instead ordered this one, with more internal memory and that extra gig of RAM. I can see a difference. Apps open fractionally faster, Periscope video broadcasts flow flawlessly, and it just has a more "smooth" operating feel than the lesser spec version - though again, for people who ONLY want a phone for basic social media, email, etc... the $179 version of this phone would probably be fine.

The 5 inch 720p screen is bright and detailed. The 13 megapixel rear camera is leaps better than previous Moto models, though not quite as good as flagship phones upon full sized inspection. But since 85% of people who take photos with their cell phones rarely (if ever) actually print them, much less print them in larger sizes, I really don't think this is going to be an issue for most people. The photos are still nice and details in decent to good light - suffering from noise/grain a bit once the light goes down. The front facing 5 megapixel camera is very capable and detailed, but lets in slightly less light than those from the higher dollar flagship phones - in other words, you may need more light in the room when doing those selfies or video chats. Battery life is fine - if you're a VERY heavy user you're going to top-up before the day is done, but for low to medium users you should make it through the day. Adding to this is splash-resistance, so that helps if you get caught in the rain... just don't submerge it.

Mine was the unlocked version and I installed a Tracfone GSM micro sim card in it and it worked just fine. It should work equally as well with AT&T and T-mobile sims but I'm told it will also work with the likes of Verizon sim cards too - I wasn't able to test that, though.

PROS: Affordable. Good screen for price. Updates to Android 6.0. Decent battery life. Improved cameras over previous models. Micro SD card capable. Speedy and fast (especially the $229 version with the extra RAM).

CONS: Volume buttons are in the middle-center of the right side, so for some, this is where you grip the phone, causing possible accidental button pushes - once you get a decent case on it, it stiffens that area up some and helps remove the problem. Rounded shape of back creates a 'rocking' motion if you try to lay it flat on a table and text that way.

I've had to remove my original review and update it to this one, and unfortunately I cannot recommend the Moto G7 any longer. Shorter after my last review, my phone started having wifi connection issues whereas it drops your wifi on and off throughout the day, causing apps and processes to stall out. At first I thought perhaps I just had a buggy individual unit, so I did the troubleshooting steps I'm familiar with such as rebooting, going in and out of airplane mode, etc. Nothing worked. I even completely reset my phone to mfg settings and redid it. Problem still came back. Turns out there are quite a few Moto forum posts about the G7/G7 Power phones having this exact same issue. It is not just my unit - it affects quite a number of them. The most troubling part was that Moto reps were asking people in these forums to create phone logs and send them in, but this has been going on for months. No updates or fixes and answers no longer being posted in their own forum about it. Not even, "We are working on a fix, thank you for your patience", type of thing. Meanwhile, the problem for many continues.

For some tasks it's not a huge deal - you can simply switch off wifi and use LTE to do them. But things like video doorbells and some video security camera apps really do need wifi to work effectively (or, at all) - when you cannot connect to home security apps, yeah, that's a problem. Phone calls/texting continue to work as there seems to be no issue with the cell tower connections.

Nice screen, decent camera, Soso battery life. But it would be a good value money wise and a good, usable phone. But it's ruined by Moto/Lenovo's lack of addressing this KNOWN issue and their unresponsive attitude so far. So again, I just have to downgrade this to 2 stars and give it a "not recommended" rating.

Ok, this is NOT an $800+ smart phone, so no one should be comparing it to one. However, it wasn't that long ago that if you spend any less than $250 on a cheaper phone, it was absolute junk. This (when on sale) is less than $50 and it's perfectly usable for many people. The screen is decently bright and clear even though it's not ultra-high res. You get a basic amount of RAM (3 gig) And storage (32 gig) but you can add a micro SD card to that and use any number of apps that keep junk files cleaned out, etc. One speaker, but you can't expect stereo for this price; it's decently loud, though.

The cameras are average but in good light the rear one does really well - in lower light, not so much, but if you tweak the pics in a decent photo app you can make them better. The selfi cam is serviceable. Video quality tops out at 1080p and is also average. Tracfone can be hit or miss - they are NOT known for their customer service skills, but generally speaking the service works and there are few outages compared to some others. If you have an older person who just wants a "use at times" phone or a kid who needs their first smart phone, this won't break the bank (just PLEASE read up on parental controls on Android before handing one over to a tween/teen!!).

Once again - "for the price" I rate this phone highly. Just understand what you are and are not getting.

Then I got mine, and sure enough, it rippled across the middle and I was having a lot of trouble clicking things. BUT I took it off and put it back on again, and apparently one of the clips wasn't completely clicked and it was holding part of one side up just a tiny bit, causing a gap and ripple effect across the middle of the screen. I love the case, now. :-) Aside from the difficulty in putting it on (and avoiding the gap) it's perfect ... and the putting on difficulty is pretty limited. Now my son wants one for his G3 he'll be getting when he returns from boot camp, and I tearfully wish they made one for my GPad.

╠ ►My Review: This 12 x 12 thin solar charger does a decent job of getting power to your sources when you don’t have access to any otherwise. This is great for long hikes, backyards, festivals, camping, beach, tailgating, emergencies, etc. It won’t do a great job with the larger appliances keeping power but does good job on the smaller ones even when 2 are plugged in at same time. It has one connector is 2.1 and the other is 2.5 for more demanding power needs like your new phone without the need to buy a separate power controller. It has a very high sun efficiency rating which helps it as well. My only wish is that I could fold this to easily transport. Otherwise I love it and would highly recommend for your small power charging needs
