I have been using the Poetic Revolution case on my gold Galaxy S6 Edge+ for about a week and a half
now. Here are my thoughts.

When looking for a rugged case, the Poetic Revolution marks off every box, and then some. This is one
of the best, and toughest cases I have ever seen. When not at work, I need a case like this for my phone.
Hiking, camping, car shows, at the lake, my kids and I are always outside. I have 2 young sons, with this
case on, I am not concerned if they grab my phone and throw it around. I have an 85 pound puppy that likes to chew. Thankfully he has never gotten a hold of my phone, but I feel confident this case would protect it.

When receiving this case, I thought it was going to be all black, but it was not. It is black with very dark
brown accents. I personally liked this as it makes the case stand out more, but in a subtle way. I also
think it makes the case look really good with my gold phone. I also really like having the port covers.
While out on the sand rails and quads out in the desert, they keep the dirt out. When you are at home
with your phone on the counter, they can keep them covered long enough for you to save your phone
should something get spilled.

I have seen where some people have said that this case is very difficult to take on and off, however, I
do not feel that way. Is it more difficult than just a thin snap on case? Of course, but I have no concern
that this case is coming off my phone if it gets dropped. I have used cases that go on and off in the same
way in the past, and I prefer them. The way it’s made, it is thinner than an Otterbox type case, but in my
opinion stronger because you don’t have 2 plastic pieces snapping together.

The kickstand on this case is also among the best I've seen. In my experience, most kick stands are
made from a cheap material, or even if they aren’t, they pop out of the case so easily the little nubs that
are used to snap it into place break. Not here, where it is very tactile in extending it to use it, but it will not pop out of the case on you. One other benefit is that I have been able use it both horizontally and vertically.

When it comes to the loop at the top, I could go either way. I would be happy with a case that did not have it, but I think it could also be more useful with some minor improvements. If I am just hiking, I feel confident when hooking it to my backpack. However, when out riding in the desert, I do not feel that it is strong enough to hook on to my keys or someplace similar. If it kept the loop, I would use it more if it were reinforced with a harder material. Either way, I think this was a good idea to separate their case from others.

Though I was pretty sure they would not work, I did test both wireless charging and Android Pay (NFC) with this case on, and neither worked. This was not a big deal for me as I do not use Android Pay much, and I only wirelessly charge at work, where I can just take the phone out of the case if need be.

This case was provided to me by Poetic for my honest opinions. For the price of this case (currently $19.95 on Amazon), it is superior to cases costing twice as much. Though this is my first Poetic case, it will not be my last. I was so impressed with this case that I recently purchased a Poetic case for my Galaxy Tab A after the old case was chewed by above mentioned puppy.

I've ordered a ton of S2O Ultra cases already, and this one is definitely my favorite. The sides, corners, and edges remind me of a UAG case. Grip is awesome. The benefit to this one is having the bottom firing speaker that's channeled to you. This improves sound significantly. I have their similar case on my Pixel 4, but that one is a 2 piece case. This is only one piece, but I feel protection is no different. The aluminum frame around the back paired with the transparent back will really make my S20 Ultra stand out.

Puns aside, I am not very impressed with this case in a few regards. So I will do a different review here and state everything good/bad in bullet format:

The good:
- Thin enough to fit in my pocket with ease and comfort
- The orange and gray looks great, but a darker, more rustic orange was desired
- Drop-test wise, this will work to protect your product, and protect it well
- The kickstand works, and it works very well (but it does not allow pivot/angle settings)
- The grips on the side are amazing; you will not drop this holding it right or left handed with your hands gripped on these abroad both sides

The bad:
- The case has the worse volume and power buttons I have ever seen. They are so flat against the grooves that I can rarely find them, but due to muscle memory the process has become easier. Seriously, if you want to find those buttons on feel alone, look elsewhere. They are recessed so far they fail miserable in that regard.
- The inner layer required far too much pressure and bending of the hard shell to fit it exactly. And when I finally got it on, things got ugly, fast!

The ugly:
- This is not Screen Protector Friendly. I have tried TWO different screen protectors that were Case Friendly. This case flexed them BOTH and I lost money on both transactions. I ended up going to a brick & mortar and got a "liquid glass wipe". It's just friggin' weird, the wipe, and I am not sure I like it. I would have preferred this case worked. However, due to its protection level, I opted to stick it out to see how it goes. As it stands now I'll likely keep this liquid screen setup, but it wasn't my first choice. The case actually being screen protector friendly as advertised would have been nice.

This is a really nice case for the money. I've only had it for about a week, but already my phone (with the Ocase wallet case on it) has fallen from bed level onto a wooden floor, without damage. The case also looks good. I have two of them, the light brown and the darker brown, and I prefer the darker one because it's more masculine. While light brown ones has attractive contours, the color isn't as much to my liking-- but that's a matter of personal taste.

You pop the phone into a plastic-y frame which is mounted inside the leather(ish) wallet. It fits very closely, so you can't use another case on the phone and still have it work with the wallet. That's OK, though, because another case would probably make the whole thing too bulky. The case protects all of the phone's edges in event of a fall; the leather(ish) wallet edges wrap around on the phone's bottom and top edges, and the strap across the front helps to protect the power button side of the phone from impact.

One caution: you need to get in the habit of keeping the case closed with the magnetic strap in place all the time, because otherwise if the phone were to fall from a desk or from your hand it could fall screen down, without protection. (The wallet could flap open if the magnetic strap isn't fastened.) This is just another way of saying that this case protects the phone well, but only if you use all of its protections. We would all be in better shape if the Galaxy Edge weren't so difficult to protect with tempered glass screen protectors, that's Samsung's fault rather than any case manufacturer's.

Wireless charging: yes, it works with the case on, BUT you have to position the case just right. I have a [[ASIN:B01M0IL0QX Seneo PA046 Fast Wireless Charger 10W QI Wireless Charging Stand(Sleep-Friendly) - No AC Adapter]], and I can't just slap the phone-in-Ocase combo onto the wireless charging stand and expect it to charge right away. But if I place it on and maneuver it a little, the stand's green charging light goes on and the phone starts charging. Those reviewers who claim that the case doesn't work with wireless charging probably haven't gone through the maneuvering steps. Oh, and it helps to have the case strapped close when the phone's on the charging stand, because when an open flap pulls the case a little bit away from the stand and that impedes the needed contact.

Last but not least: OCase has terrific customer service. I contacted them with a problem, received a response within 2 business days, and got complete satisfaction. I highly recommend this case and this company.

Pros:

Cons:
Absolutely none

Review/Explanation:
I have tried many screen protectors and really like this because of its high quality feel. I have always felt that when I have a tempered glass protector, it feels that my phone is more protected because the actual protector is much thicker. The phone also has another degree of protection because of the tempered glass.
The box comes the with same things that other protectors usually come with. The protector and a microfiber clothe that will help clean the screen and the protector in the future. Also, there is a high quality squeegee that comes in the package to help with the installation. The installation was pretty easy for this. I actually messed up the first time so I picked it up and stuck it back without any bubbles! This is a great product because you get a whole kit from a reputable company including lifetime warranty, which most third party screen do not come with. I would highly recommend this protector to anyone because for a low price, not only do you get a screen protector, but an installation kit and a lifetime guarantee/warranty.
Overall, this is a nice kit and their is no downfall for this.

I was looking for a case for my new iPhone 6S & saw this one. I really liked the black edge on the case & the gold trim that goes around the case. I really like the red leatherette look on the back too. The gold trim comes off & the case slips onto the iPhone with no problems. Then you put the trim back over the case & you have a really nice looking phone. The case itself is thin, lightweight, sturdy, very well made; it really is nice looking case.
The biggest issue I have with it, though, is that the Apple Symbol on the back is not covered by anything. This means that it's possible for this area to get scratched and/or damaged. It's the reason why I gave this product 4 stars instead of 5 because it really bugs me.
To me, if you're going put a case on your phone to protect it from scratches/damages, accidental drops, etc.; why would you want this area to be left unprotected. When you remove case, the rest of the phone will look in great condition; but the Apple Symbol may have possible scratches on it. It wouldn't look very nice & for some people it would take away from the overall value of the iPhone. After all, you did spend A LOT of money for an iPhone & spent the time to find a nice protective case for it; would you then want this tiny area to be the only spot that is scratched up and/or damaged?? (Okay, maybe that's just me then. :) )
There are other case out there that also allows the Apply Symbol to be seen; but is covered by a clear plastic type covering. I'm not sure why Caseology cases similar to this one doesn't have this feature too. I wish they did. The only Caseology cases that I have found that doesn't have this is the clearback cases, like this one: Caseology® [Dual Bumper Clearback] [Gold] DIY Customization Fusion Hybrid Cover [Shock Absorbent] Apple iPhone 6 case.
In the end, I decided to return the case & get the gold clearback case instead. I really wanted to like this case a lot; but unforunately this little oversight was a deal breaker for me. I wish that wasn't the case (no pun intended ;D); it really is a nice looking case & I'm sure this little issue is not a problem for some people. If you're one of these people, then I say; get this case, you will like it.

I did a lot of research with all the different company's selling milanese watch bands & they all seem to be exactly the same. So I decided to go with the JETech's one based on reviews & price. I received it very quickly in a nice box & immediately switched out my silicone band.
I have to say I really like this band so much better then the silicone ones. It lets my skin breath & does cause any heat rashes like the silicone did. It was very easy to attach to the watch, just slide it into the band slot. Once it clicked into the slot; it felt very secure & didn't move (from side to side) at all. It's also very light weight compared to the silicone band too.
The magnet is not very big, but is very strong. It's definitely sturdy & does not slide, unless I physically move it. The only small issue I have is that when the magnet comes in contact with something metal that's near your wrist, it will stick to it. I discovered this while slicing vegetables with my chef knife (with a full metal tang). I ended up having to move the watch to my other wrist or just took it off to avoid this problem.
As yet, I haven't seen any type of 'color' fading or rusting issues with the black band which I'm happy about. I got it wet a couple of times while washing my hands; but it easily & quickly dried off with a paper towel. The milanese links are smooth to the touch with no snagging issues too. I have read some reviews where magnet comes unglued & falls off. This doesn't seem to be the case with this band...so far.
As of the fit, there seems to be plenty of play in the length of the band so it will definitely fit a small (which I have) to pretty large wrist. Oh, if you're wondering there is no company logo on the magnet part as well.
Overall, I would definitely recommend this band in black. It looks very stylish with my gold watch & is very comfortable for daily wear. A great alternative band compared to the official Apple one ($100+) at an awesome price (under $20).

This is the best option for the 6T, and I've tried at least three other brands.  The quality seems just as good as the official OnePlus tempered glass protector, which I had on my phone until it cracked after a drop. With a case on the phone, almost none of the screen is exposed.  The black border prevents the prism edge effect found with all-clear protectors.  It is definitely full adhesive, so the entire thing sticks to the phone screen.  It is not the type that has a non-stick dot matrix with adhesive only on the edges.  There weren't many bubbles after installation, and they were easy to work out.  I don't use the fingerprint reader, so I can't say whether it works with this protector.  My only complaints are there are no alcohol wipes included, and there were a few specs of dust on the sticky side that I had to remove with the included stickers.  Overall, I'm very happy and spent way less than I would have getting another OnePlus protector.

Probably the best protector, especially with the limited tempted glass products. Yeah, it only adheres to the phone via the black edges, which will result in decreased sensitivity along the borders, but, for the most part, it is still good. I see an extremely slight sensitivity issue in my experience.
I aligned everything perfectly. If you download "Galaxy Button Lights" from the Play Store, you can set the softkey backlight to always stay on so you can align the protector to not cover those keys when lit.
The only big issue I have, which I would not reduce my rating on, is the compatibility with the Samsung Keyboard Cover. The border keys--like A, P, shift keys--have issues due to the decreased sensitivity. Since the glass is slightly raised at the black edges, the case can register multiple presses. I'd have to press keys down pretty hard to alleviate most of the issue.
Other than that, really great protector. Don't jet these sensitivity reviews stop you. It's been about a week of use so I'll see how its durability rates.

First of all I want to make it clear that I am not one of those people who got this phone in exchange for a 'honest' review.
I bought this phone because I am a 'light' phone user & want to spend as little every month towards phone service that is possible. This brought me to Tracfone. $10 a month cards with triple minutes, plus carry over that stays active for 6 months at a time if you use the phone at least once in that time period. But then Tracfone got even better with android phones that are wifi capable (install text/calling apps to use on wifi without using up your phones minutes). See where I'm going with this?
My requirements for an android phone are to at least have a 4.5 inch gorilla glass screen, decent camera that is front/back facing with a flash, run nothing less than android 4.4, be on the Verizon network (a deal breaker. I refuse to use a phone on Sprint towers due to terrible signal & blackout days), no SIM card at all (too much hassle to activate).
This phone might not be running android 6.0, nor be an iPhone, doesn't have the latest technology but it works for what I want it to do & is what I was looking for in an android phone.
Activation is easy as well. Go online to Tracfone's website, click on activation & enter in the required information, on the phone itself click on what ever window pops up to complete activation, make that one last phone call & hear the recorded message that your phone has been successfully activated. Simple.
Now it might be a hassle to some people to use 2 separate phone numbers (a text/calling app phone number-plus your given Tracfone number) on one phone but if you really think about how much money you are saving each month/year by switching to Tracfone & using wifi for the heavier calling/texting/data (for free) it really isn't a hassle anymore. It just makes sense. Why pay more when you don't have to?

Overall, this is a very nice case. I have had 2 UAG cases in the past that I loved so I thought I would get the ICE (clear) version. The case is high quality and a combination of clear and frosted plastic. However, the clear plastic is slick, whereas the colored versions (black, white, rust) are more of a matte finish and somehow feel better and more secure in the hand. The ICE version is (IMO) harder to hold and shows fingerprints and any little speck of dust that gets under the case is visible. The black "rubberized" plastic is also slick and offers very little grip. If this is not an issue with you then this case would be great! I got 4 of these resin bands in different colors. I had to resize each one by 2 links which was easy with the tool provided with each band. Just read the instruction sheet and look for the arrows on the links before using the tool. The bands come well packaged and are also wrapped to protect them in shipping by a thick sheet of film. This film has paper "tabs. To assist in removal but the clasp area has another piece of film around it so it must be removed first. The top of the clasps are also individually covered with this film. It’s easy to miss and think the clasp is easily scratched when it’s just the film. Remember to remove it.

Connect the bands. Mine are for my 42mm watch. The bracelet part is 22mm rather than the 24mm which is the standard band size for most connectors. If you need to replace the connector to match your watch, you'll need the 42/22mm connector rather than the standard 42/24mm. You'll appreciate the slightly narrower bracelet of these bands because they are not a thin flat band. Also there is a technique to putting them on and taking them off of your watch. Start installing the connectors on both sides of the watch at the same time. Then gently push them in to the watch slot at the same time until they lock. Remove them by releasing both sides, in the same direction and remove the band by using equal pressure of your thumbs on each side while supporting your watch with your index fingers. I do this over the bed or my lap in case I drop my watch ...but I’m a clutz! Lol

This band would be great for either a woman or a man.

This screen protector doesn't come impressively packaged. However, packaging aside, within the package comes all the necessary materials for a complete, clean install on the phone. I have always loved an ESR screen protector, but have always preferred a screen protector that covers the entire front screen of the phone, including the front camera setup. Unless I overlooked it, I haven’t seen an ESR screen protector in the past generations that doesn’t have a cutout for the notch, which deterred me from purchasing them, but that was the only deterrent. This year, when I went to look for screen protectors for my iPhone 11 Pro, I saw that the ESR design now fits the design that I like to use on my phone - one that covers the notch.
As I remembered, this screen protector installs beautifully. I removed the film from the unboxing of the phone and had the screen protector in hand. No dust had time to settle, so my screen was completely clean. I aligned the screen protector (this comes with an alignment tool, which is very handy, though I don’t use them), released and the screen protector did the rest. In a matter of seconds, the entire protector adhered to the screen without bubbles and, once a case is installed, it is barely even noticeable that I have a screen protector installed. This screen protector is beautiful and installs so easily. As long as you have a clean device, don’t attract dust to the protector itself, and align well, this screen protector will go on extremely smoothly.

I am very pleased with the overall look and feel of this protector. It looks and feels like the natural screen, has a great oleophobic coating, and does not hinder responsiveness to the device in any way. The only thing I cannot comment on is the test of time. If history is any indication, this screen protector will hold up well to scratches and drops, but only time will tell with this particular model since the iPhone 12 models are new. From what I can observe so far, and apply from past experience, I definitely recommend this bundle. You can’t go wrong, especially with the price and with receiving three protectors.

This is a very nice case. It is crystal clear and looks very clean once applied to the phone. The case is very easy to install - simply snap in place and it grips your phone snugly and securely. It is comfortable to hold with the back side is a little slippery due to its clear nature, but the side offers a gripper material that helps combat that. The buttons are very enjoyable to use, being very clicky and tactile and are some of the best buttons I have used on a case (Mous and ESR offer the most natural feeling button click experience). The cutouts are precise rather than symmetrical, which is my preference, though it isn’t a deal breaker either way.
The magnet strength on ESR is the best I have used on any case aside from Phone Rebel and it is a close tie between the two. Once you attach your phone to a wallet accessory, a MagSafe charging bank, a floating charger, your accessory is securely attached without issue.
The case offers good drop protection with corners reinforced and raised lips to protect the camera and screen. These lips are profound enough to be noticeable, but not so much to add bulk. When combining this ESR case with an ESR screen protector, there is an edge to edge protection that leaves no gap, which leaves a clean look and excellent protection all around.
Overall, this case hits all the important things: excellent MagSafe strength, confident phone protection in a thin case, clean clear case for those who enjoy seeing the color of their phone, superb button experience, and works well with a screen protector. I definitely recommend this case.

This is a wet apply protector... the previous reviewer probably did not put it on correctly. If you are patient and do it right there will be no issues... follow these directions:

1. Use your bathroom to wash your hands with soap to remove any oils.
2. Turn on the shower to hot, and close the door and let the steam build up in the bathroom. This will remove any dust particles from the air and prohibit them from landing on your screen as you're applying the film.
3. MAKE SURE PHONE IS TURNED OFF!!! Clean the screen with a cloth and make sure there is no particles anywhere on the display... easy to spot with the mist of the hot shower on the screen.
4. Spray your hands and fingers with the included formula. Use lots.
5. Spray the front and back of the protector. Spray a little on the screen.
6. Apply the protector to the screen and easily slide it around to get the perfect position. ---> TURN OFF THE SHOWER... Enough mist.
7. SLOWLY squeegee the excess water from under the protector, starting from the middle moving outward. Make sure not to press too hard at first or you will move the protector and lose its previously perfected alignment.
8. Once most of the water is out from underneath, press the sides of the protector (along screen edges) to make sure it is sticking to the screen and stays flush. Be patient here as it will take some time to make sure all the liquid is gone from under the protector.
9. Use a blow drier to speed up the evaporation and tighten the film over the screen.
10. Any imperfections (white areas or smudgy looking areas) WILL resolve themselves within a day or two.

I've tried this on several phones using this brand and it works every time. These types of protectors are the only ones that work well with curved screens (I tried on elite x3, blackberry priv) and will last a long time with proper application.

Hope this helps.

I know you have too many choices in the phone case world, I have probably tried them all myself including Samsung Leather cases and the cute little LED case, but I keep returning to Spigen Ultra Hybrid cases. Here is why:

1. They protect my phone ( I have never had a phone break when dropped in an Ultra Hybrid and its happened a lot over the years as I've had a lot of cases. (including S3, S4, Note4, S5, Note 8, S8, S 20 FE, Note 20, S21, S21FE and not S22 Ultra). The amount of lip that protects the screen is perfect. Its not too cumbersome to use and still protects. Teh same for the rear cameras since most phones look like they could be an alien from another planet with 4 or 5 eyes.
2. The buttons still work. Spigen has always had great design and I've never had any issue pressing phone buttons when I have had their case on my phone.
3. People can still hear me. Believe it or not, there are many cases that don't have good alignment with the phone mic and it shows when your talking. Never had an issue with this case.
4. They provide excellent grip, but still can slide in and out of a jeans pocket. Nothing says hurry up like a phone call at a funeral.
5. Price. There might be more expensive guaranteed protection phone cases. but Spigen keeps the price affordable and I balance usability and protection. My wife drops her phone almost daily and the spigen case is still protecting daily.

Overall I am really happy with their cases and as long as they keep it up I'll be buying for my next phone model.

Designed primarily for Apple devices (as easily seen from the description) this multi-device charging station will also charge a regular USB, or USB-C device on the little carousel. So, as it comes from the vendor, it will charge your Apple ear buds, Qi-capable iPhone (or other), and another Apple device (or other) on the carousel for a total of three devices. Now, if you have a wireless chargeable Apple Watch (complete with magnetic charger), you can add that charger to this unit, raising the total to four devices. Here's the downside: 1) The wall adapter is not strong enough to power so many units simultaneously. The more you have on, the longer it will take to achieve a full charge. 2) Using the stand for the Apple Watch still requires the use of a separate charger. So, you will still wind up using two outlet sockets and having two charging lines on your nightstand. If you were looking for a single charging device to handle all of your Apple devices, this is close..... so close......... worth four stars, anyway..... :)

I have been using the Scosche Magic Mount system in my car for nearly a year now and I LOOOOOVE it!! If you use it too, chances are you probably read my review for the dash mount. However, I recently upgraded from an iPhone 6 to an iPhone 8 and my plate was stuck to the back of my case. New phone, new case, new magic mount plate. Also, my last magic mount plate was black. My new phone is gold, so I wanted to get a gold plate to match.

Everything arrived well packaged and with all the necessary instructions etc. I stuck it on without any problems and it works perfectly! All that being said, I have a few quibbles with these plates. First, the gold color of the new iPhone 8 doesn't quite match the gold color of these plates. The gold of the iPhone 8 has a rosier hue than the plates. I wish they matched better, but it's honestly not bad. Second, these plates do tend to have their color wear off over time, and that is based on my year of experience with the black ones. I had to color over spots with sharpie many times when the black rubbed off and the silver plate beneath was revealed. It just starts to look cheap and tacky after 6 months. Obviously that hasn't happened with this plate yet but I anticipate that it will and I will have to buy new ones. That being said, at $10, I can afford to replace the plates every 6 months. I only use the middle/medium size plate for my iPhone so I wish I had the option of buying a 3 pack of just that size, but I don't. Boo. The other two just go to waste. Lastly, I'm pretty OCD about things being straight and even, and it's a little hard to apply these plates just perfectly without using some kind of accessory like what comes with glass screen protectors to apply them evenly. I wish these plates would come with some sort of similar guide or accessory to help apply them squarely.

One final personal note: After much trial and error, I have found that the best place to put this place to use it with the dash mount in the car is at the bottom of the phone (see pics), horizontally. It holds it very securely, makes for the best viewing angle and doesn't interfere with wireless charging etc.

I hope this helps someone else! For the record I did NOT get this product for free or at a discount. I paid full price!

There are a lot of these to choose from but I like this one for a few reasons:

1) It works with a magnet I already had on the back of my phone from a different company
2) Unlike other similar products, this CD slot mount comes with a locking mechanism that keeps it securely in place
3) Unlike other similar products, this CD slot mount doesn't fill the entire CD slot which means I can move it to one side or the other to get ideal placement. For me, the full CD slot ones that have the magnet in the center covered up my dash clock when I had my phone mounted. It's a little thing, but with this one, I can move it to the far side so that my clock and my phone are both clearly visible.
4) I love that you can literally get any angle you want using the ball adjustment and you can also turn the magnet sideways which I've used to capture video while I'm driving.

No there isn't anything exceptionally fancy about this mount but I have ZERO complaints and have used it every day for months now with my iPhone 8. I can absolutely recommend!

This case is slim and sexy looking, extremely high quality and offers excellent protection for being so light weight.  It's super easy to put on and take off my new iPhone 11 Pro and I feel like it's really protecting my investment!  I can easily slip it in and out of pockets and purses and it doesn't add too much bulk to the device itself.  That being said, while overall it is very well designed, there is one important area in which it is not well designed at all, and that is that it's much too slick to hold one handed.  Since it's supposed to have been designed with gamers in mind, I would think gamers especially - let alone every day people - would want a case that has has enough friction to keep it securely in your hands.  Unless you have perpetually clammy palms (eww) the smooth plastic of the case makes it really hard to get a good grip on - especially during one handed operation.  I used it for about a week and a half, and while I loved the look and the protection, I ended up swapping it out for an Otterbox clear shell case that is MUCH easier to hold while still slipping in and out of pockets with ease.  This case would be SO much better if at very least they had incorporated some silicone dots on the back or the sides that would make it less slippery and easier to grab on to and hold.  So while the quality is really great and I love the look, I have a hard time recommending this case.

I will first tell you that I'm a compulsive perfectionist who is very difficult to totally please but this phone is a pure BOMB!!! I'm very, very pleased with it. Is it "perfect", NO but it's far superior to the $500+ phones that AT&T sell and there is no bloatware on it. All of the AT&T apps on my Play Store list, I just hit the "X" in the top right corner...and doing that felt really good.

The app's provided by Huawei for email, music, video are far better than anything you can find it the Google Play Store.
Double tap to wake up and provided Screen Lock app to turn off are very convenient.
Any apps that I didn't want and could not delete, I just disabled.

Things that I consider suggestions for improvement...
1. it's sometimes a bit slow to redraw the screens but so what.
2.. the WiFi is not dual-band; it's 2.4GHz only.
3. not crazy about the default launcher because there is no way to hide/remove apps that you do not want on your desktop so you have to create a "crap app folder". To solve this, I just applied the Google Now launcher which works perfectly...
4. without installing a 3rd party glass screen, phone gets way too many fingerprints
5. without using protective case, phone is too slippery to handle and the power button in located right where you grip the phone. I would like to see the power button relocated to the top of the case.
6. would like the 3 navigation buttons to be part of the phone hardware, not the android OS. these buttons rotate with the screen which is a sometimes little awkward and confusing.
7. when charging, phone displays a green light...i would like to see it red when charging and to turn green when fully charged.
8. phone is slightly too big and too heavy...smaller and lighter would be better for my taste.

If Huawei offers a phone exactly like this one only with more RAM, a faster processor, 5GHz WiFi, and a little smaller screen would be nice but not if not still OK, count me in! I'll buy it immediately but until they do, this phone is still tops on my list.

Am I satisfied with this phone = YES
Do I recommend it = YES
Would I purchase it again = YES

This is the one I'm planning to use.  Prior to buying this case I put on a glass screen protector and have no intentions of removing it and touching the screen so far has not impeded the use of the phone. The headphone jack and charger port are covered, the speaker is not.  The volume up down and power buttons are completely covered and are easy to push.  The case is a little bit bulky and does add some weight especially if you're planning to carry it in your back pocket. But that is a small price to pay to keep your phone safe. There is a nice lip around the top of the case and the frame also covers the edges of your phone so I'm hoping that if this phone drops face down it will be protected. The only way to really understand how bad this case is, is to compare it to the same case that they released last year for the Galaxy S9. That was probably one of the best cases made for that phone.

It would then stand to reason, that if you were to buy the same “rugged” case for the Galaxy S10, the only difference would be that the new one would fit the new phone, right? Well, it’s unbelievable to me how much effort they must have spent looking at all the things that made the last case great, and making sure that they either removed those features or tampered with them enough to make them negatives for this iteration of the case.

1) The previous model had a hard shell that went over the corners and edges enough to give the phone protection from accidental bumps and drops. This version removed the shell around the edges and corners and kept it strictly on the back of the case. Make sure you only drop your phone flat on its back!

2) The side-protection for the S9 case was robust. There was a few millimeters of the soft TPU/silicone material that was thick enough to absorb shock. The S10 version has about 1/3 of a different material around the edges. The new material does not feel as soft.

3) That material used on this new S10 case is also very pliable. As you can see in one of my photos, it takes very little effort to peel the edges back from the phone. Sliding the phone into a purse or pocket will inevitably pull the top and bottom edges back, accumulate dirt and lint, and raise the risk of the phone falling out of the case which is actually not very snug around the phone anyway.

4) The interior of the S9 case was well-padded and felt soft to the touch. The interior of the S10 case is harder and as you can see in the photos, it’s so thin, that the ridges from the exterior shell are clearly visible through the silicone layer. Also, the name stamp on the inside of the S10 case is made out of a hard plastic-like material that I don’t want rubbing against my phone. The stamp on the S9 case, was made of softer material.

5) The fit on this case is semi-snug at best. The phone practically slides in without any pressure. How easy must it be for it to slide out accidentally? The previous version required significant pressure at the right angle to snap the phone in. This one does not at all!

Bottom line: If you thought you were getting a top-of-the-line case because you loved the last version, or because you see the brand name… Think again. This is a low-end case not worth its hefty price-tag, and you should only get it if you are extremely careful with your phone and don’t expect to expose it to any perilous situation at all.

Good wireless charger. The sensor can tell as soon as I put a phone on it, and it closes the clamp and begins charging. The sides have three protrusions that extend or contract as you turn the first dial on the base. Extending them of course tightens the base against the cup-holder and gives it a snug fit regardless of what size the cup-holder is.

The only thing is that the extension pole only goes up about 3.5 inches. Considering how the charger itself is 3 inches from its center down to the bottom holder, that’s barely enough clearance to move the phone freely. I get it though, the pole can only be as long as the base is deep, but maybe using a telescoping design in the future would be a good idea.

Honestly, I was just hoping to get something good enough to protect my new phone until I could find something better. However, this case is perfect. I can’t think of any reason that would now make me look for a different case. I guess I thought that because of its low price I was going to get something of lower quality, but I didn’t.

I didn’t know what they meant by hybrid on this case. Usually, I think that means that it’s a two-part case, but here it just means that some of the case has a rubberized and some of it has a carbon-fiber finish. The case feels luxurious in my hand in that it’s not a hard plastic stiff case. It’s got a good 'grippy' feel to it, and the inside certainly has the soft and padded feeling that I want for my phone.

The case barely adds any bulk to the phone and it does not interfere with wireless charging. I also like that it covers all the corners and the buttons all the way. The only exposed areas are of course the screen, and the camera section on the back. Also, the top and bottom edges of the case are raised just enough so that if you put your phone face-down on a flat surface, the screen won’t touch. I think I lucked out onto a high quality case for a low price.

When it got unbearable, I installed the second one, using the method provided. Fast. And yes.. easy but I got dust under it and it is NOT easy to rid the dust. So. Yea. Still easy to mess up. Yikes. It could register some touches at times. I think perhaps it compresses the display. ??? I wish I knew. I sadly had to remove it and am now waiting for another option to arrive.

Update, interestingly enough it works decently with just the front screen protector clipped into place. I kind of enjoy using it this way even though the back remains unprotected. I don't think there was an issue with needing to adjust the display sensitivity. I think the problem is that the case as a whole somehow compresses the display and it registers as one long continuous touch, so no other touch inputs can be read.

Update again SOLUTION, I turned off a setting that is supposed to keep the edges from registering a false touch and now it's working as well as everyone else's so I bumped this back up to four stars.

I reviewed this earlier for three stars; two attempts at installing it, failed and ultimately removed it.

Why am I leaving it at three stars? Because with a little more solution and a third protector I would have probably got it.

Unfortunately, dust just kept getting in (even did it at work/office to avoid pet dander) and fine particles just kept getting in. The liquid hides these particles and the "let it dry" part of the instructions really exasperates the situation. By the time it dries, you need a lot of solution to get back there, clean it, reclean for particles, etc.

I came close on the second protector by using a flat piece of plastic (no fabric) and applying pressure with a corner of it. I was able to push out the liquid bubbles early and see if there were any spots remaining. It also dries extremely fast this way. Unfortunately, there were two spots in mine. One I got out, but another particle got in another area. I was running out of solution. The other... was a part where I assume the camera punch hole area got stretched and raised.

On the parts I did do well, it looked okay actually. The fingerprint scanner also worked fine through it.

Another review mentioned that after particles got in the protector was permanently deformed. I did not experience this, I was able to flatten those areas. For me, it was botching the first install, the stretched spot on the punch hole on 2nd protector, and just plain running out of fluid.

Maybe you'll have better luck. I didn't.

So far I really like these triggers. Instead of my hands hurting trying to claw the screen/phone. I’m able to hold my phone more comfortably. Resulting in a better mobile game experience.

I have tested these on an iPhone 11 Pro Max w/otter box symmetry case. Also an iPhone 12 Pro Max w/Apples clear MagSafe case. The triggers fit tightly/snug but they do fit and work properly. Without a case the triggers easily fit.

The only games I have that can utilize the button customization are Call of duty and PUBG. Triggers work for both games.

The rainbow-ish colors on the clickers are ok. If you like multicolor they are nice.

The triggers have a very good clicky sound and tactile feel to them.

You can tap on all 3 button areas and it will produce a tap on the screen. Much better than a single L or R clicker. So it should work for most people’s hands/phones.

There are only two areas on the triggers that have a soft material to protect from scratches. One contacts the screen and the other the back of the phone. So the edge of your phone will touch plastic, I would recommend a very thin case. If you don’t care about scratches on the sides then no worries.

These triggers might be uncomfortable in a pant pocket. I think they would be perfect in a coat pocket or backpack.

Overall I enjoy these triggers and would recommend them over others I’ve tried. Decent price for nice mobile triggers. If they go on sale don’t miss it! Definitely worth a try!

Pros: Call quality is great on Verizon. Opening websites, Google images is actually faster than some of more expensive phones I've tried before! Action camera takes rather decent videos. In right lighting, photos come out crisp. Stylus is useful in taking quick notes. Dual speakers and headphone jack are great fratures to have and keep.

Cons: battery life for a 4000 mah phone is rather disappointing even for first day use. Roughly 5 hours of screen on time. Moto display, where you wave your hand to turn on screen is absent. This was one useful feature from Moto. Instead you get one tap checking. Last but not least is pure Android issue. Many Android phones allow you to control notification volume separately. Unfortunately, Moto follows Google Pixel's pure form and removes this feature. You'll have to silence ringer or media volume to silence your texts which seems like a huge step backwards.

Conclusion: overall, this is a solid device for its price. With only a battery letdown being a clear issue, the G Stylus can be a great daily device for anyone who doesn't wanna spend $500-$1000+ on a phone.

I ordered one that came with some factory imperfection in the back. Now I'm using a second and I love this phone compared to my s10! You'd think the speakers aren't good but they are clearer sounding than Samsung. Samsung's sound is louder but doesn't match the quality of LG and don't forget LG has the HiFi quad DAC. For anyone who's not familiar with this tech, it's high quality sound through headphones, especially expensive high impedance ones. The screen becomes your speaker and earpiece. This means you can hear your conversation in a loud area and you can put your ear anywhere on the screen instead of trying to find the earpiece! This is very convenient when you do the shoulder hold. Front camera shoots awesome selfies, with ok lighting you can see the color of your eyes! Back camera is great too, can't wait to take it for a manual video spin!

P.s. Don't be surprised, this Amazon exclusive model doesn't have the Google assistant button on the left because Alexa is the default assistant (you can change that to Google). At first this disappointed me a little but I realized that removing the extra button makes it easier to use your phone with a tripod/selfy stick/gimble. It's just awkward since every case is made for the regular models so you end up with a button on the case.

Everything on the user end is great. I hear it may not have been a while ago before a lot of updates but I find it a nice phone to look at and use. It's a fairly compact phone, as far as phones go these days. I appreciate that too as it makes good use of its size.

However, I find it odd to call this the essential phone and not include a headphone jack or the option to expand memory with an SD card.
What if my phone dies? An SD card makes that much less of an issue to deal with since you can backup to a little hard drive you can always pull out. Plus, it makes it so much easier for someone with a huge music library to transfer the music. I just move the card over...but with this, it's drag and drop of a huge file that takes almost an hour and sometimes not everything transfers properly and I never know why.

Anyway, back to the lack of a headphone jack...I find it mostly upsetting because it should be more water proof than it is especially without a headphone jack. So to take that away and have it's IP rating lower than a lot of it's competition is frustrating. But it's ok. I never dunk my phone in water or risk that really happening. And most of my headphone use is Bluetooth but I do use a standard one sometimes. And there's always the adapter, I guess.

Overall, a really great phone that stands out in the competition. I think it's totally worth the money and a lot of my quarrels with it are often things I would say about other high end phones too.

I hope there is a sequel to this phone that makes it even better. I'll be keeping an eye out for that.

Ever since I got the new Galaxy Watch 5 Pro I have been on the quest to finding a good watch band for wearing all day. The Samsung D-buckle looks nice, but is just too bulky for typical all day use. The Sports band is better, but still not comfortable for all day wearing. Both bands I find myself having to adjust the band throughout the day multiple times as they put slight pressure on my wrist making them uncomfortable and as my wrist swells or shrinks I have to keep adjusting the band. Just not practical for me and not to mention when it's warm outside you sweat and the band tends to stick to your skin. I just hate that feeling.

Well now to this loop band. Looks category it may not looks the coolest, but for fitment it has to be the best band that I have tried. Put it on and you will forget the band is even on your wrist. I haven't had to adjust the band once all day. The loop band is lightweight and somewhat elastic. Since the band is softer it contours to my wrist shape and does not put any odd pressure on certain parts of my wrist. And as my wrist gets larger throughout the day from either eating too much salty food or excercise the elastic band can stretch slightly. It can stretch just enough so that it doesn't put any pressure on my wrist.
The loop hook is not too hard to use. My mountain bike shorts have the same hook and loop design and once you get used to it you may like it.

The no gap design when put on the watch is nice. Doesn't looks too cheap. The provided pins are kinda odd as the sliding pin part stick out when installed, but it doesn't come to your skin. Well not even close on my Watch 5 Pro it doesn't.

This is now going to be my everyday band that I wear to work and for my everyday use. But if I'm going out for a work meeting or a fancy event I would just swap the band back to the D-buckle as it looks nicer.

If anyone is looking for a comfortable watch band for your bulky watch I would recommend this band. It may not look the prettiest, but sure is very comfortable to wear. Not sure how the band will hold up to sweat or long term use, but for the price I would gladly buy more and replace the band when it wears out.

So let’s start with the fact that there are not many metal/aluminum stands available so when I saw this one I was pretty excited. It’s made well and folds nicely if you’d like to travel with it. The cloth/foam pad is also of good quality and was a nice touch. Having a usb-c cord with a forward right angle added to the charger nicely as well. They even put cut-outs in the bottom so you can use the phone for FaceTime or speakerphone without it blocking the microphone or speaker. All this said, at this point, it’s just a very well built stand. But, it’s supposed to charge! Ahhhh! THAT’S where we have the issues. The first problem is the light. It’s under the bottom and not bright at all so you can’t see if the light is green, red, blinking, solid, or even on at all. The other issue is the top coil of the charger. This is the coil that charges your phone when standing upright. It has a bad alignment. I had an iPhone SX Max and when placed vertically, the screen would light up and make the charge noise. The lightning bolt would show in the battery and all these would tell you that the phone is charging. 30 seconds later, if you checked, the lightning bolt would be gone and if you looked underneath at the status light, it would be red. You would have to remove the phone from the charger for approximately 10-15 seconds before replacing it to get the charger to charge again. But, rinse, repeat. It would do the same thing all over again. This also happened to my new phone (iPhone 12 Pro max) as well. It would charge fine on either phone if you put it horizontally. But after a few weeks, this was the only way it could be used as a charger. It’s a real shame because the rest of the product is so well made. I sent it back for a refund. It’s puzzling how much thought and quality went into the stand, and the charger was just an afterthought.

I see a lot of review on wireless chargers and honestly, a lot of people don’t know anything about charging and rechargeable batteries. I went to school for audio/video and have a good background in electronics and I use rechargeable batteries for many applications. That being said, I have more knowledge about these things than the average person. If all you are looking for is the fastest charger possible then you want to look elsewhere. However, read on to see why this charger is fantastic! When you charge a rechargeable battery, the faster it charges, the hotter it gets. The hotter it gets the faster it breaks down and you get less cycles. So what this means is when you charge at a slower rate, your battery will continue to work at optimal performance for a longer amount of time. Not per charge but lifetime. Fast charging is fine at times but plugging your phone in will charge it faster than a wireless charger with less heat. This charger is made of aluminum so it’s very sturdy. It charges at the optimal rate which is slower than a fast charger so it is the best charger for your bedside for overnight. The light on the charger comes on and flashes for a minute before turning off so as not to bother you while sleeping. I have used this charger for overnight for quite some time now and have not had any problems. If you set the phone in the charger and it’s not aligned properly the light will flash. Other chargers I have used do this too but you have to remove the phone from the charger for it to reset before you can place it back in for charging. With this charger you only have to slide it over and once aligned, charging begins. If you want a wireless charger for your bedside that won’t blind you with a bright light, won’t cook your phone, and will charge your phone at a good rate, this is the charger you want. One thing that some people have stated is that this charger won’t work with some power adapters. This is true as it is meant to charge at the proper rate and if your supply is trying to supply more power than it needs, it’s safety features kick in and prevents the charger from working. This is a protection circuit and not a flaw in the chargers design. I have ordered 5 wireless chargers from Amazon and I like this one more than all the others.

Unfortunately, this device is my only camera so I can't take a picture for reference. This screen doesn't really fit this phone. I installed the first screen, and thought I must have surely messed it up (never had an issue installed temper glass screens before), so decided to take it off and install the spare screen. Same results. The honor 6x has slightly contoured edges on the screen. This protector isn't curved at all, which causes there to be clear spots around the left right and bottom of the screen where you can see its not fully adhered to the screen. As another review stated, it looks like a halo around your screen. The glass stops where the screen starts to curve, where I feel it would do better if the glass was made larger and curved down on the edges. I would pay the same money for a single screen that was curved like that. Because it lifts off the edges, I find it hard at times to get touch screen things to function on the edges. I would recommend finding a true fit screen or going with a harder to install plastic protector that would be able to follow the curved edges of the screen.

UPDATE:
So after my initial testing and 1* review, sparin reached out and wanted to make it right. They told me the halo issue happens due to incorrect installation and that its harder than it seems to install properly. They then proceeded to send me a free replacement screen pack, along with 3 links showing them installing it with various methods. Replacement pack came in today. Removed the old screen, watched their videos and took a lot of time placing the screen on. Used the sticker hinges but they don't work very well in my opinion. First screen lined up seemingly perfect, but alas the good ole halo and this time there was a bubble on the left side. Scrapped that screen and decided to give the last one a try. This time I abandoned their guides completely. Wiped down and cleaned for about 5 minutes to make sure the bubble wasn't caused by dust or anything else. Then I removed the protective film and began laying the protector down. I used the camera and sound bar at the top as a guide and laid it down top to bottom very slowly starting from there. Used my thumb and pointer finger of one hand( the hand holding the protector), one on each side, to make sure it felt it was completely evenly laid on both sided. I used my other hand to rub the top of the screen as It went down to make sure no bubbles were placed. In the end its now on, no halo, no bubbles. Fits well. But out of all the glass protectors Ive installed on phones, this was by far the hardest (also first 2.5d screen ive installed on which probably makes a big difference). Lesson: take your time. Reaaally take your time and this will come out fine on the phone. Took me a total of 4 screens to get this installed 100% perfect.

Installed this screen on my mate using the overnight directions to allow proper curing. Installation is pretty simple. Prepare clean workspace, clean hands, clean screen(get everything off as it will be stuck underneath) spray solution on hands, spray on top of protector, peel from backing, coat bottom of protector with solution,align on screen, gently use cloth wrapped around a credit card to squeeze excess solution out of the sides of the screen while keep one hand on the protector to keep it aligned, once that's done wait 20 minutes to adhere to curved edge screen sides. Just take your time and it's easy to 1 shot this application.

Overall this looks great. Seems to carry full touch capabilities and hasn't received dents in the screen protector from things running against it. Fits within the boundaries of the spigen case I have on it. Only negative I have is the top left corner wants to bubble off and I need to press that specific area back down once a day. Otherwise this works really well.

If it didn't come with 3 protectors, I wouldn't have anything on my screen right now.

Screen 1: The glue didn't spread to every part of my screen. Chopped that up to a loss as I maybe didnt have it on the most level surface. On to screen 2.

Screen 2: Found a level surface (used my level). Glue spread great. Too great. Glue ended up attaching to one of the 'bumpers' and took my screen off when I went to remove the bumpers. That seems like a design flaw. On to screen 3.

Screen 3: Left the pumpers out. Used only 80%(ish) of the glue in the tube as it was clear a full tube was too much glue. Application was perfect with 0 issues.

Screen protector seems great. Method to apply was not great. Probably will just order the whitestone set if I end up having to replace this protector.

For the price, the case is pretty damn good. Makes the phone easier to hold, and definitely helps protect against drops. 6 months in, and the case has completely failed. The back plastic has come loose from the rubber edge. It started to separate from one corner about a week ago, and allowed dirt to get between the plastic and glass back of the phone, and now I have minor etching issues. Within a week, the case failed completely, and is no longer usable to protect the phone. I’ve used Spigen cases for all of my phones, and this is the first one that has failed, and did so in very short time. Better bonding and a slight bit tighter fit would make this a near perfect case. Unfortunately, because of the nature of the failure, I can’t recommend this case for long term protection.

Other than an issue with the first case for my iPhone 8 Plus, I’ve always loved Spigen cases. They fit great and definitely protect the phone. This one, however, does not fit properly. The bottom right corner doesn’t “lock” over the corner of the phone, and will pull back in my pocket. I’ve had this case for 2 days, and I already have dirt particles embedded in back (it took over 4 years for this to happen on my 8 Plus, which is perfectly acceptable to me). I will be returning this case immediately, and will be searching for a better case, that does a better job of protecting the phone and not causing damage. The ergonomics are stellar, but this doesn’t do me any good if the case doesn’t actually protect the phone.

This screen protector does a great job of hiding your screen from onlookers. It shows dark to anyone who’s not looking directly from the front of the screen.

I gave 4 stars out of 5, however, because it was not as dark as a screen I had on my iPhone 7. That brand was no longer available, though, so I actually bought this brand AGAIN for my iPhone 12 Pro Max. That’s the best recommendation I can give.

Another point to note is that this screen protector doesn’t interfere with using the iPhone for payments and gift card scanning when shopping, which is something that was a problem on my prior screen protector.

Installation was super easy thanks to the included frame. You simply clean your phone screen, snap on the guide, then drop the new screen cover into place. Alignment is perfect with almost no effort!

The ultimate test of a screen saver is whether it actually saves your screen. After dropping my phone onto the edge of a granite counter top, I can definitely attest that my screen survived without a scratch thanks to the screen protector sacrificing itself for the greater good. And I had a backup!

Got this as backup power for my upcoming photo safari. After testing it out quite a bit, I'm confident that it will be a great add to my bag.

The capacity is among the highest allowable on flights. It had enough juice to completely recharge my laptop AND my DSLR camera battery. The charger itself charges completely in about 2.5 hours using my 100W laptop charger. I also like the Qi charging pad on the top for fast charging of my phone, too, while powering other devices.

The only down side is this thing is HEAVY! But, it's a battery and that's what batteries do - a lightweight battery would not have the power I need. Overall, I am extremely happy with my purchase, and looking forward to my trip.

Over the years, I've written several reviews of Encased Clip Cases and iPhone cases specifically.  I'll keep this short.  If a case I want to own can't fit an Encased Belt Clip, I won't buy the case.  Encased offers the perfect place to keep your iPhone in a case when not in use.  It protects it from falls and is engineered to stand the test of time.  And I can say this with confidence, because I've owned several with each new iPhone I've used.

Over the years I've purchased several replica Apple Sport Bands. Most were very inexpensive. And most, if not all were disappointing on many levels. They're silicone for starters and just don't match the feel of the high quality Fluoroelastomer's Apple uses. They feel sticky and they're dust magnets. And once they get dirty, they're pretty much rubbish.

This Sport Band from DONEGANI, is in a league of its own. It's not cheap, but compared to the price of an authentic Apple Sport Band, it's more than 50% less expensive. Yet is is in every way as high quality as an Apple Band and that's simply AWESOME. The way it comes packaged speaks volumes about how DONEGANI expresses quality. One has to admit that opening an authentic Apple band is an experience. And opening this band is as nice. It comes in a box with the color of the band you ordered on the front and inside it's packaged just as though Apple had packaged it. Kudos to DONEGANI for making the presentation special.

Now, the band. It's exceptional in every way. The connectors, which are made with high precisioned 316 stainless steel slide into place smoothly and lock in place resoundly. The button you attache the two pieces together with is highly polished 316 stainless steel as well. It has that Apple feel, not sticky and won't attract dust. It's very lightweight and supremely comfortable.

These are truly remarkable and a true bargain. I vowed long ago not to buy any more cheap, inferior quality bands made of sticky silicone, which won't last more than a couple months. This band isn't like a cheap band in any way and with all the really cool colors they offer, for the price, I know I'll buy more. What better compliment can one give?

This case for iPhone 14 Pro Max from Anywest, is simply AWESOME AWESOME AWESOM!!. Not only is it MagSafe, but the MagSafe circle slides out from the back of the case to become a stand, which you can place your iPhone vertically or vertically. It's super durable and was tested with 6000 drops from a height of 10 feet with no damage to iPhone. NICE!

The materials used in this case are high-grade hard PC, with a matte finish. The color of your iPhone will come through, but you won't be bothered by seeing dust or debris through completely clear cases. And the hardshell back will provide extreme protection in a drop. The side of the case are made of diamond-shaped airbags and TPU material with a 4 layer buffer structure. Translation? Your phone will be cradled in top-notch materials and should withstand falls without any issues.

The MagSafe circle is surrounded by a clear magnifying glass type plastic, which lets the full color of your iPhone come through and highlights the Apple Logo on the back of your phone. There are visually interesting cues, in the MagSafe/Stand circle, which says "Original Design Magnetic Ring Stand" with dashes going the rest of the way around the circle. Is it necessary? No. Does it look cool? Yes.

All buttons are nice and clicky, yet protect the buttons on both sides of the phone. Other cutouts for speakers, etc., are perfectly aligned. Holding the case in your hands is nicer than other TPU cases I've owned. It just feels more secure in my hands and I really appreciate that. The lip on the front of the case and back of the case for the cameras raises the case enough to ensure neither your iPhone screen nor cameras will be scratched if placed face-down or face-up.

All in all, Anywest included a lot of great engineering and technology in this case. You paid over $1000 for a premium iPhone and for the price of admission for this case, it's more than reasonably priced for all the greatness you get.

I've spend the last two weeks buying, testing (and unfortunately returning) a lot of watch bands here on Amazon.

Just last week I came across this 7 dollar quick release silicone band and decided to give this one a shot.

I'm very glad I did because this is not only the least expensive, but also THE BEST silicone band I've come across here on Amazon.

Why do I say that?

Well, this is my personal opinion of course:

I prefer all black, smooth, minimalist bands and this one fits the bill just perfectly.

I wanted something made with completely smooth silicone that had no grooves or textures on the inside of the band (the side that goes against your wrist / skin).

This one is perfectly smooth just like the outside. If feels very velvety.

It's just perfect!

I also wanted something that would match the all matte black Skagen Falster 2, and boy... This band matches the look perfectly as well.

There's no gap between the lugs an the band. It's exactly 20mm wide.

My other watch in need of a decent band was my Fossil Sport (Gen 4) and the band that came with it is just 18mm wide and it narrows towards the tips. What's worse, and what made the watch look like a toy watch is that it had a dual color, gray on the inside and black on the outside.

The original Fossil Sport band was also unnecessarily thick (in height) which did not match it's own, thinner nylon lugs (the little two poles that you attach the bands to on either side of the watch case). The original band of the Fossil Sport is just terrible.

So...

How did I fit a 20mm wide band (this one) into my tiny 18mm wide lugs?

Well, the most important thing is the correct size / width of the spring bar (or quick release spring bar in this case).

What I did was, I swapped the original 18mm spring bar from my Fossil Sport band and placed it in this 20mm band and simply squeezed / finessed it into my watch.

It fits PERFECTLY and makes the watch look A LOT better than the original Fossil band ever did. There's just no comparison.

Another thing that I absolutely love in the design of this band it that there's no need of keepers and free loops. You can simply tuck in the loose end of the band underneath the opposite end of the band by the buckle. It's a night and day difference from traditional watch bands.

I REALLY LOVE THIS BAND!

And I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a very simplistic, easy to use, easy to clean and stylish band.

Buy it. Try it. I know you will keep it. :D

Thank me later!

* * * * * Well deserved 5 stars! * * * * *

TLDR: It's an incredible charger when it works for more than 2 months without quitting on you. I wasn't so lucky. Mine died in less than 2 months, right outside the return window.

This is a very compact (for what it is) and pretty dense / heavy charger considering its size. It's definitely got a lot of mass / heft. You can tell this is a legit GaN charger. I joke about it with my wife and say you could probably use this as personal defense tool. XD

Mine lasted just a little over a month. One day it simply stopped working, when I was on a trip without any other charger to keep my devices charged. I was very upset when it happened but that's just how it goes with electronics. Usually when something fails, it fails within the first 3 months of use, if that doesn't happen, then it should last you many years to come.

I've contacted Amazon regarding my dead charger and they said I could return my dud unit for refund. I didn't want that. You can't find another 100W for 20 bucks on Amazon.

So I contacted the seller. They asked me if I'd like to have it replaced. If so, to provide them my address and they would sent me a replacement. It's been almost a week and I haven't received anything. They have not replied my message after I gave them my address. It's not looking good so far.

I will cautiously recommend this charger to anyone who owns a multitude of devices that can / will take advantage of QC 3.0 and PD (cellphones, laptops, tablets, Nintendo Switch, Steam Deck, etc). I would especially recommend it, if you can purchase it at the 20 bucks price range. Which is I've paid for it mine, combining a clippable coupon, plus a coupon code. I'd say, for regular price, this should not cost more than 30 / 35 bucks - tops.

Cheers!

I was pretty disappointed when I opened up the package and saw how heavy and bulky this charger actually was.

For the last 3 months I've purchased and tested several 4 port (3 USB C, 1 USB A) 100W, 120W and 140W GaN chargers, and every single one of those have been significantly smaller and lighter than this one.

Other than the size and weight, this charger seems to function exactly as expected. It charges at the nominal rates using the PPD for all of my compatible devices.

I appreciate the fact that there's still a USB A port useful to charge devices that are not compatible with the newer charging standards, however, it only supplies up to 12W, which is pretty inadequate in my opinion. Most chargers I own, can crank up to 18W on the USB A port.

Price is also another big reason why was so harsh on this particular charger, as a lot of the chargers I own, rated 120W, cost less than half the current asking price of this charger. This is a highly competitive and saturated segment here at Amazon, and you've got to price it just right, carefully pricing your products taking into account its features, design and the competition.

Because of the reasons above, I would not recommend this 120W GaN charger. There are much better and cheaper alternatives out there.

To begin, this unit is extremely well built using quality materials. And the performance when properly installed is spectacular. I purchased this for my son-in-law and daughter who live in a rural setting and have poor cell service.  Installation is very straightforward, BUT it does require a lot of work.  This is NOT just your plug and play type of unit.  Yet, the work is worth your time in the end.  Before this unit, their signal was hit-or-miss. It was very frustrating because 4G would come, and then it would go without anything to indicate why it was good one minute, then lousy for the next couple hours. Their home is about 5 miles from the nearest tower and their property has steep hills on both sides, so I wasn't really sure even this booster would help.  I installed the outdoor antenna about 25 feet above ground on a mounting pole (NOT INCLUDED) and we attached it to the house's  fireplace chimney.  We pointed the antenna in the direction of the closet cell tower as instructed in the manual.  I strongly recommend that you take the time to get the outdoor antenna aimed properly, I really think this is one of the most important steps!!  So far we are very pleased with how this booster is working out for her and it has given her a much stronger signal!!  Highly recommended.

This charger is amazing. Usually with the car chargers your phone never charges very quickly when compared to a wall charger. I am very happy with this product and will use it in the car all the time, especially on long road trips, it'll make charging so easy!

I bought this phone used locally but wanted to give my feedback here to help potential buyers.
First, the build quality is excellent. All metal and gorilla glass, the back does get slightly warm under heavy use and you need to be careful with the half-glass back because it'll scratch on you. But if you take care of your things then scratching it up won't be an issue. I recommend a dBrand skin for at least the glass.
Battery life was meh, not as good as I expected but then again my phone was used (although in perfect condition). I get about 7 hours of light use on a full charge, maybe 4 if I'm gaming or editing video.
Speaking of video, this camera is amazing. I've never used better on a cell phone and I've owned many different models since 1991. If you unlock the developer mode you can change the color of the phone to sRGB and that reduces the saturation of colors and makes them more accurate, very helpful for on the go video or photo editing. Without sRGB enabled colors look rich and vibrant, the reds almost hurt your eyeballs. Colors pop and blacks are inky black.
Speed. This phone is BLAZING fast, especially if you adjust the animation time/length. Things happen immediately on this phone, no lag, no stutter. Try using something like an Aristo 2 or even a BLU phone for a few hours and then jump on the Pixel. It's like night and day. The 4GB of RAM is managed very efficiently by Android and this phone can run Android P Beta, which I'm using right now. You'll always get the latest updated first, while phones like the LG G6 will still have Oreo 8.0 well into 2019.
The 5" model may seem a bit small for someone coming from a phablet but you can fully customize the icon sizes, text size, display size, etc. My last phone was a 5.7" Nexus 6P and I miss the huge screen a bit but now I can operate my phone with one hand easily, which helps a great deal in certain situations.
You can find this phone for between $150-$200 used on Craigslist, OfferUp, and eBay, and it's worth every penny. The Pixel is the best deal in a used phone right now, slightly better than the LG G6 in my opinion. You don't get waterproofing, or wireless charging, but what you do get is an unlocked phone with a million LTE bands that can work anywhere you go basically. I'm using Project Fi and my monthly bill is never over $50. For a phone of this quality that has the ability to seamlessly switch between Sprint, T-Mobile, and US Cellular, it's a no-brainer...buy yourself a used Pixel and you'll be glad you did. If the 5" model is to small for you the XL model should suit you fine.

Okay firstly this is incredibly easy to setup and has a small footprint. I thought installation is going to be more complicated but it’s as easy as it can get. Pop the small antenna on the roof, keep it connected to the unit that goes inside the vehicle and power indoor unit up and that’s it. Just minor cable work. It DOES work with our T Mobile and the signal is noticeably and measurably better, we went from 1 to 3 bars. It performs better when vehicle is not moving though. We do get better reception even when driving but it is not as steady as when the vehicle is parked. We have a bad signal due to many hurricanes passing by and poor infrastructure in the area we like to go for fishing trips. This unit is quite handy and portable, very easy to move in and out of the vehicle if you want to switch or use in home. It works home too !

First things first I’m a sucker for all RAZER gaming products and I was excited to try out their cellphone case. When it comes about the visual appearance the case is not as fancy as their gaming gear because obviously there is no RGB lightning, however the green RAZER logo and the green rim around the camera opening really pops. Outside of the case is made out of something that feels like silicone but much more premium, it almost feels velvety. Very smooth and very pleasant to touch, definitely allows for a good grip. If you touch it with greasy hands it will show and you will have to put in some elbow grease to clean up. There ain’t no just wiping down. Inner edges are tough and inside the case is lined with thermally conductive lining that redirects heat towards back of the case through small hole opening. I am very happy with this design, somehow RAZER kept it clean but special. It must be the logo 😆 Installation is easy, just pop the phone in. It works with my no name wireless charger too. RAZER claims it to be drop proof up to 10ft but I haven’t tested that one so we’ll just have to take it at a face value. Well done but stay away from food.

The problem is cell signal because we must be able to work remotely but also I can’t imagine taking small kids to the jungle without cellphone signal. What if there’s an emergency?? My husband plans on connecting his laptop to the cellphone hotspot so he can stay engaged at work. I am bringing kids tablets but only in case it rains 🙈 in which case they will have internet connection that’s to the iPhone hotspot feature but actually thanks to this cell signal booster! I am sooo happy with this!
