The stars above the textbox to write a review asks about traveling... Let me start off by saying, this is not for travel. It's not compact or convenient to carry, it's bulky and not meant to be carried around with. That being said, it's great for charging multiple devices in the home. For example, I charge my husband's iPad, my iPad, and our daughter's tablet. It makes a great power station and serves as a reminder to charge the devices. Before we go to our bedroom for the night, it's a quick glance before running upstairs.

I knew when I bought this phone that I couldn't do my classic song and dance of trying to find a tempered glass screen protector. Let me make myself very clear when I say that there is no tempered glass screen protector for a curved glass phone that will be worth the money. A lot of the reviews on this product say that you lose your fingerprint reader, and they're not wrong, BUT you will in most situations anyways.

If you get a glass one, the only adhesive is on the edge of the phone, so it kinda "floats" over the middle and you don't get a good read anyways. If you get the spray on ones, you're covering it and it will either not read, or read poorly. If you get the built in ones, you'll keep your fingerprint reader, but they're much bulkier, you get one shot at applying it because it has to "stick" to the fingerprint reader, you can NEVER take it off and you have to get a brand new one if ANYTHING gets stuck under there, including lint, dust, sand, condensation ANYTHING.

I thought that this was a happy compromise. The reviews are right, you lose your fingerprint scanner, but it is so EASY to take on and off - not to say that it's convenient by any means when compared to just being able to use it, but still. Dust, lint, etc. does find it's way in your case because there are openings near the front facing camera and all around the rear facing cameras. It gets pretty gross (I have a toddler) and you definitely have to be careful at the because because if sand gets in between it'll scratch your screen and the back of the case that faces the screen. I'm a little of a cleaning nut, so I take it apart and wipe it all down probably once a week when I clean my WFH office, but I could definitely go longer than that.

For the people that said it falls apart, there's a "switch" at the bottom that locks everything in place and I've never had it slide unintentionally. The pen was also very easy for me to access, so I'm unsure about that comment as well. I love this case, I love how sleek the phone is, I love that the screen is CRYSTAL clear, I love that I can take it on and off, I love that it has magsafe compatibility (I've bought a wallet, a dock, a car dock, and a magsafe back up charger) and it's made life and day to day ops so much more convenient.

This new G8 is a must have for audiophiles. I've been an LG fan for quite a while. Going back to the Chocolate actually! So i purchased the V-30 on release day in October 2017. Great device. Fantastic quad dac/amp. I then purchased the G-7 upon launch in 2018. Great device also that added dts-x and a digital eq. Loved the taller thinner form factor. More recently i purchased the V-40 upon release in October 2018 and the audio is the same as the G-7. So i just purchased the G-8. This is by far the best device that LG has ever produced. The audio is actually better than on the G-7 and V-40. Richer. Incredible resolution. Fantastic. I use my Sennheiser 660s and my Sennheiser 700's. Both sound phenominal. And for those folks that insist on Bluetooth headphones know that this is among a very small group of smartphones that has the incredible aptX hd!! You've never experienced hd audio over Bluetooth until you pair the G8 with Beyerdynamic's
Amiron Wireless headphones. Why Samsung and Apple don't offer aptX hd on their flagships is beyond me! I bought this incredible device from T-Mobile in Crimson Red. Its beautiful. Saw that Amazon just posted the Alexa enabled G-8 in Aurora Black so i bought that as well. If you havent read Stereophile Magazines V-30 review on the ESS quad dac i strongly suggest you do so. The G-8 uses the same dac with some up to date tweaking. I cant recommend this device enough for audiophiles. Amazon just lowered this incredible device to $500! Happy listening. UPDATE October 2019: Although I stand by my initial review there is a major problem that requires this update. If you can get this wonderful device through your carrier I strongly recommend you do so. The problem has to do with Amazon and not this device. I purchased 1 G8 through tmobile and have had no problems. I purchased 2 from Amazon and here is why I strongly suggest you dont buy the Amazon version. . The issue is....my Tmobile g8 is operating on Sept. 2019 security update. Both Amazon G8's are operating on April 2019 security update and won't update further. A 6 month old security update is absurd. The reason is that LG has not done any updates for the Amazon G8. LG just advised me that as of October 21st, 2019, the April update is the Latest!! This phone was released in April 2019 and there has Never been a security update! So...apparently there will be no updates for the Amazon G8. In essence that makes Amazon's G8 a defective device that shouldn't be sold. Great device...just buy it elsewhere. Also 1 of the 2 G8's that I bought from Amazon had an invalid esn. I couldn't register the warranty with LG because they advised that there was no such device. I called Amazon. Zero help. I sent it back for a refund and purchased another one. The replacement is ok except for having an April 2019 Android security update. Absurd and unacceptable. My Tmobile G8 has been perfect and receiving regular updates. Update Dec 30th 2019: The Amazon G8 has been $399 since Thanksgiving which is a good deal. Update August 2020: Amazon is selling this 2 year old phone now for $749. Ridiculous. Just got the new LG V60 which destroys the old G8 in every category. Bigger. Newer processor. And best of all...the V60 has the brand new ESS 9219 Sabre dac! The G8 has the 9218. The 9219 is far superior. Best of all AT&T has the V60 for FREE if you add a line. Don't buy an antique.

I like this case because it's just sturdy enough, but doesn't add bulk or weight to your already heavy iPhone. Also, love the iridescent color.

Although this protector is designed to cover the whole screen, it's designed so that it's not to be used with a case. It took me 6 times of adjusting the protector back and forth before it will sit inside my Spigen case without the case lifting the protector completely from the screen. Because of the hairline thin alignment threshold, it's almost impossible to place on the screen without messing up and having to wash the protector back to get rid of any dust particles that may have been introduced. That being said, the case tends to apply enough pressure on the corners of the protector when the phone is in operation such that bubbles may be introduced occasionally even when the protector is fully seated within the case. Most bubbles can get be ridded with a simple push, although I am finding that over the time of 2 weeks the adhesive weakens and that any bubbles being introduced gets increasingly harder to push down. The protector does have a black border that covers over a few pixels on the viewable portions of the screen, and I am not sure why they didn't just make that piece clear instead (probably to hide the bubbles on the side better). Compared to having the stock PET film, fingerprint authentication seems much more sluggish possibly due to the slipperiness of the screen, even when the fingerprint is re-enrolled (although the protector seems to deal with fingerprints quite well). Lastly, the protector is already chipped from the keys in my pocket. For that reason, I am doubting that the protector is actually that tough. A film based protector may probably be the best way to go on this...

So this is a really cool idea for a small, travel charger. I liked the idea of it being compatible with all types of ports without having to carry around extra cords. You didn't need the mini cable it comes with to charge it, any standard USB cable would work.

However, this battery got very, very hot a few minutes into charging my phone. The part of the battery that got the hottest was obviously near the top by the charging cable itself. I thought maybe the provided usb c adapter was faulty. So I used one of my own. Still got extremely hot.

Then I tried using the lightning/micro USB cable with a different device. While it didn't happen as fast as it did with my phone, the battery eventually got very hot.

It was really warm while I was charging the battery itself. Not as bad as when trying to charge a device. I realize that the more compact something is the quicker it will overheat. Still, I didn't expect it to happen so fast or last as long. It took quite a bit of time to cool down after being unplugged.

I may have received a faulty product or it may be that this is a particular problem with the battery overall. It's also possible my phone was drawing power from the battery too fast. I'm not sure. Whatever the case, I'd just be careful using this. I suggest periodically feeling it to make sure it's not extremely hot. I've seen portable batteries melt from these kinds of problems. You can find photos and videos of them everywhere. So do your research and keep safe.

Okay so maybe my title is a bit cheeky but I can't deny that this is one of the best $4 I've spent in my life. I wasn't expecting much from this case to be honest. I've always wanted to try a glow-in-the-dark phone case but most reviews for them aren't great. However for the price (plus I could return it) I took the plunge.

I'm glad I did. I like that it is not too bulky. All the cutouts are where they need to be. The S-Pen is easy to get to. It is not going to provide a lot of protection. However, if you are like me and simply need a case to protect the back glass and give you some grip this does the job. I baby my phones and carry them in a neoprene pouch or a small portfolio so I typically don't need to encase my phone in a brick.

As to the GITD feature I've included a few pics. One is just of the case in regular light, full darkness, and very minimal light. It definitely looks cool. I'd be lying if I said I didn't "charge" the back just to see it glow from time to time. I also should mention it's helped me find my phone once or twice when I misplaced it and it was not very bright. I could see the glowing back from afar.

The picture isn't the most vibrant though I assume that has to do with the GITD technology. I don't think it looks bad but it's not going to "pop" in person as you might expect. I think it mostly looks like how it's advertised here. I'm not disappointed at all.

It feels good in the hand. Gives enough lip around the front and sides. The camera lip could be a little higher but it's enough to keep it off whatever surface you may put your phone down on. Has the little holes to attach a lanyard, wrist strap, or keychain. Overall I think it's a very nice case for the price. If I could, I would give it 4.5 stars but I can't so I went with 5 because for the price it delivers more than you'd expect.

It comes with three protectors. The first I messed up by not knowing the trick to avoid bubbles. But after installing my fiancé’s and then mine a second time I got it down. You’ll notice when you apply the screen protector after using the guide that some bubbles remain. Pressing down won’t get rid of the bubbles. However if you lift the side just up enough for the screen to separate to where the bubble is and then reapply using your finger to press firmly and smooth it out the bubbles will go away and they will not return. Don’t wait to do this. Do it as soon as you apply it. If you wait, it may not work as easily. Once this screen protector is installed it is virtually invisible. The edges are black (as shown in the photo) this helps avoid the reflecting the colors of the pixels at the end of the protector which you may have notice with clear protectors. Worth the money 100x over. If I crack this one I’ll be back for more!

The Samsung Galaxy S20+ is a good phone overall with decent battery life; but being a longtime Samsung Galaxy user, I find myself missing my Galaxy S9 which I found to have maybe a slightly better battery. It also have a way better fingerprint system and placement of the fingerprint scanner. This current generation has the fingerprint scanner on the screen, similarly to last generation. However, the idea may be nice but the execution, not so much. Setting up the fingerprint scanner was a pain. It kept telling me I have to press harder. I had to delete my prints several times over because it would never work. I have the phone for almost a week now and I finally think I figured out how to set it up "properly." I had to use a wet wipe during the whole setup process.

Since the first Galaxy I got, which was probably S3, I have been able to get screen protectors and eventually glass screen protectors for cheap on Amazon (or eBay back in the days) and they would work great but for the first time ever, I had to spend more than $40 on a ultrasonic glass screen protector because none of those cheap protectors would work on the fingerprint scanner.

Those are the only complaints I have. Otherwise, I'm a big fan of the Android phone system. Everything works seamlessly. I own everything Apple except for my phones.

First off, I regret that I cannot give this phone a higher rating. it says that it has the proper antenna bands to work correctly in the United States with GSM carriers but, this is absolutely not true! it is supposed to be unlocked for all GSM carriers and Amazon says that it will work for T-Mobile, AT&t and all their subcarriers. in fact, I checked the specifications of this phone and it said that it had magic band 12 would you absolutely have to have if you want to use it on T-Mobile and their subcarriers. It does not have band 12 no matter what they say. I could make phone calls at 2G but, every time somebody would call me it would kick right to voicemail. In fact, I gave the IMEI number of the device to my carrier and they did a fool signal check on it and determined that it did not have hardly any of the bands that was needed including band 12. Trying to use this on a GSM carrier in the United States is very undependable. The phone is gorgeous and has great specifications and the 720p looks like a 1080p screen. The software has great optimization but, that is irrelevant if you cannot receive a phone call.

I have actually purchased several cases for my OnePlus 8T and sent them all back for one reason or another. This is a very difficult phone to get a decent case that offers a lot of protection. The 5G antenna bands for this phone are right at the top and a lot of the case manufacturers that I would normally go to do not manufacture a decent case for this phone because, their designs would cover up the 5G antenna array. Poetic does in fact design several cases for this phone that do offer a lot of protection without covering the 5G antennas. I did in fact purchase this case the poetic revolution and it does offer a lot of durability for the money. This is what I call a sandwich case because it features a top portion and a bottom portion that you have to place the phone in the middle of and squeeze together. Moreover, poetic offers two different top pieces that feature one of them that has a built-in plastic film screen protector and the other one does not for those of us that prefer to use tempered glass directly applied to the screen. This is a wonderful option to have but, it does cause a little bit of a flaw in its design.
The flaw that I have found is that the top portion with the built-in screen protector is much stronger and far more solid than the top piece that doesn't have that. In fact, the top piece that doesn't have the screen protector is the one that I use and I have noticed that the bottom portion of my phone while it's in the case has a tendency to rock around a little bit and does not fit quite properly. This is actually a little puzzling because I have the same series revolution case on my Motorola Moto g power and use the open top piece with that case and my Motorola fit snug and does not move at all. This tells me that the poetic designers did not do a thorough job in the testing phase before they released this case. I have found a way to modify the case to cut down some of the movement at the bottom of the phone and it is pretty simple in its application. I cut quarter inch rectangular shaped pieces of plumbers tape and place them in the bottom corners inside the case. This functions as kind of a spacer or gasket which does cut down on a lot of the movement but, you do not want to make it too thick otherwise it pushes the top portion of the phone out further at the bottom corners. Thus, the bottom outer corners of the case are not level with the top outer corners of the case. It makes it look kind of imbalanced so it is better to use as little tape on the inside of the case as possible. There really isn't any way to stop all of the movement without causing the consequence that I mentioned. Still, your phone will be very safe in this case either way. However, I really feel poetic could have done a better job and this is something that should have really been addressed before they released it. In my opinion, cases should just fit without having to make modifications or alterations. If you are fine with making a little bit of a modification or if you plan on using the top piece that has the built-in screen protector then I would absolutely recommend this case. In all reality, I was torn between this and the guardian series case and I have been wondering if I made the right decision. The guardian series case does not have the kickstand which I believe to be responsible for the movement of the phone while inside the case. I have really just never cared for clear back phone cases so I skipped the guardian and went to this.

I'm quite happy with this bumper, but before purchasing, make sure you know what you're getting. The product is called "Rugged Armor," but that's a pretty generous description. This bumper is quite thin, and the TPU material is pretty soft. I have very little faith that this bumper will offer meaningful protection if subjected to legitimate abuse. Light falls will probably be fine, but falls from a significant height, stepping on the phone, dropping something heavy on it, etc. are all likely to be bad news. This bumper offers very little screen protection: there are small lips on the top and bottom of the bumper that stick out above the screen - even with a screen protector on - but that's it. Because of the size of the Pixel 6 Pro, unless your phone falls perfectly level on a perfectly level surface, you're not going to get any meaningful screen protection. GET A SCREEN PROTECTOR! Fortunately, the bumper fits perfectly fine on my device even after installing a Whitestone glass screen protector.

All those shortcomings aside, I don't need an Otterbox-like case that offers truly rugged protection as I take good care of my electronics, don't drop them, etc. All I wanted was a bumper to protect against incidental bumps against walls, add some protection on the back of the phone so that it doesn't sit directly on the camera module glass when placed on a surface, and add a bit more grip to the very slippery Pixel 6 Pro. For those purposes, this bumper has been perfect. As mentioned above, it's quite thin and light, so the case doesn't add much bulk. The design is minimalistic and looks great on my black device - the carbon fiber accent on the back is a nice flourish that doesn't look over-the-top. The covers for the side buttons are quite nice: the Pixel 6 Pro provides very little tactile feedback when pressing one of the side buttons, but the bumper makes finding the buttons in the dark or by feel a lot easier. Other reviewers have described rapid discoloration of the case; I went with the black version and have only had it for a week at this point, but so far this hasn't been an issue, and the case still looks great. I'll update this review if I end up having trouble with it.

For $15, I'm completely happy with this purchase. If, like me, you just need some lightweight protection that adds minimal bulk to your device, I think you'll be happy with this bumper. If you're looking for something that offers heavy-duty protection for your device that will stand up to any abuse you throw at it, DON'T get this bumper - you'll be disappointed.

I wear my Apple Watch every day, and, after a few years, the included Apple band was starting to wear. There are a ton of third-party bands available on Amazon, and I went with these mostly because they were available on Vine. I've been using these bands for a few months at this point, and they're just as great as the original Apple band.

Compared to the Apple band, these are slightly smaller in width and a bit thinner. I've got small wrists, so these are actually a plus: the smaller band is flatter on my wrist compared to the Apple band. The spacing of the adjustment holes allows for a more comfortable fit than the Apple band, interestingly enough. The clasp functions well, and you're able to tuck excess band under the rest of the band, just like the original.

For $15 - about $5/band - this is a great value: the bands are made of quality materials, they're comfortable, and minimally different than the original Apple bands. Recommended!

The Zorida cell phone booster claims to boost the signal to 65dB, expanding your coverage to 5500 sq. ft. Within minutes of turning on the device, my network bars went to full, lingering around 1-2. The internet speed has boosted up five times within minimum call drops. The voice quality has gone up significantly.
I would 100% recommend this product to anyone who wants to boost their 4G and 5G signal, especially for people living in rural or remote locations, basements, or living in a building surrounded by high rises. From what I have experienced, a low network not only hampers signal quality but also drains your battery faster. This is because, with low networks, your phone constantly searches for better signals. As a result, it puts a lot of pressure on your hardware, resulting in faster battery drainage. After using the Zorida cell phone booster, my Iphone does not feel hot to touch all the time and the battery life has also never been better.
The device is fully compatible with all U.S carriers, including Verizon, Sprint, AT&T, T-Mobile and more. It is working perfectly on Verizon and Sprint. It supports multiple bands ranging from 700-750MHz, 80-850MHz, 1900MHz, and 1700-2100MHz, complying with all types of mobile devices, old or new.
The package comes with an indoor antenna, outdoor antenna, RF feeder box, power supply cables, indoor cables, outdoor cables, 3M waterproof takes, and accessories. Install the outdoor directional antenna pointing it toward the nearest signal tower. You can check the signal strength through the LCD display. Once your outdoor signal is improved, you can install an indoor antenna to broadcast these signals throughout your room.
It comes with a user manual with a clear step-by-step process. I had a little setback in mounting the external antenna as I couldn't find the direction of the nearest cell tower. So, I used a cell tower locator app. You have to hook up the power cable to the RF feeder box at one end and use the external cable to connect the external antenna on the other end. Then open up the Zorida application and follow up on the setup process. The app automatically detects the main box, and once it did, you can monitor the signal strength through the LCD display box or your app.

I always go for big and rugged cases and Poetic never disappoints. I've dropped my phone a few times already and hasn't had any damage at all. My favorite thing about this case is the spare piece without the plastic screen protector; now I don't have to remove that myself and can use that extra piece as a spare should I break my screen protector.

(6.7 inches just for the screen alone) Be ready to wield a mighty large device if you buy this case. One additional thing is that button on the left side, that's for the international model of this phone which has a programmable button. That's more Motorola's fault than anything.

I'm not too ruff and tumble with my phones, but this case makes me feel safe when I'm out and about. I'm glad Poetic made this case as there's not a large selection of cases with this phone. Even to where if Poetic or Supcase don't make a case available for a certain phone, it's a pass.

I've had many of their cases over the years and while Unicorn Beetle hasn't made one yet for the 5a, this is still an excellent buy. Feels quite sturdy and the front frame is just about the same size of screen protectors I bought, so it can act as an adjuster when applying one. (Which they also include one with a built in screen protector should one break)

The material is just as durable as always. The rubber on the back isn't slippery and the case itself is actually easier to apply this time. If I had one complaint, I'd love it if they made a belt clip for his) this case. Been kinda spoiled by Unicorn Beetle in that regard.

Poetic continues to be one of my favorite case manufacturers. Rugged and durable with an extra front frame without the built-in screen protector. (Which were always so hard to remove) And also rather cheap, especially compared to Otterbox.

While I am quite pleased with the quality of how this case is built, it sadly will barely fit my already large phone (Moto One 5G Ace) with a rugged case. It's certainly tall enough but it's width wasn't an easy fit for my phone, as I kinda had to wedge it inside like a stubborn pair of pants.

I do plan on keeping this as I plan on getting a different phone soon, so hopefully that will be be a better fit. Look at the dimensions of this case and do the same with your phone. Reviews say it's large but its width may not be enough. A phone the size of a Galaxy Note or S22 Note with a rugged case likely won't fit.

I do like that it doesn't have its own watch bands like some rugged smartwatch cases have, however it does feel rather limited when compared to Spigen's other rugged cases. (Or Unicorn Beetle as well)

In short I do like this as it's probably the best out there for a watch case you can use existing watch bands for, but I wouldn't trust it if you have an active lifestyle like bike or motorcycle riding. (Do get a screen protector for this watch in addition to this case)

I own another flat larger size car mount phone bracket, which is also MagSafe so my review will mainly focus on the difference, as that one is also one of my favorite.

The locking mechanism is very commonly seen at this price range, it’s secure, easy to install, but the downside is that if the design does not remedy the natural flaw, it will vibrate, just like this one. It’s not that bad, probably even not noticeable by most people, but I am sensitive to sound and vibration, so this is a huge thing to me. The main reason that caused the vibration is because it’s only the hook to drag on the plastic inside the vent, and the contacting point is very limited. My current one has another small bracket that will support the whole mount, which reduced the vibration hugely.

Good news for some people. They do have an excellent customer service team. They contacted me asking if I need any help so I brought this up. They sent me another mount, problem solved.

Other than this, I really like the strong magnet that holds my iPhone 12 Pro Max very tight, even on bumpy roads. It mentioned something like a fan to cool it when charging, don’t reply on that. Any car mount charger won’t be able to cool your phone when charging, especially when this is a true MagSafe 15w charger. The phone heats up quickly and screen dims immediately after overheating.

The ONLY solution is to use your AC air from the vent to cool your phone.

I sometimes listen to YouTube during the high way driving, might take a peek from time to time. (I know this is unsafe so I do that very very occasionally) The round base makes the rotation extremely easy than when I was using that larger plate charger. So this is a huge plus for me.

Overall this is a very decent charger with tidy packaging. I will buy again if I need another.

Hope my 2 cents help.
